Title: Add home button when away from home view
Body:
<!--
Ensure your title is short, descriptive, and in the imperative mood (Fix X, Change Y, instead of Fixed X, Changed Y).
For a good inspiration of what to write in commit messages and PRs please review https://chris.beams.io/posts/git-commit/ and our https://docs.jellyfin.org/general/contributing/issues.html page.
-->

**Changes**
Added a home button to the top toolbar when away from the home view, allowing for a quick "back to home" feature. The Jellyfin web UI and android mobile app has this. Having this button provides a quick way to leave a long navigation chain without having to press the back button multiple times.

The home button has been added to the `ClockUserView` as this appears to already be shown on all views although it does make the name of the view a little misleading.

**Issues**
Closes #2053 and #2717

#2053 does mention that new navigation features may provide a resolution for this in future. However, given the nice quality of life this provides I decided to tackle it as my first PR, as I also have frustrations with this.

Title: Null pointer exception during playback
Body:
### Describe the bug

After a few minutes of direct playback the player begins crashing until it gives up. Happened with 2 separate movies so far. Closing the app and returning to the same timestamp works without issues.

I wasn’t able to find any consistent way of triggering the crash. Will update the ticket if I do.

Title: Shuffle All only shuffles first season
Body:
### Describe the bug

I sleep to the big bang theory all seasons shuffled and just switched to jellyfin, the show and episodes are all named properly and in the library and sorted by season automatically but every time I hit shuffle all it will ONLY shuffle episodes from a single season and then completely stop playing anything. Is there a way to shuffle all episodes from the entire show and repeat them?
this behavior happens on the web browser jellyfin client on pc and on jellyfin for android tv

Title: RTL Subtitles (Puncitions)
Body:
### Describe the bug

1. Download subtitles that it's language is RTL such as Hebrew, here's an example of `.srt` subtitle:
```
1

.את חייבת לספר לי

```
2. Play the video file with the RTL subtitles on the Google Chromecast device
3. Puncitions are shown LTR instead of RTL:
```
1

את חייבת לספר לי.

```

Note: Plex has the same issue.

Title: Simpler dark screensaver
Body:
### Describe the feature you'd like

I would like to request a simple dark screensaver, not that screen savers are an option. The issue is that I tend to fall asleep in front of the tv and the changing pictures are quite bright with the movie poster. My proposal is something like a floating around jellyfin logo similar to how plex used to (still does?) do it. Okay, thanks for reading.

Title: Display date next to time
Body:
### Describe the feature you'd like

I think it would be helpful to see the date next to the time in the upper right hand corner of the screen. Maybe an option to toggle it on/off and date options? Not sure if anyone would be opposed to this, and it seems like it should be simple enough to implement. Although, I suppose it would complicate things for different countries with different date formats. Just a thought. Okay, thanks.

Title: Crash when seasons marked as played
Body:
### Describe the bug

1. Go to a show
2. Go to "Seasons"
3. Mark a season as played
4. ASAP; mark another season as played

**Expected:**
No crash and both seasons are marked as played in the UI

**Actual:**
At step 3 the season is not marked as played in the UI
At step 4 it crashed almost everytime (at least I'm moved to the Android TV launcher app) - guess it depends on how fast you can perform step 3 and 4 and maybe how fast hardware you got. After going back into Jellyfin both seasons are correctly marked as played.


### Logs

_No response_

### Application version

0.16.2

Google Play

BenQ QS02

11

10.8.13

Title: Tries to load subtitles when It shouldn't. Errors out, then lets me select the subtitle track
Body:
### Describe the bug

1. Add a tv show episode that has at least one subtitle track (don't know if it happens with movies, haven't tried)
2. Play it.
3. Try to check the subtitle track as soon as the show starts. Can you?

In my case it starts by saying "loading subtitles" although I can see that no subtitle track is selected (the track of "no subtitle" is checked). I try to select the subtitle track that I want, but it doesn't let me do that. I have to wait until it gives out an error basically saying it couldn't load the subtitles (can't remember the exact words) and then It does let me select the subtitle track, and displays it correctly.

10.8.13

Title: [Google Chromecast Google TV 4K] h265/HEVC framerate 23.976216 issues
Body:
### Describe the bug

After a lot of testing I've found out that the HEVC support is very flaky on the 4K google tv. 
For all my test files 23.976216 fps is killing the playback. Transcoding fixes the issue instantly. High/low bitrates don't matter, the playback is simply juddery. With any other HEVC file it works perfectly fine. 
This issue is only present on Exoplayer. VLC works fine but will have desynced audio after a while (10mins). 

Would it be possible to detect the google tv and simply transcode to H264 for those files? Or simply add an option on the client to always transcode h265/HEVC to h264 always.

Title: Pause the video when losing network connection

### Describe the feature you'd like

Pause the video when losing network connection (and give a simple "lost connection to server) and then resume when network connection is back,

Title: Downmix to Stereo Option is Ignored in Exoplayer for AAC Audio
Body:
### Describe the bug

**Steps to Reproduce:**

1. Enable the following options:

    Preferred Media Player: Exoplayer
    Max Streaming Bitrate: 120 Mbit/s
    Audio Output: Downmix to Stereo

2. Attempt to play a video track with an AAC audio track.
3. Note that transcoding doesn't take place - Jellyfin ignores the "Downmix to Stereo" option and attempts to direct play the file.

**Comments:**

This issue affects all tested video files with AAC audio tracks, regardless of container / video codec. I will attach an example file to this issue.

LibVLC respects the "downmix to stereo" option when playing AAC files. Exoplayer is the only one affected.

Why is this a big issue? Two reasons:

1. AAC is an extremely common audio codec, and there are at least two very common scenarios where Exoplayer fails to play AAC tracks without downmixing / transcoding:
    - When the channel layout isn't "C L R Ls Rs LFE". (FFMPEG / Tdarr can cause this: https://github.com/HaveAGitGat/Tdarr_Plugins/issues/571)
    - When the channel count exceeds 6 (5.1).

    See: https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin-androidtv/issues/1753#issuecomment-1853148550 for more info.

2. LibVLC works as a fallback but has limited [and soon to be discontinued](https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin-androidtv/pull/3039) support, and fails to play other audio codecs effectively, meaning that users will have to switch back and forth to get the best experience possible. If LibVLC is completely removed, users will be unable to play AAC at all assuming that downmixing is necessary (see scenarios above).


Download test file here: https://www.mediafire.com/file/xb5wqphw353x53s/AAC_5.1.mp4/file

Title: Playback stops and player closes after resuming
Body:
### Describe the bug

Case A:
1. Choose a movie or episode that has a resume option available.
2. Click on resume.
3. Playback starts normally.
4. After about one minute the playback stops and the player closes. 
5. The view before resuming is shown.

Case B:
1. Play a movie or episode.
2. Pause the playback for some minutes.
3. Press play.
4. Playback continues normally.
6. After about one minute the playback stops and the player closes. 
7. The view before playback is shown.

I experienced this bug with several different movies and episodes.

### Logs

No crash occurs.

Title: [Bug] <Musicify not responding to touch commands>
Body:
### Acknowledgements

- [X] I have searched the existing issues and confirmed that this is a new issue, not a duplicate or related to another open issue.
- [X] I have written a short but informative title.
- [X] I have updated the app to the latest version. You can find the latest version [here](https://github.com/gokadzev/Musify/releases).
- [X] I will fill out all the sections in this issue report.

### Current Behavior

When I launch a fresh instance of the app, it works okay. If I go to the about page in the settings tab, and then go back, the app won't scroll up or down. This happens if I search for a song, play it, go in player view (shows album art, download, etc.), and then go back, the app won't respond to anything I do, although I can play-pause from the player popup within android.

### Expected Behavior

If I search for a song, play it, go in player view (with the big album art, download button, etc.), and exit that view, I should still be able to navigate the app.

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Launch new instance of the app.

2.1 Go to the about page in the settings tab, and then go back

3.1 The app won't scroll up or down.

1. Launch new instance of the app.

2.2 Search for a song, play it, go in player view (shows album art, download, etc.), and then go back.

3.2 The app won't respond to anything besides interaction from the player popup within android.

### Musify Version

6.5.2

### Operating System Version

OneUI 5.1, Android 13

### Additional Information

Installed from Droid-ify
Device: S22 Ultra

Title: Private selfhosted instances
Body:
From matrix chat:

Hey! I was looking at the source and I see you already support sending the SID cookie header for the existing authed invidious API endpoints. This is pretty cool. :)  Invidious are adding support for private selfhosted instances so they can accept the SID cookie on all the endpoints such as /api/v1/search  and /api/v1/videos/videoid.
yattee have added support for sending the SID cookie on all invidious requests in the testflight build (not in the appstore yet) (this is the GH issue and the pull is referenced https://github.com/yattee/yattee/issues/552

I was playing with it last night and was seriously excited to see the network traffic showing the SID in video stream requests in yattee. Do you think maybe clipious could also start sending the SID cookie to all the /api/v1/ requests when logged in so I could use it for private instances too?

Title: Expand Pull Up Area of Playing-Now Bar
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When you want to play certain content, you press on it, it loads, you press play and said content plays. Sometimes you want to perform different actions while said content keeps playing. You do this by pressing and holding on it's "GIANT" thumbnail (which streches the whole width of the display) and pulling down and it pulls down to a playing-now bar. If or when you want to pull it back up you can't press and hold and pull up on the whole width of the bar, you have to press and hold and pull up only on it's "MINI" thumbnail (which is in the lower left corner). It's frustrating and it doesn't make sense.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I would like to be able to press and hold and pull up the playing-now bar by pressing and holding and pulling up from anywhere on the bar. I think it can be done, I've seen it done in Phonograph Plus.

Describe alternatives you've considered
I have not considered other solutions, I don't see any.

Additional context
None.

Title: Download entire playlist
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I have a playlist for videos I want to watch. In the YouTube app, I set this playlist to have its videos downloaded automatically, so that I could watch any of them when out and about. When I add a new video it is downloaded after some time, and when I remove a video from the playlist the download is eventually deleted.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Ideally, I would be able to toggle a playlist to be downloaded or not, and it would do what I mentioned above.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I think the only alternative at present is manually downloading each video when adding it to the playlist, and then manually removing the download when removing the video from the playlist.

**Additional context**
It would also be helpful to have a single button to download an entire playlist at once (in a queue, of course), even if downloads weren't updated automatically going forward. Otherwise I think I have to manually download every single video in my playlist individually to get set up.

Title: Quick Account Switcher
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Using AndroidTV, it's entirely possible that multiple people would have different profiles used to watch different channels on YouTube.  SmartTube has the ability to quickly switch between authenticated accounts so that each of those users are able to easily access their own account.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
It would be great if within the UI there was the ability to either:
- Add multiple accounts to the same server, or;
- Add copies of the same server with different names and authentication

With either of these solutions, it would be great to have a method within the UI to switch accounts with a click or two.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
- Technically, I could spin up multiple private Invidious instances with a single account per instance, but this feels pretty wasteful.

Title: Respect animation settings in Android system settings
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When you switch tabs or open some interfaces in clipious, there is a transition animation, and I do not want that.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
In Android system settings, there is a "Remove animations" setting in the accessibility settings (may not be available to every Android system); and in the developer options, there are three settings called "Window animation scale", "Transition animation scale", and "Animator duration scale" which can be set to "animation off" to disable animations (available to every Android system). Clipious should respect those settings.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Add an option in the in-app appearance settings to disable animations.

**Additional context**
![Screenshot_20231014-112741_Settings](https://github.com/lamarios/clipious/assets/26196187/6f3ecc10-cc0d-46da-9a20-db18fc3ac771)

I found that this issue is actually a dup of #222, but I am not closing this issue because the author of that issue has become a ghost.

I have set it as Assist app. I have even tried it after a reboot, force-stop etc. but no luck so far.
My device is a Samsung Galaxy running on Android 11.

Edit: I forgot to add, after changing to "Screenshot_%random%" if you revert to the default "Screenshot_%timestamp%", it still does not work.

Title: Hide ScreenshotTile editing tools from Edit With menu in other apps
Body:
When i'm using other apps that allow editing pictures through another app, the ScreenshotTiles tools are always listed as available…
But they are the same exact tools as several other editing apps which they share their library with, such as PhotoEditor (https://github.com/burhanrashid52/PhotoEditor). So having the ScreenshotTiles tools show up along them is a bit redundant…

I'd like that the in-app tools ScreenshotTile uses stay in-app and don't appear when searching for editing tools.

Title: Floating button moves unpredictably
Body: I really enjoy the app but since upgrading to Android 14 on pixel 7 pro, the app has been very buggy, including placement of the floating button, which won't stay where it's put, bouncing to the top of left side of the screen. Please fix.

Title: Camera hole/notch/cutout screenshot
Body:
On many devices the camera hole/notch/cutout in the screen has touch capabilities.
It would be convenient to tap on the camera to start a screenshot. 
Currently the floating button cannot be moved over the notch.

- As a first step the floating button should be allowed to be placed over the notch.
- As an improvement there could be a separate option to enable the camera hole. This would make it clear to the user that this feature exists. Dimension and position of the hole/notch can be retrieved programmatically. Possibly a slightly larger touch area can be used, so that even devices that lack touch capabilities on the notch, can use the feature.

Title: [feature request] Reproducible F-Droid app
Body:
@cvzi If possible can you please make reproducible F-Droid app. For more information see [Towards a reproducible F-Droid.](https://f-droid.org/en/2023/01/15/towards-a-reproducible-fdroid.html)
* see also: 
** [Total number of apps using RB: 6 + 75 = 81. Last updated: 2023-03-04](https://gitlab.com/obfusk/fdroid-misc-scripts/-/blob/master/reproducible/overview.md)
** [Encourage new apps enable reproducible build](https://gitlab.com/fdroid/fdroiddata/-/issues/2816)

Title: Option to select specific app to open image
Body: Offer a list of apps (activities) that can open an image with Send/view/edit intent and automatically open the image after the screenshot

Title: floating window feature
Body:
Thanks for the app, it's great.
After taking a shot, if a floating window appears with various options, it would be easier requiring less taps and finger movement compared to going to the notification area.
Here's the floating window of https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=pl.waskysoft.screenshotassistant providing various options at bottom [first option is for scribbling, second for cropping or selecting area post taking fullscreen screenshot, third and fourth for saving and sharing]

Title: Remove duplicates with shared albums
Body:
**Describe the feature**

As of now, the `Remove duplicates` feature does not take into account items shared with us but only items owned by ourselves.

I would like to be able to automatically detect duplicates including from albums shared with me. This would be an awesome feature to consolidate albums with others.

As a concrete use case, friends and myself are uploading common memories to shared albums on Ente. We often encounter duplicates due to pictures having been shared by other canals (e.g. Signal) before starting to use Ente.

Title: Move to album even when not owned by ourselves
Body:
**Describe the feature**

As of now, it is not possible to use the `Move to album` action with a shared album not owned by ourselves.

I would like to be able to move items to a shared album that I do not own. Once moved to the shared album, my items should be automatically marked as uncategorised on my side.

Title: Remove from album hangs forever
Body:
**Describe the bug**

In some situations, the `Remove from album` action hangs forever.

**To Reproduce**

My personal workflow regarding sorting my photos is as follows:

1. When I take a picture with my phone, it is uploaded and placed into my `Camera` album on Ente.
2. In order to sort my photos, I open the `Camera` album on Ente, select a bunch of items and click on `Add to album` (as of now, it is not possible to use the `Move to album` action with a shared album not owned by ourselves).
3. When asked, I select the album where I want the items to be placed.
4. Once the album selected, it is automatically opened as the current view in the app.
5. I hit the Android return button to go back to the `Camera` album.
6. I select the same items once more.
7. I click on `Remove from album`.
8. The loading animation hangs forever.

As a workaround, I can hit the Android return button once more after step 5 to leave the `Camera` album, renter it, reselect the items and click on `Remove from album` which works this time.

**Expected behavior**

I expect the items to be removed from the `Camera` album from the very first time (step 7).

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**

 - **Device:** Pixel 8
 - **OS:** GrapheneOS (Android 14)
 - **App Version:** 0.8.21 (F-Droid)

Title: Allow adding to Ente through iOS share sheet
Body: Hi! I'd really like to be able to upload photos and videos by sharing them to ente through the system share sheet and not having to go through Apple Photos at all.

Title: Shared Album - Hide photos already in album, from recipient.
Body:
I think it would be nice if when you shared an album, one of the toggles was to hide the pictures already inside the album. For example, if I want to collect photos from multiple people into an album which already has loads of photos I don't necessarily want them to see them. Basically, they could upload their own pictures but not see what's in the album. So when they clicked on the link it'd say, for example, "Content is hidden" with an upload button.

I say this because I have an album for a social game I play called VR Chat for all the photos taken in game by myself and friends, and I'd like to be able to let them directly add photos without seeing all the pictures already in there.

Title: Shared photos cannot be organized own albums
Body:
I cannot assign an individual album to a photo shared with me. In fact, no organisational feature seems to work. This is not only confusing, but IMHO a sever limitation in the user experience. This makes family sharing quite hairy.

I'd like to suggest that photos shared with me should behave just like my own photos.

Perhaps a duplicate of #1401?

Title: Hidden album contents still shown within the "Uncategorized" section
Body:
**Describe the bug**
I've recently moved a lot of my media over to ente and it's been pretty painless so far, although one strange thing is happening and I'm unsure if it's me or not!

I've sorted contents into multiple albums and then I've set a few to "Hidden" so they don't show up on the main screen. In the app's main screen this seems to work OK, although if I go to Albums I have two entries for "Uncategorized" (see screenshot), one has all of my images (including those within Hidden albums that I can just view) and one has (the correct) images that are currently unsorted.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Import content
2. Add albums and move content around
3. Set some albums to Hidden
4. Check the two Uncategorized sections

**Expected behavior**
There is no way to access the Hidden albums unless you authenticate into the Hidden section and the Uncategorized sections should match with the amount of images

**Screenshots**
![Screenshot_20240112_093957_ente Photos](https://github.com/ente-io/photos-app/assets/7994724/a0c348ff-d802-423d-ae24-9d4ebee1493e)


**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
Samsung S23 Ultra, latest Android UI 6

**Additional context**
This may be related, but on ente web the main image feed shows ALL of my images also (even from the Hidden folders, so I presume this is loading from the full "Uncategorized" section, while ente app is showing the correct main feed without the Hidden items. I've tried the clean option, but it is still the same.

Title: Integerate FFMpeg Probe for parsing video metadata
Body:
Currently, the app doesn't parse any metadata related to video, apart from whatever is already provided by the file system.

Using `FFMpeg Probe`, we can parse the video metadata and display relevant information.

Package to explore:  [ffmpeg_kit_flutter](https://pub.dev/packages/ffmpeg_kit_flutter)

Note:
- Adding the parsing during upload can result in slight performance degradation.
  - Test & evaluate forked version if plugging it during upload https://github.com/arthenica/ffmpeg-kit/pull/792
- Back Fill: When a video is loaded by the media_player, the client can check if the `location` or `editedTime` in the `publicMagicMetadata` is missing. If it's missing, we can run the probe command and try to back-fill the information silently.

Title: Live Activities and Dynamic Island support for uploads
Body:
With the release of iOS 16, Apple introduced two powerful new UI features - Live Activities and the Dynamic Island. I think these would be incredibly useful to integrate with the media upload experience in Ente Photos.

When users are uploading photos and videos to the cloud, it would be great to display a Live Activity showing upload progress. This would persist on the lock screen allowing users to monitor status without needing to open the app ([background upload](https://github.com/ente-io/photos-app/issues/1532)).

Additionally, upload status and controls could be surfaced through the Dynamic Island for devices like iPhone 14 Pro. This space is perfect for showing realtime progress, pausing/resuming, etc.

Integrating these iOS 16 features would make the upload process and visibility into status much more seamless. Users would feel more confident their media is backing up properly without needing to actively monitor the app.

I suggest considering to add Live Activities and Dynamic Island support for uploads in a future app update. Please let me know if any early design/testing with these features would be helpful!

Thanks for your consideration!

Title: Feature request: adjust multiple parameters with one Midi command
Body:
Dear Tuntorius, currently one MIDI command (pedal controller or button) can only be assigned to one parameter. 

Controlling several effects toggles with one button would  enable switching on multiple effects (drive, modulation, delay) with one button.

There are several uses I can think of for the ability to assign the sweep of the pedal to several parameters at once:
- increase delay level and feedback to  freeze a sound with toe down and unfreeze with heel down, create wacky delay effects
- control pedal gain, amp gain  master level (and perhaps tone) with a pedal in real time to change the distortion amount without changing the volume.
- increase gain, modulation and delay for a solo.

Proper implementation of multiple continuous parameter control would require setting the parameter change ranges. E.g. controller Pedal sweep from 0 to 100 corresponds to changing amp drive from 10% to 80% while simultaneously reducing amp master from 80 to 50% (to increase the drive without increasing the volume).

Title: Application does not support private X.509 PKI
Body:
**Describe the bug**
EasySync does not appear to read user-defined certificate authorities from the Android system. Trying to connect to a server that uses a private CA results in:

```
Error: java.security.cert.CertPathValidatorException: Trust anchor for certification path not found.
```

This certificate works in Chrome and other applications that read the CA, so appears to be limited to EasySync. EasySync should preferably read from the user's trusted CAs, but could also allow for an "insecure" option in which the user disables TLS validation (e.g. for self-signed certificates.) 

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
 - Device brand/model: Pixel 6a
 - Android version: 13

Title: Use WebDAV library
Body:
There are some existing libraries for Android with WebDAv support:
https://github.com/WebDAVDevs/awesome-webdav/tree/main?tab=readme-ov-file#java

It would be really great if you can reuse or contribute to this libraries. This will make it easier to support many servers with their quirks and also avoid known bugs. But what is more important is that it will make other app creators easier to add the WebDAV support.

Some libraies:
* https://github.com/thegrizzlylabs/sardine-android based on OkHttp. I'm not sure who using it and also it has a lot of open PRs without traction.
* https://github.com/bitfireAT/dav4jvm from DavX5. This is a very popular program for Calendar syncing but not sure if it works with files. 
* https://github.com/McDjuady/dav4jvm fork of the dav4jvm rewritten in Kotlin

I don't know what the NextCloud app uses itself but the owbCloud has a separate lib https://github.com/owncloud/android-library

Body: It looks like EasySync is currently syncing (or trying to sync) the entire "Internal shared storage" on the phone to the DAV folder you specify. Some might prefer to have more control over which folders are synced.

Title: Wi-Fi-only episode downloads missed if podcast refreshed over mobile data
Body:

- [X] I have used the search function for [**OPEN**](https://github.com/AntennaPod/AntennaPod/issues) issues to see if someone else has already submitted the same bug report.
- [X] I have **also** used the search function for [**CLOSED**](https://github.com/AntennaPod/AntennaPod/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed) issues to see if the problem is already solved and just waiting to be released.
- [X] I will describe the problem with as much detail as possible.
- [X] If the bug only to occurs with a certain podcast, I will include the URL of that podcast.

### App version

3.2.0

### Where did you get the app from

F-Droid

### Android version

Android 13

### Device model

OnePlus Nord CE

### First occurred

_No response_

### Steps to reproduce

1. Set _Mobile updates_ to permit _Podcast refresh_ but not _Episode download_.
2. Receive a new episode whilst on mobile data.
3. Reconnect to Wi-Fi later.

### Expected behaviour

Ideally, episodes discovered whilst connected to a metered network would be processed for downloading on the next sync when an unmetered connection is available.

### Current behaviour

The downloads of these episodes are missed, and they remain in the Inbox not downloaded.

For context, I'm generally hands-off with the app when I'm not listening, and mostly listen on the move or otherwise when Wi-Fi isn't readily available, so it's not until I go to listen to a regular podcast that I find it hasn't been downloaded.

I could limit podcast refreshes on mobile data as well, but it's nice to get notifications for new episodes of less-regular podcasts as they come in.

### Logs

_No response_

# Info

## App version

3.2.0

## Where did you get the app from

Google Play

## Android version

14

## Device model

Samsung Galaxy S21

## First occurred

1 month ago, after upgrade to Android 14 / OneUI 6.0

# Problem Description

## Problem 1

Media controls disappear after playing music in another app.

### STR
1. Play an episode on AntennaPod (media controls are correctly showing in drawer)
2. Open another media app (VLC, Youtube...) and play a media

### Expected behaviour
Media controls should show controls for both apps with pagination. This is what happens when you launch first the other media app, play a media and then open AntennaPod to play an episode.

### Current behaviour
Media controls only show for the other media app, controls for AntennaPod are gone.

**Note: after changing an Android setting called "Pin media player" the AntennaPod media controls are now correctly visible (with pagination), but it does not seem reliable since they still disappear after short time (only other app media controls are then visible).**

## Problem 2

Media controls disappear after turning screen off and waiting 1-2 minutes.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Play an episode on AntennaPod (media controls are correctly showing in drawer)
2. Turn screen off, wait for 1-2 minute.
3. Turn screen back on.

### Expected behaviour

Media controls should still be there.

### Current behaviour

The media controls are gone.

### Logs

_No response_

<hr />

edit: added a note for problem 1 after changing an Android setting (see comments below). It is now more complicated to reproduce.

### App version

3.2.0

Google Play

### Problem you may be having, or feature you want

On my phone I have set User Interface -> Default Page to "Subscriptions", which I love.  Unfortunately, when I connect my phone to my car and use Android Auto it uses a different default page, and as near as I can tell that view doesn't exist at all.

### Suggested solution

In an ideal world whatever setting I had for my Default Page would also be used in Android Auto.  I much prefer this view for my car because the large square icons are much easier for me to see and choose than the smaller text that comes with the list of files.   And regardless of that fact, presumably people who choose a default page will want to use that same view in both places.

Alternatively, if you don't think people would prefer to have the same default page in both environments, you could have a "Default Android Auto Page" that allows users to pick their default screen in Android Auto.


### Screenshots / Drawings / Technical details

This is just a screenshot of what the Subscriptions page looks like on my phone.  I'd like to use that exact page layout in Android Auto.

Body:

### App version

3.2.0

### Where did you get the app from

Google Play

### Problem you may be having, or feature you want

* The current statistics screen contains a mix of historical data (playback; Subscriptions & Years display) and current state-of-affairs (downloads). This is a bit confusing, and distracting.
* The screen is used to manage downloads & cache (see #5987), so should be accessible quickly from the Downloads screen.
* #6875 will cause the other two tabs to take a bit more space (especially in some languages).

### Suggested solution

Move this overview of the current state of affairs to where it belongs: the Downloads screen.
* Remove the 'Downloads' tab from the Statistics screen.
* Add an icon in the 'Downloads' screen: [data usage](https://fonts.google.com/icons?selected=Material%20Symbols%20Outlined%3Adata_usage%3AFILL%400%3Bwght%40400%3BGRAD%400%3Bopsz%4024) or [Incomplete Circle](https://fonts.google.com/icons?selected=Material%20Symbols%20Outlined%3Aincomplete_circle%3AFILL%400%3Bwght%40400%3BGRAD%400%3Bopsz%4024)
  * Tapping on the icon, opens a [full-screen dialog](https://m3.material.io/components/dialogs/specs#bbf1acde-f8d2-4ae1-9d51-343e96c4ac20) with the content as currently in Statistics > Downloads.

Title: Statistics screen improvement: allow to view by 'Month', 'Week' and 'Day'
Body:
### Checklist

- [X] I have used the search function for [**OPEN**](https://github.com/AntennaPod/AntennaPod/issues) issues to see if someone else has already submitted the same feature request.
- [X] I have **also** used the search function for [**CLOSED**](https://github.com/AntennaPod/AntennaPod/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed) issues to see if the feature was already implemented and is just waiting to be released, or if the feature was rejected.

### App version

3.2.0

### Where did you get the app from

Google Play

### Problem you may be having, or feature you want

I would like to see more detail on the statistics screen; not only see the hours per month, but have the option to view by year, week or day.

### Suggested solution

* Rename the tabs:
  * 'Subscriptions' --> 'Per podcast'
  * 'Years' --> 'Per period'
* Add a ['Grouping' icon](https://fonts.google.com/icons?selected=Material%20Symbols%20Outlined%3Agrouped_bar_chart%3AFILL%400%3Bwght%40400%3BGRAD%400%3Bopsz%4024) in the app bar of the 'Per period' screen
  * When tapping the icon, a [basic dialog](https://m3.material.io/components/dialogs/specs#23e479cf-c5a6-4a8b-87b3-1202d51855ac) opens which presents three grouping options:
    * Years
    * Months
    * Weeks
    * Days
  * The selected option determines the defining period
    * The bars take the selected grouping
    * The table takes one level higher (i.e. if the bars show months, the table shows years) - as is currently the case
    * If the highest level is selected (i.e. years), no table is shown
  * By default, the level 'Months' is active - in line with current behaviour
  * Bars are coloured by groups one level higher than selected - in line with current behaviour
    * If the highest level is selected (i.e. years), bars are not colour grouped (every other bar gets the other colour)

### Screenshots / Drawings / Technical details

Title: Audio playback sometimes stops when a Bluetooth device is disconnected, even if it's not the device the app is playing through
Body:
### Checklist

- [X] I have used the search function for [**OPEN**](https://github.com/AntennaPod/AntennaPod/issues) issues to see if someone else has already submitted the same bug report.
- [X] I have **also** used the search function for [**CLOSED**](https://github.com/AntennaPod/AntennaPod/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed) issues to see if the problem is already solved and just waiting to be released.
- [X] I will describe the problem with as much detail as possible.
- [X] If the bug only to occurs with a certain podcast, I will include the URL of that podcast.

### App version

3.2.0

### Where did you get the app from

Google Play

### Android version

Android 14

### Device model

Google Pixel 7a

### First occurred

First observed after update to Android 14

### Steps to reproduce

1. Connect a Bluetooth media device.
2. Begin playing media in AntennaPod through that device.
3. Some time later, use the Android media player widget from the notifications/settings pull-down to change audio playback to be through the phone.
4. Some time later, disconnect / power off the Bluetooth media device that was connected in step 1.

### Expected behaviour

AntennaPod continues to play audio thorough the phone, as was selected in step 3 of the "Steps to reproduce".

### Current behaviour

Audio playback through the phone stops when the Bluetooth device is disconnected.  Playback must be manually restarted from the Android media player widget or in the AntennaPod app itself.

Based on my observations, this behavior may only occur when the Bluetooth device that was disconnected has speakerphone capabilities.  I have tried this with one Bluetooth speaker that doesn't have a microphone (so has no capability to work as a speakerphone), and the behavior does not occur.  However, it consistently occurs on all Bluetooth speakers I have tested with that are capable of being used as a speakerphone.

This behavior was not observed before updating to Android 14.

### Logs

_No response_

Title: Media file missing after successful download
Body:
### Checklist

- [X] I have used the search function for [**OPEN**](https://github.com/AntennaPod/AntennaPod/issues) issues to see if someone else has already submitted the same feature request.
- [X] I have **also** used the search function for [**CLOSED**](https://github.com/AntennaPod/AntennaPod/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed) issues to see if the feature was already implemented and is just waiting to be released, or if the feature was rejected.
- [X] I will describe the problem with as much detail as possible.
- [X] This request contains only one single feature, **not** a list of multiple (related) features.

### App version

3.2.0

### Where did you get the app from

Google Play

### Problem you may be having, or feature you want

I recently mass downloaded about 500 back episodes of a podcast. During this a lot of downloads failed but were still marked as downloaded. The result was that when tapping "play" on an episode, it would display something like "file missing" and the episode would be marked as undownloaded. About 1/3 of the episodes were like this.

It would have been inconvenient to leave it broken because when I reached those episodes after my patreon expired I would have been unable to download them.

To fix it I had to add all the episodes to the queue then go through them tapping play on each one so the app would detect which episodes hadn't been downloaded successfully. 

### Suggested solution

Provide an option in multi-select or in a podcast's settings menu to verify downloads, detect missing files, and mark any missing episodes correctly as undownloaded. That was I can filter by undownloaded on the podcast's page and download all the ones that failed.

P.S. after using my makeshift solution I found the multi-select menu had no option to reset playback position en masse. Is there a reason for this?

Title: AntennaPod Echo enhancements
Body:
As discussed with @ByteHamster, a list of desired improvements to AntennaPod Echo:

- [x] Force recache of top 5 podcast images. Many of the shared images had grey covers (examples: [1](https://twitter.com/teavangelize/status/1733867860756455879), [2](https://twitter.com/Karlos_Georgy/status/1734337340061958232), [3](https://twitter.com/WSEXTonIII/status/1733990904225227217), [4](https://mstdn.io/@steelman/111562798682978793), [5](https://layer8.space/@RyunoKi/111557733849828469), [6](https://paquita.masto.host/@FabianRY/111555666846287361), [7](https://eattherich.club/@category/111607834247212544))
- [ ] Ensure that in top 5 screen, full First place podcast name is visible, even if it's long (example: https://twitter.com/cyberkeak/status/1734568920076485033)
- [x] Ensure that in top 5 screen, if 2nd to 5th place poscasts' names are too long, cut it and put an ellipsis (example: https://twitter.com/NerdpunkE/status/1733773598077178229)
- [x] Improve horizontal aligning of numbers in the top 5 screen (centred below number 1, while title texts are left-aligned)
- [ ] Make design between the screens more differentiated, making it more engaging (needs a designer to come up with proposals)
- [ ] 'Podcast addict' definition: currently based on how quickly you listen after release, but only for those episodes you listen to. Should also consider the total number of hours one listened (we should establish arbitrary cut-offs). (needs discussion)
- [ ] Display the Echo card on whichever screen is selected as AntennaPod's default screen: some users didn't see the block as they don't use the home screen (example: https://toot.site/@cryptoxic/111641350314434338), and we don't want a notification.
- [x] Correct the Echo card flicker (show & hide after a second or so) when not reaching the 10 hours minimum (example: https://chaos.social/@ollibaba/111597116616849308)
- [ ] 'Favourite' definition: currently only based on the number of hours you listened. Should also consider other metrics: how often favourited an episode from that podcast, the percentage of episodes played. (feedback: https://tech.lgbt/@cuboci/111554972640118012)
- [ ] New screens:
  - [ ]  Did you know that x% of your podcasts support new podcast features (transcripts, chapters, finding tag). Podcast Y has all that we recognise!
  - [ ] Your have applied tags to your podcasts, and from among them, x is your favourite.
  - [ ] You use podcasts to fall asleep, during lunch, afternoon activities (based on last played time-in-day).
- [ ] Display Echo until first days of January (feedback: https://forum.antennapod.org/t/thanks-for-the-antennapod-echo/3784/6?u=keunes)

Title: Add option to add new episodes to queue
Body:
Added extra option to "New episodes action" to add all new episodes of a subscription to the queue. This is mostly useful for local feeds where there is no downloading.

I was not sure whether to include inbox or not (setting is radio-style, so user cannot choose one or another) and I decided to include it whenever user uses queue. Not sure if that is correct or not, please advise.

This fixes #5246

Title: added link to playback history to queues toolbar
Body:
Theres still space in the toolbar && i feel this is an organic place for it:
The normal flow goes like this: an episode is added to queue, then I listen to it (eventually) and then the episode moves over to playback history.
So its related to queue. And it happend to me that i wanted to find arecently played episode, searched in queue but it was already gone. it feels natural to search where you've last seen it (queue). now it would be easy from there to head over to playback history. :)

Resolves #6874

**Steps To Reproduce**

Title: [Mac] - First song played exceeds playback time, not skipping to next
Body:
**Describe the bug**
Selected song plays to the end, then instead of skipping to the next song, the timer continues running (beyond the actual playback time).
Hitting "Skip Next" button resolves the issue.

**Steps To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior: (Haven't retried, as I suspect it's a matter of first-ever installation)
1. Use a machine that never installed BlackHole. Or REALLY clean every cache or config that might affect
2. Install the dmg (as downloaded from [Releases - v1.15.10](https://github.com/Sangwan5688/BlackHole/releases/tag/v1.15.10))
3. Open the app, go to 'Home'
4. Click on Search bar
5. Enter some song name
6. Select YTMusic results filter
7. Choose song, let it play to the end

**Expected behavior**
To be short in words, I'll describe the expected behaviour to be to flow normally to the next song, like when the player is not buggy.

**Screenshots**
![timer-exceeded](https://github.com/Sangwan5688/BlackHole/assets/84509513/230e7bd3-acdc-46d3-a402-32c30cf43e83)
*Playback cursor (4:30) exceeds Playback time (3:37)*

**Device Details (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: OSX (macOS Ventura)
 - Version: 13.0
 - Device: MacBook Pro M1 (2021)
 - App Version: v1.15.10

**App Logs:**

Place several songs with individual embedded art and nothing in the [album] tag field into one folder, observe the same picture for all songs

Android 10

Title: [Feature Request] Shuffle play all tracks in a folder without manually selecting tracks one by one
Body:
### Desired behaviour:

1. Navigate to folder containing music files (either in Files or Folders tab)
2. Select Folder without opening inside it
3. Select "Shuffle all" from options menu
4. All tracks in folder are shuffled on queue, with first song being random as per shuffle

Alternatively, a "Shuffle play" button visible when browsing inside a folder, or at least the option to set that in Settings.

### Current behaviour:
1. Navigate to folder containing music files (either in Files or Folders tab)
2. Select Folder 
3. Select "Shuffle all" from options menu
4. Error displayed `Queue (size:0) submitted is empty or start position (0) is out ranged.`

To circumvent this I have to manually select all files in the folder and then select Shuffle All from options. It becomes very impractical for large folders with lots of music.

This can potentially be filed as a Bug as well because of the error above.

Title: [Bug]: Synchronized lyrics don't load correctly
Body:
### Bug Summary

Synchronized lyrics are not shown correctly if there is a space after first timestamp. Example:

If lyrics look like this, then only first line of the lyrics gets shown:

```
[00:14.76] My heart is paralyzed
[00:18.12] My head was oversized
[00:21.58] I'll take the high road like I should
[00:28.43] You said, "It's meant to be
[00:31.90] That it's not you, it's me"
[00:34.54] You're leaving now for my own good
[00:40.52] That's cool, but if my friends ask where you are, I'm gonna say
```

If space between `[00:14.76]` and `My heart` is removed then the lyrics are loaded correctly and all lines are shown correctly.
So below works completely fine.

```
[00:14.76]My heart is paralyzed
[00:18.12] My head was oversized
[00:21.58] I'll take the high road like I should
[00:28.43] You said, "It's meant to be
[00:31.90] That it's not you, it's me"
[00:34.54] You're leaving now for my own good
[00:40.52] That's cool, but if my friends ask where you are, I'm gonna say
```

**Note**: Only the first space has to be removed and lyrics start working correctly.

This is a bug and the first one should work correctly too.

Title: [Feature Request] Stream Player
Body:
Hi dear developers.

I'd like to propose another feature for Phonograph : Stream Player.

I was wondering if I would spend some time to develop a specific app then I thought it would be better to just add that feature to an existing app. Since I've been using Phonograph for some time now, I thought it would be best to have it here.

So here is how I see it :
- a configuration page allows to add streams URLs and associate them with a downloaded image (the original could be removed once integrated into Phonograph database)
- a tab presents a list of stream (with their associated image) : either in list or in grid
- playing a stream wouldn't destroy /reset the local files playlist of course :)

One should be able to switch from a stream to another easily...

Do you think it could be implemented ?

Title: [Feature Request] properly deal with multiple artists
Body:
id3v2.4 and tags for ogg containerized vorbis/opus files support adding multiple artists to a song.
for the latter it would look something like:
TITLE=Under Pressure
ARTIST=Queen
ARTIST=David Bowie

when Phonograph reads such a file it seems to get confused.  In the "Artists" tab only the second artist will show up with the song.  However looking at the file details or the tag editor only shows the first artist.

What should happen is that both artists will be present in the Artists tab and both will have the song associated with them.  When I do a search for each artist both should show up with that song (now only the second does).

Ideally the tag editor would also support multiple artists.  In id3v2.4 it can be confusing as to what separator to use (semincolons or " / " (space slash space) are popular but the null character is official) so ideally we should just be able to get a + or the like and add multiple artists and have the software figure out how to properly add the tag for the filetype.

Title: Hierarchical Tags | Parent/Child Tags
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Detailed tagging can get tedious and redundant. This is one idea to help speed things up in a logical and, hopefully, intuitive way.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
The idea is to set certain tags as a parent for others, so that tagging can be faster and still accurate. Essentially, one tag is describing a "type" and the other tag is describing a "subtype." Every time a subtype is added, the parent tag would be added as well.
A photography example would be setting `Italy` as a dependency for `Venice`, so that every time a photo is tagged with Venice, Italy will also be added automatically. Or having `food` be added when tagging a photo of `pasta`.
A non-photography example would be having a tag for a franchise and another for a character of that franchise. So, if I didn't feel like tagging a character I don't care about, but I want to make sure everyone (me) knows this is from `Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles`, then I can simply add that tag on its own. But, if I want to add `Donatello` or `Shredder` or `April O'Neil`, then the `TMNT` (or the full name) tag gets added automatically. This can also be used for details, like `jewelry` (which could be the parent for `earrings`, `bracelets`, etc.) or any other arbitrary thing.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Aside from others' suggestions of auto-tagging through either AI/ML detection methods or through learning to suggest tags by common association, I can't think of anything else, right now.

**Additional context**
Admittedly, I haven't thought through the editing portion of this, so I'm not sure how best to suggest the interface for that. Mayne long-pressing on a tag from the main Tag Menu (in the shelf) and then adding an option in the 3-dot menu to "Set Parent" or "Set Child" or "Set As Parent(/Child)," which could then open the same kind of menu as the Move To/Copy To options, but it would show the other tags instead of albums? That *might* be easier, since it uses existing elements and, theoretically, wouldn't need to add much new designs or interfaces. 🤷‍♂️

And, thinking it through some more, there may be some issues (bugs) coming up with having unlimited "nesting" levels, but I think it would still be nice to have more than one layer of Parent/Child tags, so that I can select one tag and have two or more be applied as a result. Not sure about multiple parents for a child tag, but obviously this idea is intended to have multiple children attached to a parent tag.
An example would be to have `Captain America` automatically add `Avengers` which would auto add `Marvel`. Or `Flounder` adding `The Little Mermaid` which would add `Disney`.

Maybe a button could be included to also show images tagged with child tags when selecting a tag from the Tag Menu? Idk. Low priority for me, but thought I'd mention it just in case.

Thanks again for this amazing app and the work and creativity you've put into it.

Title: Can't delete image that is in a DCIM subfolder
Body:
**Describe the bug**
Giving storage access for an image located inside a DCIM subfolder and grants access to "DCIM", resulting in a failed message upon pressing delete. 

But doing the same for an image directly inside the DCIM folder grants access to a "dcim" folder and allows for successful deletion of media both in the DCIM folder and its subfolders.

The "DCIM" storage access entry (in Aves settings) doesn't seem correlate with the actual folder on the device (but the "dcim" entry does.)

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Have an image in DCIM/*subfolder*/*image.jpg*

Title: Continue in same direction after deleting a photo
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
After taking a bunch of photos, to delete ones I don't like: sometimes I go through my photos chronologically, sometimes anti-chronologically. After I delete an image, I'd like the next image shown to follow the direction I've been scrolling through the photos.

In other words --  if I am proceeding from newer to older photos, show me the next older photo after I delete something. If I am instead scrolling from old to new, and I delete something, continue to the next new photo (and don't show me a photo I've already looked at, which is current behavior). 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
The app remembers which direction I'm scrolling, so that after I hit "delete" it moves to the next photo in that direction. 

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
None come to mind. 

**Additional context**
I think that this is the default behavior in the samsung gallery

Title: Queue multiple operations
Body:
When deleting/tagging/editing multiple files, it takes a lot of time to process larger numbers of files. like adding 1 tag to 400 images takes several minutes. it would be nice, if one could do multiple edits, like adding a tag to a set of images then removing a tag from another set and so on, without waiting for each operation to finish. 

maybe this could work with some sort of queue system where the operations are stored in a list and then applied consecutively. 
maybe another approach would be to save edits and changes in a database and then sync the database with the actual files.

this slow behaviour is unfortunately a significant hurdle for me to make use of the editing features of aves.

thanks for your awesome work!

Title: Trashed images in a vault can be seen without authentication
Body:
**Describe the bug**

Trashed images in a vault can be seen without authentication.

AFAIK Vaults are implemented by moving the image into the internal storage of Aves that can only be accessed by Aves (and the OS, any app given root and maybe adb IDK). However trashing images in a vault move them to the shared-internal storage (under `/Android/data`) where they can be seen with Files w/o Authentication.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Create a vault (leave anything on default)
2. Move any image to it
3. Trash the image
4. Open Files and move to `/Android/data/<aves app id>/files/trash`
5. Tab on the image

**Expected behavior**

Either trashed images in a vault are kept in the internal storage or behavior is more transparent. 

**Screenshots**

n/a

**System information and logs:**

On request if necessary.

**Additional context**

Vaults have a option to disable the trash for them.

Title: Add SAF folder to collection
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
The Android Media Store API is limited and abstracted.
As a privacy and security enthusiast I use a Common encryption scheme with many platforms (CryptFS)
I mount my images via an encrypted at rest App (EDS Lite)
I can access the images and video via file explorer apps that support Storage Access Framework.
This is not ideal and I'd really like to use Aves with a truly cross platform encryption scheme.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
It would be severely advantageous to add folders manually via Storage Access Framework as that would severely unlock a lot of cool abilities. Not just what I have.
It would allow someone to view SMB Hosted galleries with [Android Samba Client](https://f-droid.org/en/packages/com.google.android.sambadocumentsprovider/)
We could mount cloud services with [Rclone](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=io.github.x0b.rcx&hl=en_CA&gl=US)
Could mount [Webdav](https://github.com/deckerst/aves/issues/126) as well with rclone

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I have installed and uninstalled many gallery apps and basically all of them do not have the ability to manually add a folder to them or mount anything through SAF.

I find that I really like aves, but if I added SAF albums It would become THE Gallery app to rule them all.

Title: automatically offload photos/videos to the (de)centralized storage
Body:
Hi,

The goal is to save space on smartphones. The idea is very close to what iCloud gives (no Android analog as far as I know). The brief description:

    There is a control that defines a threshold. Like, "only 10GB of media is kept locally"
    There's a remote storage setup. Any service that provides API might be used. Would be great to start from [Syncthing ](https://github.com/syncthing/syncthing)integration
    The gallery app (or better a side service) checks the space taken by the media, and when the threshold reached makes sure the media exists on the remote storage and deletes it from the smartphone based on the date of last access (hence, only frequently used media is kept on the smartphone). A thumbnail is generated.
    When a gallery app user hits the thumbnail, the app automatically retrieves the offloaded media

What do you think? Is there a chance to see this in not-so-far future?

Thanks!

Title: input.conf `no-osd` command prefix has no effect
Body:
Android version:
`11`

mpv-android version:
`2023-08-27-release`

I have the following in my `input.conf`:

````console
REWIND no-osd seek -3 exact
FORWARD no-osd seek 3 exact
PLAYPAUSE no-osd cycle pause
````
, the keys function, however, the `no-osd` command prefix does not stop the interface from popping up.

An identical configuration for mpv on Windows works correctly, with nothing popping up when I press the keys.

This is annoying for me because I'm watching Anime with subtitles and I want to use these keys so I can pause to read the subtitles or skip back to read text I missed, and the interface is on the bottom where the subtitles are.

Thank you for this amazing project.

Title: Custom option for horizontal and vertical Touch gestures
Body:
The double tap gestures have a custom option where you can use the respective key codes in input.conf to repurpose them to do something else. Just want to know if the other gestures have a similar key code and if not request their implementation.

I know each drag gesture would have to be split in two for different drag directions but the usefulness of the increased costumization you get outweighs what in my opinion is a relatively simple implementation.

It can even be done without touching the UI. If any of the key codes is used in input.conf it could simply override the default behaviour just like mpv on windows.

Title: Xperia 1 V no 4K output
Body:
### Important information

Android version: 13

mpv-android version: 2023-08-27 release

### Description

I am using an Xperia 1 V, which (along with the rest of the Xperia phones with 4K displays) without modification will only use the full 4K display resolution in specific use cases (primarily video playback), rendering at 2560x1096 otherwise. Unfortunately there isn't (as far as I know) detailed documentation from Sony on how exactly the system works, and how developers can natively support it.
Regardless, both MX Player and VLC appear to work fine displaying 4K videos (without, as far as I'm aware, explicit support from either Sony or the app developers), whereas MPV does not. As an example, here are screenshots of each player displaying a test pattern video
![MX](https://github.com/mpv-android/mpv-android/assets/9629341/6fe2bcb2-15c4-45ef-b4d9-a24cdd975c2b)
![VLC](https://github.com/mpv-android/mpv-android/assets/9629341/81c75d80-e8d4-4d8b-ab6a-82abbf9b9127)
![MPV](https://github.com/mpv-android/mpv-android/assets/9629341/85fdc10e-a419-42fd-8e02-e6c0b718e6d5)
Unfortunately, my brief skimming of VLC's logs when displaying 4K video did not show any clear point where the 4k display was recognized, all the logs about the output resolution appeared to treat it as a 2560x1096 display. Given that in both VLC and MX the 4K output appears to only work whe using HW acceleration, I assume this means the phone is doing some trickery when the app is passing the video off to the hardware, that the app does not have any part in. By extension, I assume it not working MPV is due to some sort of bug in the hardware acceleration, not a missing feature.
Since it is a relatively obscure device (and again, lacks the documentation it really should have), I realize this may be difficult to work on, so please let me know any information or testing I can provide to help find a solution.

Title: How can we edit mpv conf so that we can crop and completely hide OSD?
Body:
As the title says, I am looking to be able to crop a section of some videos when I play them (bottom bar) and also I would like to completely hide any info that spoils the length of a video (in this case it's sports games, so you cannot know the running time, otherwise it will spoil if it went to overtime etc).

Attaching a couple of screenshots, so you know what I want to crop out.

![Screenshot_20230923-163446424](https://github.com/mpv-android/mpv-android/assets/30951795/2b8184b5-af96-41f9-a190-24fab776225e)

![Screenshot_20230923-163446424](https://github.com/mpv-android/mpv-android/assets/30951795/e51c4d26-c988-4682-af77-5836b912a665)

On my PC, I can easily do this.

> vf=crop=in_w:in_h*0.928:0:0
>osc=no
 
I have tried for a long time to play around with the conf on mpv on android and never have been able to crop the video or hide the spoiling status bar/display.

So thought I'd ask here to see if what I want to achieve is possible on android or not.

The other question I have is:

Where can we find the mpv.conf file on android or is it possible to create a folder or file somewhere? It would be much easier to test the configurations if we could just edit the mpv.confi file directly.

So far I've had to do it via mpv -> settings -> advanced.

**So to sum up, questions:**

1. Can we crop a video on Android using the conf?
2. Can we totally hide the OSD/time bars, remaining time, total time on android... or for some reason there will always be that bar showing the time and elapsed bar (screenshot above=?
3. Can we switch to a different profile (with unique settings) by using input.conf? 
4. Where can we find or create the mpv.conf file so that we can more easily edit it?

### Additional information

Tested these settings and no cropping happened and also have that display above (I guess that bar may be external to mpv?)
![image](https://github.com/mpv-android/mpv-android/assets/30951795/4bde6889-805a-44e6-ad04-8d1b19e373f2)
 
Thanks in advance for the feedback and clearing these doubts for me. Tried hard to figure it out myself, but ended up giving up each single time :)

Hope my text made sense!

Title: inconsistent choosing of `mediacodec`(hw+) and `mediacodec-copy`(hw)?
Body:
i have noticed that it is rather inconsistent whether `mediacodec`(hw+) or `mediacodec-copy`(hw) is chosen, though i have found some relations on when something is chosen:

- opening the same file multiple times may sometimes choose "hw" and sometimes "hw+", choosing the same file again (if it was the last one opened / is cached, it almost certainly chooses `hw+`)
- toggling the hw/sw switch while it is still switching from some hw to sw, will almost certainly choose "hw"
- occasional hw/sw switching (while waiting for it to have switched) may choose either (even if "hw+" was previously working fine on the same instance)

(hw/sw switching as in "pushing the button", not opening up the manual selector)

(tested on the exact same file(s) and same device)

is this intended to be this inconsistent or is something in my setup wrong? (mainly asking about the "codec chosen on file open")

to my understanding `mediacodec`(non copy), at least in this case, should always be able to be chosen and should provide better battery efficiency because it does not need to copy anything back (if https://github.com/mpv-android/mpv-android/issues/623 is explanation is correct)

file(s) in question are `mkv` with `h264(high)` or `h265` & `aac`

### Additional information

android 13
mpv-android: 2023-08-27 (fdroid)

to my knowledge i have no filters enabled (aside from scaling?)

Title: mpv has issues entering fullscreen on PC mode

Before I tried to downgrade it, I did a quick check on a borrowed Samsung Tab S7 PC mode(Dex), and there the issue was a bit different, with mpv going fullscreen, but pressing button to change the decoder would cause mpv to exit fullscreen, and never go back until it was restarted, I think some other things also caused the issue, but didn't have enough time to test properly.

Title: Backup or restore the content of `config_dir` in internal storage with zip files
Body:
#### Settings->Advanced->Backup: Zip all the content of `config_dir` in internal storage and allow the user to save it somewhere.

Use cases:
- Let the user backup configuration files
- Allow the user to create a log file in internal storage with the option `log-file=~~home/mpv.log` and export it.


#### Settings->Advanced->Restore: Replace all the content of `config_dir` in internal storage with the content of a zip file the user has chosen.

Use cases:
- Let the user restore configuration files
- Allow the user import scripts into internal storage by editing the zip file 


Would you consider the addition of the above feature to `mpv-android` ? Thank you!

Title: After using Vanilla, adsprpcd keeps device awake constantly [A13]
Body:
- Vanilla Music Version: 1.3.0
- Android Version: LOS20, Android 13
- Phone vendor / model: guacamole
- Application was downloaded from (use `[x]`):
  - [ ] Play Store
  - [x] F-Droid
  - [ ] Other (Nightly Build)
- Did this work in a previous version?
  - [ ] Yes
  - [x] No
  - [ ] NA / Don't know.

## Description

After using Vanilla, device is no longer able to enter Deep Sleep. Battery monitors will show that AWakeLock is responsible for keeping the device awake, this WL is shown to be associated with Qualcomm's aDSP RPC daemon aka adsprpcd

I've been experiencing this problem for a few months now, it was never an issue with LOS18 (Android 11)

Most audio playing apps I have are unaffected, I can use: AntennaPod, VLC, AudioAnchor, and more, without ever triggering this behavior.

The Retro Music Player, OTOH, is affected by the same issue.

The problem has been observed by others as well, this is an [XDA post](https://forum.xda-developers.com/t/app-2-2-11-dec-2022-v3-1-betterbatterystats.1179809/post-88695721) that describes almost exactly the same issue that has been plaguing me.

However, I don't need to enable idle or sleep timers for the problem to emerge, and it does pop up for me even when using wireless headphones.

According to pine74, a similar issue was present in FoldPlay and subsequently fixed by the devs, however that appears to be a closed-source app.

## Steps to reproduce the issue
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Add Vanilla widget to home screen (might be unnecessary)
2. Play some music with Vanilla
3. Stop playing music
4. Lock the phone and let it idle still
5. After a suitable number of minutes has passed, unlock the phone
6. Using BBS, GSam or adb, verify that the Deep Sleep timer no longer increases, the device is indeed kept awake, and that AWakeLock, adsprpcd are responsible.

Title: Notification fix
Body:
Fixes #1151

It appears to be a race condition. It is not based on any kind of thorough debugging and whatsoever, I just had a look at other Java/Kotlin based music players that use the same approach.

The culprit, at least as I see it, is `metadataBuilder` that pushes album art bitmap to the current metadata block.

Putting a metadata builder function call _right after_ pushing cover art to metadata seems to cure this problem.

Also moved the Playback status update after metadata push, as it happens in some other players.

For the reference: https://github.com/SimpleMobileTools/Simple-Music-Player/blob/ef6706fc3f333264b06726ae90fd41ab85a2731b/app/src/main/kotlin/com/simplemobiletools/musicplayer/services/MusicService.kt#L716-L720

Please feel free to close if the PR does not fit for any reason, but on my device it seems to have fixed the problem.

Title: WinGet installs Spotube to CWD
Body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the existing issues

### Current Behavior

WinGet, which downloads Spotube-windows-x86_64-setup.exe, installs Spotube to the current working directory. The installer defaulting to the CWD isn't ideal because when installing non-interactively, you'll end up with Spotube installed at unlikely the desired location.

Presumably this is something you've chosen rather than being the fault of the Inno installer.

### Expected Behavior

The default path suggested by Spotube Windows x64 installers should be _C:\Program Files\Spotube_.

### Steps to reproduce

1. `winget install spotube`
2. Spotube installed to CWD of the CLI.

### Operating System

Windows 11

### Spotube version

3.4.0

### Installation source

GitHub Releases (Binary), WinGet (Windows)

### Additional information

[WingetUI](https://github.com/marticliment/WingetUI) is front-end for Windows package managers and it installs WinGet packages non-interactively by default (which I think is the default behaviour of WinGet itself too). Forcing WinGet to do an interactive installation is the only way around the bug (or by downloading and running Spotube-windows-x86_64-setup.exe directly from GitHub).

WingetUI issue: https://github.com/marticliment/WingetUI/issues/1730

Title: Time elapsed in Discord Rich Presence is not working right
Body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the existing issues

### Pain

The displayed time is the minutes and seconds elapsed while the client is opened with that song. So it doesn't display the actual running time I'm listening to. The biggest problem is that the timer doesn't stop when it's paused either. 

![image](https://github.com/KRTirtho/spotube/assets/84403208/dc11501a-aa5c-44e1-bf41-5661009d64b1)
It looks like I'm a psycho

### Suggested solution

- Pause the time elapsed display when the song is paused 
- Make the display synchronized with the running time 

in `lib/provider/discord_provider.dart`  the time is given by `startTimeStamp: DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch`. It should be something like `startTimeStamp: DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch-(runningTimeInSeconds*1000)`
I don't if  it's easy to get a `runningTimeInSeconds`, but it actually just need to get that value only when it's paused.
### Useful resources


### Additional information

It's technically a bug, but it's a minor one that doesn't actually ruin anything.

Title: docs: reword and improve the support message
Body:
This is based on feedback from a constructive comment I saw on Hacker News: > I hope musicians can pay bills with likes, since ponying up €10.99 is a huge ask.
Key changes made:
- We realistically won't convince anyone to buy Spotify Premium if they are using a free music client
- Mention better options like buying merch, albums, tickets instead of superficial engagement ideas
- I also improved and shortened the wording a bit as it was too long and hard to read before

Title: Scripting/shaders support — adding new features while keeping the base app simple, and getting the community involved
Body:
Take a look at the mpv-android app, its GUI is as simple as in Just Player, but you are able to extend mpv-android’s feature-set with scripts and shaders.

I am writing about this because it would have also been great to have an ExoPlayer-based app with scripting support (for instance, with Lua and Python).

This could get more people into Just Player development, and allow for adding useful features without making the app feel generally bloated. Such as automatic subtitles downloading, custom sharpening algorithms, [inventive stretching options](https://github.com/androidx/media/issues/857), and so on.

Every user would simply choose what they need for their particular use case.

Title: MediaCodecAudioRenderer error when playing videos with DD+ on Pixel 7
Body:
**Describe the bug**
When opening a video file that as a Dolby Digital Plus audio track, the video doesn't play and displays the error:

`MediaCodecAudioRenderer error, index=2, format=Format(2, null, null, audio/eac3, null, -1, en, [-1, -1, -1.0, null], [6, 48000]), format_supported=YES`

There is a details button after the error that displays:

`Decoder failed: c2.dolby.eac3.decoder`

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open video file that has a Dolby Digital Plus audio track ([for example one of the videos with a Dolby Digital Plus audio track on the Dolby page](https://ott.dolby.com/OnDelKits/DDP/Dolby_Digital_Plus_Online_Delivery_Kit_v1.4.1/Test_Signals/muxed_streams/MP4/MP4_Muxed_Streams.html))
2. Video doesn't play and error on screen displays: `MediaCodecAudioRenderer error, index=2, format=Format(2, null, null, audio/eac3, null, -1, en, [-1, -1, -1.0, null], [6, 48000]), format_supported=YES`

**Expected behavior**
Video should play with audio

**Device (please complete the following information):**
 - Model: Google Pixel 7
 - OS: Android 14
 - Version: 0.154

**Media**
Any media that has an audio format listed by MediaInfo as `E-AC-3 JOC`

**Additional context**
I found a workaround where the video will play by toggling the decoder priority in the video settings. Steps for the workaround:
1. Open video with Dolby Digital Plus audio track
2. Audio decoding error on screen
3. Go to settings by long pressing the cog button
4. Change `Decoder priority` from `Prefer device decoders (default)` to `Prefer app decoders`
5. Exit settings
6. Try to play video again and get the same audio decoding error
7. Go back to settings by long pressing the cog button
8. Change `Decoder priority` back to `Prefer device decoders (default)`
9. Exit settings
10. Play video again and video will play with audio and video

I've attached a screen recoding of the workaround (I removed the audio in the recording, but the audio does play)

https://github.com/moneytoo/Player/assets/6766805/d34c978d-5e12-488b-94a1-92a08f831de0

Title: Low quality content smoothing not working
Body:
**Describe the bug**
When using Just Player to play 480p content, the LG C3 low resolution content smoothing is not working, resulting in a picture with a lot of noise

**To Reproduce**
With a recent TV supporting low quality content smoothing (tested on LG C3):

- Open a 480p content in Just Player (firestick tv 4k max): content is *not smoothed*
- Open a 480p content in the Jellyfin (firestick tv 4k max) or via a USB drive connected to the TV: content is correctly smoothed

**Expected behavior**
Content smoothing should work properly, as it happens with the Jellyfin app

**Device (please complete the following information):**
 - Model: Fire Tv Stick 4k Max 1st gen 2021, LG C3
 - OS: Fire Os 7.6.6.4 (Android 9)
 - Version: 0.143

**Additional context**
In my understanding, the issue is that the stream information is not passed correctly to the TV, which thinks that a native 4k stream is being played instead of 480p. Enabling "Tunneled playback" or "Auto frame rate matching" does not fix this.

Thanks
