Title: REMUX MKV RIP Playback - DoVi Profile 7 Issues - Multiple Devices / Android Versions
Body:
**Describe the bug**
I have multiple Android TV boxes and/or Smart TVs in our home. I am using Just Player 1.51 as the primary player across all my devices. DoVi Profile 7 failback only works on my devices that support the full range of DoVi encoded profiles. 

With my Sony Bravia Android TV we can natively play DoVi Profile 7 videos. On some rips the videos default to HDR anyways without an error and there is no way, that I am aware of, to run a debug to find out why this is happening. This issue exists regardless of the switch turned in the advanced settings is toggled to play it unchanged or play it in HDR mode. Using the prefer decoder settings toggles doesn't impact the failover functionality. Tunneling also does not impact or change this functionality. No combination of Tunneling on/off, combined with decoder preferences, or DoVi failback. I went through a rigorous testing process to verify each combination. 

With my RockTek G2 Android TV I cannot run DoVi Profile 7 videos in Just Player. I get the error code saying that the video file exceeds the limit of the supported format. This issue exists regardless of the switch turned in the advanced settings is toggled to play it unchanged or play it in HDR mode. Using the prefer decoder settings toggles doesn't impact the failover functionality. Tunneling also does not impact or change this functionality. No combination of Tunneling on/off, combined with decoder preferences, or DoVi failback. I went through a rigorous testing process to verify each combination. Oddly enough on this device DoVi failback and/or DoVi pass through of Profile 7 work in Nova Player on this same machine. I have Nova Player setup to use the Android Media Library, basically the same as Just. Just Player can play and passthrough other DoVi profiles to the my Sony Bravia, and I can verify this by checking the HDR/Picture settings the picture works fantastic. Dolby Vision Bright and Dolby Vision Dark display perfectly. With this box there is also no issue playing any Dolby Audio formats. The DolbyVision rips that work are MKV UHD 2160P x/h264 or HEVC. Web-DL, WEB, and x/h264 HEVC DoVi haven't been an issue. 

**Expected behavior**
DoVi Profile 7 movies failback to HDR 10 when opening Hybrid REMUXs that include DoVi Profile 7 and HDR10 / 10+.
(Nova Player's implementation works better) 

**Device (please complete the following information):**
Sony Bravia Android TV
 - Model: Sony X950H (XBR-65950H) 
 - Processor: MediaTek Mt8993
 - OS: Android 9
 - Version: Bravia_UR3_UC-user 9 PTT1.190515.001.S104 650661

RokTek G2
- Model: RT-G2 / SEI804RT (YYJ)
- Processor: Amlogic S905X4
- Android 11
- Version: RTT0.211009.001.53100

*** WILLING TO PAY TO GET THIS BUG RESOLVED ***
$150-200 CAD

Title: Cache and ftp

Title: Inconsistent UI hiding behavior when you tap to hide as opposed to letting it hide itself
Body:
1) When allowing the UI to hide itself, most of the UI (the video overlay) goes away, but the progress line at the bottom, the nav buttons and the taskbar remain, and then they all finally hide. This is not preferred and just makes the UI hiding process last even longer by giving it 2 steps. The entire UI should vanish in 1 step, leaving only the video.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/49374058/235105695-d4a08a8c-0978-4af1-9a64-97dc76c1960a.mp4

2) When you tap to hide the UI, the entire UI vanishes, It happens in 1 step. This is the preferred behavior and should always be how the UI is hidden. 

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/49374058/235105609-8f21b610-dd35-4513-b2f8-03ba8febdf27.mp4

The entire UI should hide itself in 1 step when tapped or when allowed to hide itself. This makes the UI hide behavior more consistent. Please make the UI hide itself in 1 step when allowing the UI to hide itself. Allowing the UI to hide itself is what most people will do anyway when watching fullscreen video, so making that process happen in 1 step is important and saves the viewer from having to see the UI for more than they should have to.

Title: TV UI controller bug
Body:
When using a TV device there's a visual bug that occurs when selecting either subtitle, audio track or speed variation while the player is playing.

When these selection items are visible and the player is playing, after a short delay the controller disappears and the selection box remains on the screen indefinately.

The bug occurs after the controller disappears and a selection is made from the still visible selection box, where soon after there is a flash on the screen which briefly shows the controller again.

I checked version 0.23 with update "Hide controls only during playback" and the bug occurs from this point.

This behavior doesn't occur on mobile UI because the controller remains visible and doesn't hide until after the selection has been made, so TV UI should probably behave the same

Title: [Feature Request] Display Follow Requests More Clearly Everywhere Where a User can be Followed
Body:
To explain my problem clearly, if I look at the amount of favorites or boosts a post has received, the Mastodon app displays a list of people and next to their name gives the option to follow them / follow them back:

![Normal Follow vs. Follow Back](https://github.com/mastodon/mastodon-android/assets/52011431/5dca7075-8c80-4585-9524-d92093f7d508)

Whether I follow someone usually depends on the quality of their posts or often I recognize their name because I saw them on my home timeline, etc. Whether they already follow me is usually not an important factor, but other people may see that differently so I see the value in discerning between a simple "**Follow**" and a "**Follow Back**", as well as the display "**Mutuals**". 

However, this distinction is not being displayed when sending a follow **_request_**.

I would like for a **simple follow to also be differentiated from a** _Follow Request_ because many users have, for good reasons, set their accounts to private and often display terms for following them in their bio.

From the list of people who follow you or who interacted with one of your posts, **one cannot distinguish between sending a follow request to a user or simply following them**: 

![Normal Follow vs. Follow Request](https://github.com/mastodon/mastodon-android/assets/52011431/1aeb5721-8d57-412f-af4c-ff79a31bb93b)
(_if you press the "**Follow**" button next to the first user it simply follows them, if you press the "**Follow**" button next to the second user, you send a follow request_)

Simply revoking the follow request still sends a notification and can, rightfully so, be seen as an annoyance to users. I cannot judge whether this is a problem multiple people face, hence I do not know whether this feature is a priority. It is certainly not hard to just look at the profile of each user before following them - I just think generally replacing the button you press with a different button if a different action is performed is very helpful. Sending a follow request vs. simply following that person are two very different actions in my opinion.

This icon helps:

![Follow Request Icon](https://github.com/mastodon/mastodon-android/assets/52011431/cc741aa6-ff94-4792-92f5-0a2387dfd08e)

However, I'd wish for something like this:
![Follow Request Button](https://github.com/mastodon/mastodon-android/assets/52011431/0c9ad2d7-cfd2-424f-a830-5c825faf0370)

And then that "Follow Request" button to be standardized across all places where it can be pressed, just like the "Follow Back" button (_across the lists where you can show **Favorites** and **Boosts**, as well as the **Followers** list_) 

# Thank you for reading through my concern and developing Mastodon <3! 🦣

Title: Add audio controls for background playback
Body:
1. Come across a post with audio attached.
2. Start playback.
3. Scroll away, read other posts, switch timelines: Mastodon continues playing the audio, and you cannot stop it without manually going all the way back to the original place where you initially found the audio.

There are a couple different solutions to this:

1. Automatically stop the playback when the user leaves the post. Better UX than currently, but still not good. Trivial to implement.

2. Similarly to WhatsApp, Signal, Telegram etc., provide a small one-line audio control popup fixed to the top of the screen. Fantastic UX, but implementation would take time, as it would affect the UI.

3. Create a notification that provides audio controls, similarly to music apps. Good UX, implementation shouldn't take too long, as it wouldn't affect the UI.

Title: Improve handling of skipped posts when scrolling up the timeline
Body:
I like to read my timeline in chronological order, that is, from bottom to top.

When returning to the app and scrolling up on my timeline from where I left off, sometimes I see a break in the posts with a grey "load more" message. Tapping that message loads any posts that were skipped between where I left off, and the newest ones.

The problem is that those posts cause my current position to be shifted downwards out of view. If I want to continue reading from where I was, I need to scroll down and look for the post I last read.

I'm not sure if anchoring the bottom position and pushing the new posts towards the top would work for people who read their timeline in reverse (top-to-bottom). Does scrolling top-to-bottom even cause the "read more" separator to appear?

Anyway, it would be nice if either the bottom position could be retained, or if there would be an option to disable the "load more" functionality  altogether.

Title: Parametric EQ ?
Body:
Let me apologize if this has already been discussed elsewhere, but I could find no mention of it.

Would it be possible to have a Parametric EQ implemented?

The Arbitrary Response Equalizer and AutoEQ are cool, of course.

However, I very much prefer using oratory1990's hand-tweaked presets, which are constructed for a 10-band Parametric EQ, whereas an AutoEQ profile has dozens and dozens of nodes.

The Parametric EQ is much better because it allows further, easy customization of the sound directly on the device.

To illustrate why, here is an excerpt from one of oratory1990's documents:

```
Adjust gain of band 1 to preference (Bass)
Adjust gain of band 5 to preference (boxiness/tonality)
Adjust gain of band 8 to preference (treble presence)
Adjust gain of band 10 to preference (airiness)
```

In this case two of the bands are shelf, and two are peak with specific Q-factors. With just four sliders, it is possible to affect wide ranges of frequencies easily, and do so in a way that is meaningful and easily comprehensible for everyone.

As far as I understand Wavelet has a Parametric EQ, and that's the reason why the AutoEQ project maintainer recommends it.

However, RootlessJamesDSP works better on my device, and of course I would rather use a FOSS project over a closed-source one whenever possible.

Title: [Feature request] Add option to "Store in subfolders based on year and month."
Body:
**Problem**
The backup/upload feature from Lespas just replicates the file structure exactly as it is from Android. Over a long period of time, this makes it hard to find photos easily on the nextcloud server (not on the phone). And requires organisation efforts.

**Solution I'd like**
In the official nextcloud app for Android, there is an option under auto-upload to "Use subfolders - Store in subfolders based on year and month". 

When enabled, this option checks the dates that the photos were originally taken or files created on the phone, and uploads into subfolders structured by Years/Months in my base upload folder on server. Thus even older photos go to the right subfolder.

This organisation system makes it SO EASY to find exactly what I am looking for after years, without putting in ANY effort into either organising albums or searching for them.

**Feature Requested**
Add the same "Use subfolders" option to the upload configuration menu in les pas for each folder. **Specifically for the Backup archive folder stored on server.**

**Additional context**
Screenshots from Nextcloud app's implementation below.

Title: Third-party app access support + Better Setup Documentation
Body:
Hello, I have already emailed @scubajeff about this topic, but unfortunately, I have not received an answer. So, I am now trying it like this.

I'm having a hard time setting up LesPas correctly, especially because I couldn't find any instructions on how to do it.

I currently use a Samsung mobile phone, which syncs the pictures and videos created with the camera to OneDrive via the Samsung Gallery app. This app also takes care of cleaning up my phone memory afterwards.

I would like to replace OneDrive. For this reason, I have tested various self-hosted cloud alternatives, including NextCloud. Uploading pictures has never been a problem, but cleaning up the phone and accessing uploaded data has been.

I would like to have it as follows:

1. Take a photo on the mobile phone → photo is saved in the gallery on the mobile phone.

2. An app, possibly LesPas, transfers the data to the cloud server at regular intervals.

3. After a certain time interval, e.g., after half a year or when the phone's memory is full. A cleanup is started on the phone, which removes all photos that have been uploaded and are older than the configured interval from the device.

I have been able to achieve steps 1 – 3 with LesPas so far. Now the question is, how can I access these pictures again? They are no longer visible in the gallery app used by the phone. They don't show up in the LesPas app either, but I can see them with the Nextcloud app.

I could now create a remote album in the LesPas app and link the pictures manually, but that would be quite a hassle. In addition, these pictures no longer appear in third-party apps such as WhatsApp, Instagram, etc.

The ideal behaviour would be for LesPas to replace the local files with a placeholder that downloads the original from the server when used.

Alternatively, when pictures are removed from your device, they are automatically moved to a remote album with the same name as the local album.

However, it is solved, the pictures stored on the server should still appear in WhatsApp or similar and be downloaded from the server if necessary.

Can I achieve this behaviour with LesPas and if so, how? Without the possibility of using the images stored on the server in third-party apps, this variant of cloud storage is unfortunately not suitable for me either.

I could cope with the behaviour myself, but I can't impose this overhead on my family.

Best regards

Title: UI improvement - more consistency
Body:
I am using Les Pas together with friends and family, and one thing I realized was that they found it difficult at some points to understand the GUI easily. So, I would like to give a recommendation on this basis, but please don't hesitate to simply neglect this if you think otherwise :)

One little problem was the different design choice for the same functionality on different windows. I can remember on two things right now: 

1. The move/copy button is different if you are in the gallery or in a Les Pas album and have pictures selected as presented on the screenshots here:

**Gallery**
![Screenshot_20230620-082352_Les Pas_1.png](https://github.com/scubajeff/lespas/assets/83548283/5a3de0f4-deae-4506-926a-26131dd0aefd)

**Les Pas album**
![Screenshot_20230620-082444_Les Pas_1.png](https://github.com/scubajeff/lespas/assets/83548283/89419b3b-5f1d-4333-9f6d-3fff8bb80308)

![Screenshot_20230620-082448_Les Pas_1.png](https://github.com/scubajeff/lespas/assets/83548283/d5c2e0c1-b203-44b1-8ab1-6df8e0124ff0)



Proposed solution: Use the same three icons (Trash, Share and Les Pas icon) on both windows if a picture is selected. So basically replace the "to album" in this submenu by a les pas icon, which is in between the trash and the share icon. Obviously, the dots for the menu would remain on the right side, to access functionalities, which are only relevant for Les Pas albums and not that frequently used to keep the interface clean. I generally think, that one main use case of Les Pas is sorting photos and thus, having delete and move/copy directly available without being placed in a menu makes sense to me.

2. Referring to #126: When creating a new album, a magnifying glass is displaced to filter by name, but on the main screen it is only available via the filter by name entry in the menu.

Proposed solution: On the main window, I would directly display the magnifying gas and ones it is clicked, there can be this light gray text showing filter by name. This way, the search functionality can still be called search in the menu, which is used to search for faces and so on. So no renaming necessary, but the function would be more intuitive for normal, not techie users.

Title: Improvements to digiimport.py
Body:
Hi, I couldn't find a way to open a ticket for the [digiimport script](https://gitlab.com/steviehs/stevemisc/-/blob/master/digiimport.py) here: https://gitlab.com/steviehs/stevemisc

The script works excellently for images with exif information, however it didn't work for me for Signal images (which has no exif information). The regex rules for the Signal images also need to be more complex as the Signal app saves images in various formats. 

I did a 2.3 gb Signal export (3k images) and re-wrote the script to suit my needs. Main changes:

1. Regex rules updated to suit the various versions of "signal-" filenames in my export
2. Removal of exif check (this slows the script down immensely)
3. Output format somewhat changed to accommodate Signal filenames

If anyone wishes to optimise the script more that would be great, I didn't know any regex or python before I did this, most likely it is not done in an optimal way. That said, it I used it for my 3k Signal images in various formats, so works OK.

I hope this can be useful to others as well

Andreas

edit: added v2 as I had the same problem with WhatsApp images


v1: [digiimport-NoExif.zip](https://github.com/scubajeff/lespas/files/10097144/digiimport-NoExif.zip)

v2: [digiimport-NoExif.zip](https://github.com/scubajeff/lespas/files/10097209/digiimport-NoExif.zip)

Title: Multiple lespas folders for easier sharing workflow / family album
Body:
(As you already know) I have organized my digital memories in YYYY/MM/(optional Event) Folders, which are flattened by my [phonepics.py](https://gitlab.com/steviehs/stevemisc/-/blob/master/phonepics.py) script. 

Right now I have a flattened folder list with approx 400 entries, one new every month at least.

Some of my albums are only for my, some of them are shared with my family, which is a group on nextcloud. So have expanded the phonepics script to add sharing on Nextcloud via it's api if this is demanded. 

As you can imagine, it looks really ugly in the root folder of the family member and he has to move all this shares in a lespas folder on his side (and this also once a month for the new folder)

An alternative would be to setup the "shared_with_me" folder to lespas in Nextcloud's configuration, but this would irritate users who receives other shares than pictures. 

I tried to play around with group folders and more granular permission inside, but it seems rather complicated to script this. 

The easiest way to achieve this behaviour would be - from my perspective - to support multiple lespas folders via the app. Maybe just a regex in finding the folders would be sufficient, so lespas would not only look for the lespas folder but for any lespas* folder. With this, every family member could easily share his pictures with the rest of the family and does not need to setup complicated sharing stuff. 

Do you think there will be a chance for that? I know it has been discussed in the past, but I wanted to get around it with no success with the existing means :-)

Title: Extract media from les pas private storage ("export all"-) option #FeatureRequest
Body:
Hi @scubajeff,

I know, that you probably thought about this a lot, since you mentioned your reasoning (privacy) in the readme file, but I wanted to give you 2 arguments for giving the user the option to store media in an external directory.

1. Preventing data loss. Uninstalling les pas would no longer remove all users media (see https://github.com/scubajeff/lespas/issues/56).
2. Decoupling from nextcloud backend. Les pas could be used as a local gallery and album manager and the external media directory could be synced for example through syncthing (p2p) or foldersync (FTP, SSH, Samba, Webdav, a lot of cloud providers).

I'm curious about your opinion.

Title: Orientation/Upper Screen Issues
Body:
**Describe the bug**
I have noticed two issues relating to the upper part of the screen either not working or not displaying properly in certain situations.
The player controls for videos at the top of the screen do not work in portrait mode unless the buttons are tapped quickly after the player control bar appears. Landscape mode does not seem to be affected. In addition, the title bar at the top of the screen that either displays "Les Pas" or the currently selected album's name is moved slightly below the upper edge of the screen if an image/video is viewed and then backed out of quickly afterwards. Opening another image and waiting before going back seems to fix the issue.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior for the player controls:
1. Open a video in portrait mode.
2. Wait ~1 second for the media player controls to appear at the top, tapping the screen if necessary.
3. Attempt to interact with any of the controls.

Steps to reproduce the behavior for the title bar:
1. Open an image/video.
2. Quickly back out to the album view.
3. Note how the upper title bar is no longer at the upper edge of the screen and has been moved slightly down.

**Expected behavior**
I expect the video player controls to work regardless of how long the bar has been on-screen and I expect the title bar to remain at the very top of the screen whenever it is being displayed normally.

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
 - Device: Pixel 5A
 - OS: Android 12
 - Version 2.4.5

**Additional context**
This may be related to some of the updates/bug fixes related to screen orientation/resizing.

Title: support for printers with TSPL protocol
Body:
Added support for TSPL protocol.

Allows printing to TSC-based printers including the majority of cheap generic thermal label printers, for usually less than 100€ (e.g. TSC, Yinleader, Vretti, Vevor, Phomemo, …)

Features:
- tested and working over Bluetooth and USB
- applies floyd-steinberg-dithering on the android device, because TSPL printers only support threshold-dithering.
- supports different page sensors: gap, black mark or continuous

Seeking help and opinion:
- The default page size is that of KLT-labels (Kleinlastenträger). We use them, because they're cheap and so are we. Anyone who uses these printers probably is, too. I'm open to changing it to default ticket sizes.
- The settings might be too powerful and confuse users. Please remove any options you consider too much (except the print height, which is required)
- The settings fragment has many duplicate calls to view.findById. For the sake of consistency with other modules, I did not DRY them.
- Might work over LAN, but I don't have a testing device with ethernet/wifi. I excluded ethernet for now.
- Although it works great, I marked it as experimental. I think anyone who's afraid of that, probably shouldn't use such cheap printers anyways ...


This is my first pull request, so please forgive any foolish mistakes. I'm open to feedback, roasting and rejection.

Title: Sunmi: Migrate to new printer SDK
Try to speedup ESCLabel printing (Z#23124315)
Body: Switch ESCLabel image transmission to binary, use other print command that may be faster

Title: ESC/Label: Shrink payload size
Body:
Right now, we are using the `P,P`-command to encode and transmit the picture-payload to the printer.

We might (!) be able to speed up the printing process by using the `B,P`-notation instead.

<details>
<summary>Implementation Details from Epson</summary>
Lets first understand the parameters "P,P" vs "B,P"
 
"P,P", this means the following data is in ZB64 encoding.

Base64 encoding means 6 bits per character for increased compactness/ efficiency.
 
Their command to upload the image to E: is:
 
~DYE:1_01.PNG,P,P,177595,0, ....
 
But the data "..." has 236806 bytes, not 177595. Why?: 

There are 12 control character bytes:

5 prefix: ":B64:" - to show the data is ZB64
7 suffix: "==:76EF" - to show the CRC
 
236806 - 12 = 236794

236794 * 0.75 = 177595 (where 0.75 is the conversion rate from 8 bit to 6 bit)
This is where 177595 comes from.
To change the parameters from "P,P" to "B,P", the data will also need to be transformed. 
So you cannot simply change from "P,P" to "B,P"
But I would need to investigate what is required for a conversion of the data. further.
 
Note there is a key printer limitation of uploading ZB64 data to E: (permanent) memory .

It is extremely slow is due to significant translational complexity -- where it does the 8 to 6 bit conversions.
So avoid "P,P" when uploading to "E:"
 
Countermeasure:
Instead this can be mitigated if you send the data to R: instead =>
~DYR:TEST.PNG,P,P,177595,40, ....
 
The upload time is instant.
Then apply the  ^TO command, to move the data to E:, which is instant.
XA^TOR:TEST.PNG,E:TEST.PNG^XZ
 
Please make this suggestion.
But ensure the FW is latest, if it is older than 06.32.TG19N5 then a "Memory Full Error" will occur.
</details>

Title: Label mode printing for Sunmi devices with integrated printer
Body:
This implementation extends and improves the support for printing on Sunmi devices, more specifically for badge printing.

It implements support for "label mode" (and should also work for "black mark mode") printing on compatible Sunmi devices for printing badges, where the device makes sure that the print is well aligned/synchronised to the start of a label. After printing the label, it also feeds the roll to the correct position in a gap between 2 labels to tear off the printed label(s). The device will retreat the fed paper again a bit, when necessary, at the start of the next print job so that also subsequent prints are aligned correctly with respect to their labels.

It supports devices with both a 58mm printer and an 80mm printer. It is flexible towards all device-compatible label sizes.

This implementation was tested on a Sunmi V2s Plus device (P06050006). The combination of a built-in 2D scanner engine and an 80mm printer makes it a very interesting mobile all-in-one device for validating pretix tickets as well as badge printing.

Sources:
* [SUNMI Printing SDK Overview](https://developer.sunmi.com/docs/en-US/xeghjk491/maceghjk502)
* [APIs for Printing Labels & Receipts](https://developer.sunmi.com/docs/en-US/xeghjk491/mameghjk546)

Title: Added Brother Print SDK for Android implementation
Body:
Implemented the Brother Print SDK for Android, which allows printing on Brother QL series label printers via USB, network and Bluetooth.

It also offers additional settings (print resolution, auto-cut on/off, ...).

Bitmaps to be printed are scaled and centred if necessary.

Tested on a Brother QL-720NW (USB and network) and on a Brother QL-820NWB (Bluetooth).

Title: Add Bluetooth support for Brother label printers (QL series)
Body:
Currently, the app supports the use of Brother label printers (QL series), connected via LAN/WLAN or USB. The Brother QL-820NWB and QL-1100NWB, however, also support printing via Bluetooth, the use of which for these printers is not yet supported by the pretixPRINT Android app.

Adding Bluetooth support for these printers to the pretixPRINT app would be interesting to us for the following reasons:

- The Brother QL-820NWB can optionally be equipped with a battery pack, providing large flexibility for where it can be used. It can thus be used in a very mobile way, as no power socket is required.
- Printing via Bluetooth does not require the presence of and access to a WIFI network (as connecting via WLAN does). This is thus very convenient when organising events at a location which you do not own yourself, but for instance rent for an evening. No network configuration is thus necessary. The mobile phone running the pretixSCAN app can connect to WIFI (if present) or 3G/4G/5G, while for printing, only a one-off Bluetooth pairing with the printer is necessary.
- These Brother label printers, offer the DK-11234 60mm x 86mm name badge media format, which would be very interesting to use as badges in combination with pretix.

Title: Draft: Maintenance
Body:
This PR adds a maintenance utility to configured printer entries. With this, it is possible to send maintenance data packets to the printer to configure stuff like paper sensor calibration values.

It accepts data in ascii or hex (with `0x`-prefixes, spaced or simply endless hex characters) formats.

![maintain](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/172415/168923167-a87590aa-5181-494d-ab63-6e4703ef086b.png)

Maintenance and the also added printer removal feature are placed in the new three dots `⋮` menu on the right side of each printer row.

This PR also migrates the settings screen from the deprecated `android.preference.PreferenceFragment` to `androidx.preferences.PreferenceFragmentCompat`

Title: Give up timer on long buffering
Body:
### Describe your suggested feature

# Problem:
The player sometimes enters an endless loading mode, when the screen is black and the user has no control over the player, other than closing it.

Rarely, the piped backend fixes the issue and the playback resumes, but it usually takes > 30 minutes, by which point, the user most likely gave up.

# Possible solution
Have a timer from when the last frame was received from the Piped instance and played. If it exceeds a predefined value (maybe 10 minutes), it gives up for good and doesn't try to start loading the video again until the user explicitly requests it.
This state could be indicated with the play button appearing, the same as when the video is paused.

Maybe this would only work when the screen is turned off?
Since if the video doesn't start playing even after 10 minutes, it is safe to assume the user gave up.

# TLDR
Give up on playing the video after N seconds from the last frame received (from Piped instance).

# Priority
Low.

Title: When exiting full screen, video stops being interactable (if it ended, it starts again)...
Body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Click on video
2. Enter fullscreen
3. Exit fullscreen

### Expected behavior

Video should exit fullscreen without interruptions like loading again, or inconveniences like going back some seconds after loading.

Also, it shouldn't have 'visual glitches' like hiding the name of the channel, or stop being interactable.

### Actual behavior

Video loads a bit (which should not be necessary if  the video had that part already buffered), and then resumes, but it resumes some seconds before the current position.

Also (and maybe more important), it stops being interactable (it seems you still can double tap to pause, go back or forward, but no such icons appear on screen, which makes it very hard to do it), and it stops displaying the channel's name that's below the video. 

Finally, if one exits fullscreen when video has already ended, it starts playing again from the beginning (or, as it seems, from last video position that was saved in watch history). This one is very inconvenient when, for example, I just watched a music video and then exit fullscreen.

All in all, very strange behavior.

### LibreTube version

0.20.1

### Android version

Android 13

### Other details



https://github.com/libre-tube/LibreTube/assets/143112461/54770d1d-8d6c-4b9e-9433-525c5cdf52c4

Title: Videos stop playing when locked, because Libretube is put to sleep
Body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Play any video
2. Lock your screen
3. Wait 5 minutes (This may depend on how aggressive the device is with power management)

### Expected behavior

Video should continue playing.

### Actual behavior

Video stops playing.

### LibreTube version

0.19.0

### Android version

Android 13

### Other details

This is because Libretube needs to acquire a wakelock https://developer.android.com/training/scheduling/wakelock
in order for Android to not put Libretube to sleep after a few minutes.

Exoplayer can acquire this wakelock for us when it makes sense (i.e. video playing while the screen is off), so I thought I had a 10 line fix for this.
```kotlin
// for online usage
player.setWakeMode(WAKE_MODE_NETWORK)
// for offline usage
player.setWakeMode(WAKE_MODE_LOCAL)
```
https://developer.android.com/reference/androidx/media3/exoplayer/ExoPlayer#setWakeMode(int)


However when debugging afterwards I learned that exoplayer can only do this when it runs in a service like is already done in the audio player.

This should probably be implemented by extending the Offline and OnlinePlayerServices to also support video, and offloading all PlayerFragement player interactions to it.

The officially recommended solution is a [mediasessionservice](https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/media/session/mediasessionservice), however that would require a major refactor which is probably not something that can be easily done (I tried and failed miserably, but I also know little about android development).

I will send a PR to fix the audio player, it is less affected because it is already running as a foreground service, but will also be put to sleep after a longer interval or when Battery Saver is active.

I'm also trying to fix the video player but I'm not sure if I will succeed.

Title: App freezes on load after not using for extended period of time

0.19.0

Android 10

### Other details

I typically have my phone in Medium Power Saving Mode.

Also, the number of subscriptions may have something to do with this. You get a ton of notifications in an instant after killing and relaunching the app, which is why I think channel subscription count might be related.

Title: Consistency on swipe down to hide DMP
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
To hide DMP, you can use gestures and swipe down, but it only registers your swipe if the first swipe point is outside the app's window.
This becomes difficult to do when the music file you're opening has an image file attached (a cover) since you can only swipe on the outside of that image and tge app's window areas.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Let the user swipe DMP down from anywhere on the screen, even when starting the swipe on the dialog window utself.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Since there is no actions related the cover picture (that i know of), let users swipe tge app down starting from inside the image's area.

**Additional context**
Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

Title: Add support for WMA audio playback
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Currently, the player is unable to play .wma files.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
For the player to be able to play .wma audio files.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
The alternative is to either convert .wma to .mp3 or use a different audio player that supports .wma.

**Additional context**
Used the Material Files app to navigate to the .wma file and selected DialogMusicPlayer to play it.

Title: Close DMP after file finishes playing
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
A current bug makes DMP repeat playing the last played file after certain actions in other apps. This could be avoided if DMP wasn't running at all after it ended playing the lart file.

Also most of the time i don't find myself needing to play again files i previously listened to. Since my workflow implies switching apps and opening the top menu for the smartphone pretty often, i have to make with the passive dmp notification player taking a good bunch of space, and the app appearing in the Recent/Active Apps button (◻️ or ≡).
Closing the notification and app would do, but it would gelp having the app do it for me

**Describe the solution you'd like**
DMP should close altogether after the previous file finished playing. This could be an option.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
It could close after a determinate amount of time (1 minute, 20 seconds, 10 seconds maybe) to allow the user to play it again.

**Additional context**
As DMP opens very fast, i don't see much drawbacks to this.
It probably will conflict with some people's workflow, hence the ability to make it an option.
This is mainly to circumvent the mentionned bug, but it would still be interesting as a feature.

Title: Fallback to accent color upon dynamic colors' unavailability
Body: Fallback to default DMP accent color (`#E8374B`) on A13 if dynamic colors are not generated. Currently it falls back to default color `#6750A4`. I have disabled dynamic colors on DMP for A12 for this issue. Labeling this as help-wanted, any help will be appreciated! 🙂

Title: Question on permissions
Body:
My recently improved scanner just reported the following for today's update of SimpMusic:

```
! repo/com.maxrave.simpmusic_15.apk declares flag(s): usesCleartextTraffic
! repo/com.maxrave.simpmusic_15.apk declares risky permission(s): android.permission.READ_MEDIA_AUDIO android.permission.READ_MEDIA_IMAGES android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE
```

Now comparing it to the app description, I don't find what they might be needed for:

* `usesCleartextTraffic`: description names YouTube as (only) source, which uses HTTPS. What for are cleartext connections (HTTP without the S) needed then?
* storage: if streaming from the net, what for is local storage access needed? And especially:
* `READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE`: the minimum Android version your app supports is Android 8. Since Android 5, there's SAF (Storage Access Framework) available for file system access – so this permission should not be needed at all unless your app is supposed to support Android < 5?

Thanks in advance for clarification!

Have a volume slider in the app

Title: Remove phone menu buttons
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
The android menu buttons are displayed and it doesn't look great. There is already the app menu so having also the android menu doesn't fit in.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Hide it. Have it in Fullscreen mode.

Title: increase album tracks from 100
Body:
There's been a few instances where an album I'm trying to listen to is cut off at 100 tracks where the album itself is beyond 100, I know it's few and far between but it is annoying.

Would you please kindly increase the number of tracks that can be played via an album from 100 to let's say 150.

This is not a high priority issue and I'm more concerned with bug fixes and crashes.

Title: Liked content
Body: When we like a song or an album, the content liked isn't saved properly. Almost all the song liked are not saved in the liked songs playlist, just click on the like button is a bit difficult, I have to click numerus time on it to make it work but even after that the song isn't saved. For the album it's a bit different but the problem is the same, I 'don't have to click multiple time on the button but sometimes it's not saved.

Title: Small Formatting Changes :-)
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
This isn't a problem, just a few formatting changes that I think would make the UI more cohesive.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Please refer to the pictures in the additional context section (I've numbered the changes)

1. The search bar text is not aligned vertically. It should be moved down so it is vertically centered with the box.
2. When you scroll down a playlist, album, or artist page, the title that stays at the top is not the same font as the rest of the app. Making it the same font (is it Lexend? I'm not sure) would improve the overall look.
3. This is subjective, but I think there should be less space between the song and artist in the now playing bar. Right now (to me) it looks not as nice as ViMusic or other alternatives because the title and artist are too close to the top and bottom of the now playing box.

N/A

Title: Some (most) downloaded songs do not play or stop right in the middle of playback.
Body:
**Describe the bug**
When playing some downloaded songs, the song never starts because it stays at 0:00 or stops in the middle of playing with the error "Time out, check internet connection". In some songs this is fixed by choosing another song, but for most songs it does not.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Download some songs
2. Play any song
3. Wait some time and maybe the song would never start, or it will stop playing exactly in the middle of the playback.

**Expected behavior**
It's a downloaded song, it should be play even without internet connection.

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

**Log (Error Details)**
Paste the log from the crashing screen, if it is not a crash bug, skip it.

Title: Cast & local media add
Body:
Support for casting your audio through Chromecast would be amazing! I currently have SimpMusic as my main music player, yet when I want to cast music on my "Smart" speakers, I have to use another app, which can't locate my downloaded SimpMusic library. Also as other users asked, the ability to add our local media into the player, hopefully in a file-tree way, as in (Artist Folder> Album folder> songs.mp3) to keep things organize. 
One last question, where are the files downloaded saved? I'm in Android 14. 
Thank you! And again this player is amazing.

Title: Save Downloads to Local Storage instead of Cache
Body:
The first time I used the app, I was so excited because the download feature works and it's also fast. The problem is I searched for the downloaded files and I noticed they're only saved in the app's cache and not accessible to other apps like Poweramp. I also use a temporary files cleaner app and it clears the Download cache of SimpMusic.

I think it would be better if the downloaded music files and playlists are saved to the Local Storage to solve this problem. 

Would be best if the Music files are embedded with Album Art, Metadata and Lyrics. Also saving the playlists as .m3u files, and the music files as .m4a.

Title: Loading inefficiency issue
Body:
### Describe the Bug/Crash

I was trying to profile the scanning process and noticed some less than ideal behaviour for larger libraries.

Firstly setText() seems to be using about 30% of cpu time on the main thread just updating the "Loading your music library... (xxx/xxx)" text.  Secondly a rescan launches thousands of threads.

I tried using a threadpool executorservice in exoplayer/upstream/Loader, this about halved the threads created but it didn't affect wall-clock-time and android studio is so miserable and buggy it's hard to know if it improved cpu usage (I think it did though).

I don't know enough about android event loops to work out how to change exoplayer/MetadataRetriever to use a thread pool.  That bit of code seems terribly complicated and designed for a different use case such as async retrieval of metadata from a small number of remote sources and not thousands of local files.

So I didn't have much luck improving performance, although I might suggest removing the numbers from "Loading your music library ... (xxx/xxx)" since all they do is jump around annoyingly and don't tell the user anything useful.

I did notice the following comes up a few times during a rescan in the unmodified version.  I couldn't identify what causes it.

```
Handler (android.os.Handler) {5a20ac4} sending message to a Handler on a dead thread
java.lang.IllegalStateException: Handler (android.os.Handler) {5a20ac4} sending message to a Handler on a dead thread
	at android.os.MessageQueue.enqueueMessage(MessageQueue.java:567)
	at android.os.Handler.enqueueMessage(Handler.java:754)
	at android.os.Handler.sendMessageAtTime(Handler.java:703)
	at android.os.Handler.sendMessageDelayed(Handler.java:673)
	at android.os.Handler.post(Handler.java:403)
	at androidx.media3.exoplayer.source.ProgressiveMediaPeriod.onUpstreamFormatChanged(ProgressiveMediaPeriod.java:721)
	at androidx.media3.exoplayer.source.SampleQueue.format(SampleQueue.java:594)
	at androidx.media3.extractor.mp3.Mp3Extractor.readInternal(Mp3Extractor.java:277)
	at androidx.media3.extractor.mp3.Mp3Extractor.read(Mp3Extractor.java:242)
	at androidx.media3.exoplayer.source.BundledExtractorsAdapter.read(BundledExtractorsAdapter.java:133)
	at androidx.media3.exoplayer.source.ProgressiveMediaPeriod$ExtractingLoadable.load(ProgressiveMediaPeriod.java:1067)
	at androidx.media3.exoplayer.upstream.Loader$LoadTask.run(Loader.java:433)
	at java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1167)
	at java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:641)
	at java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:919)
```



### Describe the intended behavior

Better cpu usage and running time.

### What android version do you use?

Android 13

### What device model do you use?

pixel 4a with lineagos 20, but also a motog8+ with android 10.


### Bug report

as above.

Body:

Allow items on lists, such as artists, albums, genres, etc. to be pinned on top. Pinned items will stay on top and will not move.

Body: It would be nice if the project's code were moved to GitLab or Codeberg. GitHub is not an open source platform, and being owned by Microsoft makes it more of a danger to open source projects, rather than a help.

Title: Make common action row for menus
Body: Admittedly, now the bottom sheet menus are also growing a bit too much for my tastes. I'm going to see if moving some common actions to a row at the top might help alleviate this a little.

Title: Improve foldable support
Body:
I finally got the chance to see some foldables in person, so now I actually know what I want to do in order to improve Auxio's support with foldables (Or at least landscape foldables, portrait foldables seem okay right now.)

1. Change the playback bar to have song information centered on one half, and playback controls on the other half. This should probably look really good.
2. Make sure the dual-panel playback view is properly split at the folding point in the playback screen.
3. Make sure that list items are correctly split at the folding point as well, which basically means I fix the column size to two items on foldables as well.

Title: Animate skip transitions
Body: Spun off from #94. Effectively, animate the transitions between covers in a way that does not use ViewPager and works with the gestures added. This is somewhat difficult and not high priority.

Title: Improve tablet UI
Body:
### Description

Now that Android itself (And a lot of tablet apps) are adapted to a Tablet UI, I feel that Auxio could also implement some extra adaptations for tablets (Also I guess foldables, but those usually go hand in hand, so I'll just refer to both as tablets). Right now, Auxio's UI is pretty well adapted to tablets and I wouldn't say that, if these don't get implemented UX would be dead, but there are something things that would nicely fit on tablets. 

### Problem solved

With that being said, here are my main ideas that could improve the UI for tablet.
First, we have the main screen, which on a tablet looks something like this:
![Screenshot_20231123-111846_Trebuchet](https://github.com/OxygenCobalt/Auxio/assets/79670265/12cbf92d-99a3-4eee-976e-537ab8f7d38a)
It's not bad, however, 2 things really jump to me, the first is the shuffle button that's too big, but that's most likely a bug so that probably needs it's own issue. The main one is the top buttons, they looks too big and separated, but also (this may be nitpicking) the top bar is off-center, this works on a phone, but I feel that, in a tablet, it would be better centered and a bit mapper and compact (pictures of how other apps usually handle this in the next section).

Next, it hast to be the settings, which looks something like this:
![Screenshot_20231123-112238_Trebuchet](https://github.com/OxygenCobalt/Auxio/assets/79670265/3c4d4f07-8a1a-40a2-b497-b064fb7592ab)
Which again, looks fine, however (and this probably is pretty hard to implement, and if not implented it would be fine) is that the settings be a dual-pane layout, the pop-ups (like the one for excluding folders) would still remain pop-ups, but the settings that have 2 screens would be really nice as a dual-pane UI.

Lastly, we got the Playlist, which looks like this:
![Screenshot_20231123-112458_Auxio](https://github.com/OxygenCobalt/Auxio/assets/79670265/d835a982-d3c1-48d7-962b-7385153b5a99)
Which I'd say it is already adapted, but, continuing on dual-pane layouts, I feel that this UI could also benefit from a dual pane layout (screenshots on how another music player handles this).

### Other implementations

I got 2 examples which (doesn't have to and hopefully it doesn't look like them) can be used to get an idea on how the top bar may look. We first have newpipe:
![Screenshot_20231123-111954_Trebuchet](https://github.com/OxygenCobalt/Auxio/assets/79670265/defb4509-0b4d-4fec-8711-e3ce7b16fcb5)
Which is centered and compact, however, it only has icons with no labels and, realistically, they are unreachable on landscape view when holding up the tablet, but can give an idea on how it may look. The second one is Infinity for Reddit:
![Screenshot_20231123-112121_Trebuchet](https://github.com/OxygenCobalt/Auxio/assets/79670265/e4010b90-a206-4d6e-98de-b4172bed2722)
In this case, it is only labels, but it is a bit more separated, and I feel that, with icons, the UI could be ideal.

Next, with the settings, we don't even have to go that far in the OS, we can go to the system settings:
![Screenshot_20231123-120442_Configuración](https://github.com/OxygenCobalt/Auxio/assets/79670265/251818ff-6b34-4925-8375-0dc9356c3cab)
A dual-pane layout that let's you quickly change anything in settings and it not looking stretched out. The next example could be Tachiyomi:
![Screenshot_20231123-120638_Trebuchet](https://github.com/OxygenCobalt/Auxio/assets/79670265/81132c11-75fd-447b-8364-472818c5f575)
Which, I'd say it looks a bit weird, but the idea is the dual-pane settings layout. Also, if we want to stick with music players, we got YouTube Music:
![Screenshot_20231123-120848_Trebuchet](https://github.com/OxygenCobalt/Auxio/assets/79670265/51f9e2dd-a68c-46a8-ae27-5ac5413b3714)

And with the Playlist, again, I feel that they are fine if they are really hard to implement (or if you disagree that a dual-pane layout doesn't work here), the best example here is YouTube Music:
![Screenshot_20231123-112543_YouTube Music](https://github.com/OxygenCobalt/Auxio/assets/79670265/3bf1b9ef-c001-492b-98a3-8360d4105b15)
You get the Playlist controls and name at the left, with the content to the right. 

### Benefit

Having these Tablet UI Adaptations (or extras I guess) would benefit with a better UX when using a tablet/foldable. It also would mean a better/more consistent UI when comparing to other apps (even with Android itself, albeit the latest versions).

Title: Allow splitting featured artists with multi-value separators
Body:
### Description

When multi-value separators are enabled and found, and the artist field also contains featured artist(s), the field may get separated oddly.

### Problem solved

Examples:

1. With one artist and one featured artist:
`Artist 1 feat. Featured 1` -> `Artist 1 feat. Featured 1`
  
2. With two artists and one featured artist:
`Artist 1, Artist 2 feat. Featured 1` -> `Artist 1`, `Artist 2 feat. Featured 1`
  
3. With two artists and two featured artist:
`Artist 1, Artist 2 feat. Featured 1, Featured 2` -> `Artist 1`, `Artist 2 feat. Featured 1`, `Featured 2`

Example 1 is working as intended, but when adding more artists like with examples 2 and 3 the field gets oddly split.

4. With one artist, and another song with one artist and one featured artist:
`Artist 1`, `Artist 1 feat. Featured 1` ->  `Artist 1`, `Artist 1 feat. Featured 1`

This is fair, but if there are multiple ones like these, the artists tab will be cluttered with many entries for the same artist with different featured artists.



### Other implementations

I haven't used any other music players with multi-value separators.
If possible just adding " feat. " as a separator could do the job. I'm not familiar with Kotlin, but from a quick search splitting with a string seems possible.

### Benefit

Artists tab would be less cluttered with multiple entries for the same artists from songs with featured artists.

Featured artists would also be better accessed from artists tab, as every one of them would have their own entry.

Title: First-class release type support
Body:
Upgrade release types from simple sugar on an album object to a type of music item that can be actively filtered against. This would involve:

1. The "Albums" view being retooled into a "Releases" tab that then contains a row of chips that allow one to filter by Singles, EPs, Compilations, etc. See below for what I mean by chips:
![image](https://github.com/OxygenCobalt/Auxio/assets/65370175/a1c86a06-245c-4f28-b8f4-940b8c07d367)

2. The Search view having a "Releases" filtering option that then opens to have sub-options related to Albums, EPs, Singles, etc. This depends on #329 and would have a similar design as below:

https://github.com/OxygenCobalt/Auxio/assets/65370175/2a94618e-f857-4eeb-ba7d-b4fd34da0960

The primary issue here is that I do not want to make this the new default. A lot of users with simple libraries expect a tab called "Albums", and the ability to filter for "Albums". Thus, there needs to be some kind of dynamic aspect to this feature where the "Releases" tab activates itself only if release types are detected in the library. This is what makes it complex and low-priority.

Title: Display multi-artist credits as the artist credit
Body:
### Describe the Bug/Crash

1. You need an album with multi-tagged **albumartists** (note plural) tags, as well as a singular albumartist tag. This is a developing standard for how music tagged from MusicBrainz data should be tagged, and is e.g. how [beets](https://github.com/beetbox/beets/) does it in the latest development versions. All my music is tagged like this.
2. Open an album with multiple albumartists credits.
3. Note that the album is credited to "Artist 1, Artist 2", i.e. the several album artists with ", " used as a join phrase.

### Describe the intended behavior

Better behavior would be to use the multi-tags for appropriate credit on the artist pages (which Auxio already does correctly - thanks!), but use the singular albumartist tag (or albumartist_credit, if available) as the credit for the album. The usual expectation is that this value will have a nice version of the multi-tags meant for display.

As an example, `mutagen-inspect` shows the following (partial) information from one of the files in the album in the screenshot below:

```
ALBUMARTIST=Danger Mouse & Black Thought
ALBUMARTISTSORT=Danger Mouse & Black Thought
ALBUMARTISTS=Danger Mouse
ALBUMARTISTS=Black Thought
```

Rather than substituting a constant join phrase between the two albumartists, I'd like to see the tagged album artist used.

If you don't consider this behavior clearly correct, or you're worried about users with badly tagged collections, maybe this could be made optional similar to the "hide collaborators" feature. Personally I wouldn't worry about this, because users using the brand new `albumartists` credit with multi-tags are pretty unlikely to have badly tagged music without something reasonable in `albumartist`.

### What android version do you use?

Android 12

### What device model do you use?

Samsung Galaxy S10e

### Bug report

Screenshot of the issue:

![Screenshot_20230922-141403_Auxio](https://github.com/OxygenCobalt/Auxio/assets/3870410/de2750ae-72ee-4613-a2db-3e7667564db5)

Title: Playback bottom sheet can get stuck when paused
Body:
If you pause playback and then try to swipe down (not fling down) the queue panel, the playback panel will get stuck. This is hard to trigger normally, but probably is annoying when it does.

I already know the cause, so here is the absolutely insane series of events that happens to trigger this bug.

This is because of an insane workaround I do where I not only have to disable the playback panel when you open the queue, I also have to check for this ***every drawn frame*** since the normal callbacks aren't reliable enough for unknown reasons. Turns out even that is not enough, as android will skip drawing frames at some points when (rightfully) nothing has changed. Normally this doesn't matter *when playing*, as the SeekBar will move and force a redraw, unless you're paused. Then if you swipe down the queue panel slowly, you can actually skip a draw and make the playback panel remain disabled.

Once solution then is to create a coroutine that does this update every 50ms or so, which is such an absurd brute-force solution that will probably tank performance. Alternatively, I see if the MDC team actually fixed the busted UI component I use, which I highly doubt since they are extremely busy. Or perhaps I should give up and migrate Auxio to Jetpack Compose since I heard bottom sheet components are easier to write there. Who knows.

Title: [Bug]: Minor Bug? Resuming Issue on Google TV
Body:
### Problem description

When I binge-watch multiple episodes of a TV series and have to pause the player due to other commitments, it restarts from the first episode of that viewing session upon resuming. This issue **only** occurs if the Google TV has entered screen saver mode in the meantime; otherwise, it continues from where it was paused. In essence, if you leave the player paused for too long, allowing the Google TV to activate its screen saver, this problem arises. I'm unsure whether this is a bug with Nova or a limitation of Google TV.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Watching multiple episodes back-to-back, pausing the player, and then resuming the video after a half hour break.

### Expected behavior

The player always resumes playback from where it was last stopped."

6.2.50

### Additional system information

Title: [FEAT]: Videos over Nova's SMBJ start up nearly 3x slower than they do on Kodi's SMB
Body:
### Problem description

Videos over SMBJ start up nearly 3x slower than they do on Kodi

I notice one major difference when I compare the two players with a somewhat scientific test. 

**Nova's SMBJ starts videos in about 5.75-6 seconds.** 

**Kodi's SMB starts videos in about 2 seconds.**

I tried controlling for all variables in the steps below. I was able to reproduce the same result every single time. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1) Get fresh install of latest Nova (6.2.51) and Kodi (21 beta 2)
2) Play the same videos (in this case 1080p web-dl) from the same share, on the same network, using the same device. 
3) Get a stopwatch and time how long it takes for videos to open over SMBJ on Nova and SMB on Kodi. 

No matter how many times I kept testing and re-testing this, Kodi's SMB would start up faster than Nova's SMBJ. I went through about 20 videos of about the same bitrate and resolution so far and I would always get Kodi starting videos about 3x faster. 

Video would begin playing on Kodi's SMB in about 2 seconds.
Video would begin playing on Nova's SMBJ in about 5-6 seconds. 

Can anybody else reproduce this behavior? @courville this happens on both my TV devices, the Onn 4K 2021 and the Fire TV 4K stick.

### Expected behavior

You'd expect Nova's SMBJ to start videos as fast as Kodi does. Is there any way to speed up the video startup over SMBJ? 

This is not a huge issue but I do wonder why Kodi has closer to instant playback while Nova takes little while to start playing video under the exact same conditions. 

### Your phone/tablet/androidTV model

Onn 4K box (2021)

### Operating system version

Android 10

6.2.51

Title: Recherche "Réseau et fichiers" / Disque dur externe
Body:
### Problem description

Lorsque l'on a beaucoup de dossiers sur plus d'une page classés par ordre alphabétique ou autre, il y a un problème très gênant sous Nova lorsque l'on consulte ces dossiers et que l'on ouvre une fiche film. 

Si l'on consulte un dossier qui se nomme par exemple "Alpha", "Beta", "Chili", ...., "Posog" , "Zêta" si l'on fait un retour AR après consultation de ce dossier, on reste bien dans l'ordre logique de consultation! (Si l'on a consulté le dossier "Posog", après un retour AR, on revient bien au niveau du dossier "Posog"!)

Par contre, si l'on consulte ce même dossier "Posog" et que l'on ouvre une fiche film qui s'y trouve,
lors du retour AR, Nova se comporte comme s'il procédait à une réindexation de dossiers (il y a bien une petite "moulinette dans le centre de l'écran de quelques secondes...) et l'on se retrouve systématiquement au niveau du 1er dossier alphabétique (Par exemple: "Alpha"),

Ce qui oblige quand on a beaucoup de dossiers à parcourir à nouveau de page en page jusqu’à atteindre le dernier dossier consulté (Exemple:"Posog") pour poursuivre la consultation.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Consulter un dossier par exemple "Saga Le seigneurs des anneaux", consulter une fiche film, faire retour AR et au lieu de rester au niveau "Saga Le seigneur des anneaux", on va revenir systématiquement sue le 1er dossier "Saga 300" par exemple...

### Expected behavior

On attend le même comportement lorsque l'on consulte un dossier que lorsque l'on consulte un dossier+fiche film

Title: [New Feature] Add audio compression
Body:
**Describe the new feature you'd like**
I'm trying to use this awesome software to monitor a remote audio feed in as close to realtime as possible. It's doing good but would like to know if there's a compression available that doesn't introduce crazy delay or kill the CPU but still reduce some of the fat WAV bits going down the pipe.

**Describe the reason why you want to add the feature**
I need this feature because I stream audio across the internet to monitor inputs and such. That's not in the same local network, across different ISPs.

**Describe how to implement the feature if you can**
ffmpeg seems to encode and decode mp3 and m4a at 30x or so, maybe that's enough performance to not introduce a huge delay. I also don't mean you should go the mp3/m4a route, it's just an example.

### Steps to reproduce

1.create a local playlist
2. add more than 200 songs into this playlist
3. download all these songs
4. change the storage path to any folder
5. choose "yes" for Transfer existing library
6. add any other songs to this playlist
7. download newly added songs 

### Expected behavior

1. after step 6 we should see the newly added songs in the playlist in a short time
2. after step 7 songs should start to download

### Actual behavior

1. after step 6 it will take more than 5 mins, then the newly added songs can be found in the playlist

Title: [Feature Request]  album artist view
Body:
### Description

I have a relatively large music collection of popular music with multiple artists featured on a song. An example might be "Galleons feat. Garret Rapp" or "Killer Mike feat. Curren$y". I also listen almost exclusively to albums. With the current artist list, it is imppssible for me to "drill down" from artist > album, which seems to me to be most natural.

### Solution

I can see one of two solutions being useful: either prefer sorting on thr "album artist" tag, if it exists for any file. This should be backwards compatible with the current view, so long as one does not have that tag populated. Alternatively, a new setting could allow users to toggle this sprting on or off.

### Alternatives

A survey of FOSS Android media players seems to indicate that Symphony isnt alone in this behavior. Vinyl, PhonographPro, Auxio, VibeYou, and one or two others show off this same behavior. I don't know how best to alleviate this wothout retagging my library and I VRRY MUCH dont want to do that

### Additional Context

N/A

-   [x] I have read the [Contribution Guidelines](https://github.com/zyrouge/symphony/wiki/Contributions-Guidelines#issues).

Title: [Bug] Symphony shows nonexistent files.
Body:
### Description

Symphony shows nonexistent files. If you delete a particular file, Symphony still shows it. Normally, if Symphony lists a nonexistent file and tries to play it but can't, it skips to the next file. But if it can't find any other playable file, it simply crashes. Even clicking on "Clear song cache" in Settings does not seem to remove the invalid listings, and clicking "Re-scan" doesn't change anything either.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Add a file to a folder Symphony is tracking.
2. Make sure Symphony recognises the file (Open Symphony, click on "Re-scan", etc.)
3. Delete the file.
4. Open Symphony.
5. Locate file (problem: file should ideally not _be_ there)
6. Try to play the file.
7. Symphony skips over file.

Alternatively, to get the Red Screen of Death:

1. Make sure Symphony is only tracking a specific folder with a file or two in it (Whitelist, "Songs filter pattern", etc.).
2. Delete all files in the folder Symphony is tracking.
3. Open Symphony and see files in listing. (Problem)
4. Try to play any file.
5. Red Screen of Death.

### Expected Behavior

- If a file was previously listed in Symphony but then deleted, Symphony should detect that it no longer exists (if someone tries to play it) and remove it from its listing.
- It should also _completely_ clear its internal file listing on clicking "Clear song cache" ( or "Re-scan") and regenerate its listing from scratch.
- A quick fix could be to add a button to manually remove a file from Symphony's listing.

Title: [Bug] Albums with the same name but different Album Artists are erroneously grouped together
Body:
### Description

If you have two artists in your library that both released an album with the title of "Example" they get grouped together despite being completely separate albums. For instance, I have the album "Antagonist" by Apashe and "Antagonist" by Neurotech. These get mixed together as if they were one. They are not.

### Steps to Reproduce

Name two albums from different Album Artists the same. Look in album view in library. They will be grouped together.

### Expected Behavior

Albums should respect Album Artist and possibly Year to determine whether or not it is an album.

### Description
The current shuffle feature in the app only applies to the current queue, but some users, including myself, would like to have the option to apply shuffle globally. This request is to propose the implementation of a global shuffle feature to enhance the user experience.

Title: [Bug] Some songs are not recognised / cannot be played
Body:
### Description

Some songs in my collection (seemingly random) cannot be played and the metadata isn't recognised either. I have uploaded one of them to gofile for you. [One of the songs in question](https://gofile.io/d/evIjYZ)

The song can be played with `mpv android` and on my pc as well, so the file is 100% not corrupt.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Import the song
2. Go to songs
3. Try to play it (album art + metadata aren't present either)
4. See error

### Expected Behavior
Song plays and metadata is present

Title: [Bug] Mini-player progress bar high GPU usage
Body:
### Description

Hello again! The truncated title option works well, props to you, and your 10X dev response! But, sadly, another element of it keeps the GPU running at full speed. 

My best guess would be the progress bar, which is pretty smooth, lets me think it updates at an uncapped rate. Luckily, the full screen player doesn't have this issue, as the progress bar does indeed move by small increments gradually without pushing render frames at a fast pace. You could somewhat copy that fullscreen bar code for simplicity's sake, maybe?

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Play music
2. Have the mini-player marquee on display
3. Battery drain

### Expected Behavior

A less gpu-intensive marquee, which is displayed a lot of the time navigating the app, would prove battery saving.

### Screenshots

![Screenshot_2024-01-05-21-54-15-08_c328a126cb0fb561391a2f6f61559b68](https://github.com/zyrouge/symphony/assets/95828007/69f6cceb-8200-4a66-8c62-1e79e580664e)

Title: [Feature Request] Identify Opus audio as separate from Ogg in audio information
Body:

Currently, Opus audio shows up as OGG in the player screen. I'm not sure if it's merely because the codec is missing in a list somewhere, or because the app shows the container instead of the actual codec.

If Opus merely needs to be added to a list, then that's fine. But if the app chooses to show the container instead of the codec, then my request is for that to be changed.

Title: [Feature Request] Better genre tag value parser
[Feature Request] Keep music playing when closing Symphony
Body:
### Description

Currently Symphony only plays music while the app is kept in the recent apps menu. If one removes Symphony from recent apps by swiping the music stops and the media player notification disappears. It's inconvenient because one has to make sure not to close Symphony by accident. 

### Solution
Music and the media player notification should stay active after closing Symphony unless the queue/current song is dismissed. Maybe this could be achieved with a permanent notification for music control. 

### Additional Context

On ChromeOS the lack of a permanent notification appears to prevent the native media player panel from picking up the current song. But that's just my observation when comparing with VLC for Android which does have a permanent notification.

Title: [Feature Request] Dedicated pages for recommended albums/artists/tracks
Body:
### Description

On the home screen, the app recommends various albums, artists or tracks from the library to the user. This is a very useful feature, but could be expanded. Recommended albums, artists and tracks should each have their own page, as one would expect to explore this in more detail.

### Solution

When the application is started, the user will see the sections with 6 albums/artists/tracks displayed. There, it is possible to go to each section to open a new page with the respective recommendations, with a varying list of them. On this page there is also a button to shuffle the current list of recommended music.

### Alternatives

Alternatively, it would be a nice idea to offer more elements than only six. I could imagine a setting where it is possible to set the number of recommendations.

### Additional Context

None.

```
<string name="help_data_backup_heading">Is there a way to backup my data?</string>
<string name="help_data_backup_description">Yes. You can use the Privacy Friendly Backup app to backup and restore this application's data. In this way, the data can also be transferred to another device.</string>
```

Title: Missing info about how-to back up this app's data
Body: Missing info about how-to back up this app's data, which has been added in PR https://github.com/SecUSo/privacy-friendly-qr-scanner/pull/199 to make it clear to users how they can do this. This is very necessary when a user is switching physical phones.

Title: Feature request - Encrypted text into QR Codes.
Body:
First of all, I would like to know if this project is still alive since it is the best QR Code scanner out there. 

My request...
I use a password manager, as I hope all of you. I have a very strong master password, U2F and so on, but I am getting older so I'm afraid of forgetting the password eventually. Print the master password in a paper is an option, but I am looking for a better way to store it so I realized I could encrypt the password using symmetric GnuPG encryption and generate a QR Code with your application.

So basically:
1- I use gnupg on Linux (gnupg -c -a mypassword.txt)  or OpenKeyChain on Android. 
2- I use your QR Scanner to create a QR Code with the output.
3- I print it and store in a fireproof bag. 
To decrypt it:
1- I read the QR with your app
2- Copy the text to the clipboard
3- I decrypt it using OpenKeyChain or (gnupg -d )

Advantages:
- The password is encrypted with an easy to remember pin or short password, it is better than print it in plain text. 
- If someone find the password he/she must know the pin AND has to have ALSO the knowledge to decrypted it.

Off course this can be use to store safely any kind of information not only passwords.  

I thought maybe you can include all these steps in the generation/reading of a QR Code, for example, in the QR Generation, it could have a Encrypted QR or GPG whatever option. Then when the app reads a QR Code that has the "BEGIN/END PGP MESSAGE"  could request a password and decrypt the QR content.  

You could use OpenKeyChain but it is no longer actively maintained, I found some other libraries you could use:
https://github.com/pgpainless/pgpainless
https://github.com/ProtonMail/gopenpgp

Hope this is good idea for you and that the project is still alive. Thank you very much for the app and your time.

Title: Use the Public Suffix List
Body:
It currently uses a regex: https://github.com/SecUSo/privacy-friendly-qr-scanner/blob/cbbf609c9a4bedf0f58e0ceb5616d002cbb59bfa/app/src/main/java/com/secuso/privacyfriendlycodescanner/qrscanner/ui/resultfragments/URLResultFragment.java#L60
Because of that it highlights wrongly a lot of domains not in the regex. Using the public suffix list is probably the best way to handle those cases: https://publicsuffix.org/

Title: Add feature to label stored QR-Codes
Body:
It would be helpful if you can add a label to the stored (i.e. historical) codes. The app shows only the scan date and time, so it can be difficult to identify your scans. 
Typical use-case is when you scan a ticket. You buy a ticket, you scan the QR-Code and a few days later you go to the event, show, etc. Then it can be difficult to find the QR-Ticket from the history without having any label.

Title: Do not prepend http:// to URLs that already have a protocol
Body:
If the URL already contains a protocol (e.g. `fdroidrepos://example.com`), do not prepend `http://` when sharing it.

See https://gitlab.com/fdroid/fdroidclient/-/merge_requests/1133 for the breakage that this can cause.

Related to #104.

My suggestion: If a string starts with `^[a-zA-Z0-9]+://`, do not modify it in any way when opening.

Title: Red line in scanning view
Body:
Hello, first of all, thank you for this useful app!


## Situation

In the main view, i.e. when scanning a QR code, the QR scanner shows a pulsating red line on the screen. The help section in the app leaves it unexplained, but I suppose it is a skeuomorphic representation of a red laser used in classic barcode scanners (please correct me if I'm wrong).


## Feature request

- I would mainly wish for this red line to be optional, i.e. to have an option in the settings to disable it, just like the beep can be disabled.
- Secondarily, perhaps it's worth explaining in the help section.

Title: Cannot return to UI when in Park.
