Title: Ask what sort of boats can be rented
Body:
<!-- 
Please read the guidelines for new quest suggestions before posting one: 
https://github.com/streetcomplete/StreetComplete/blob/master/QUEST_GUIDELINES.md

Also please search existing issues (both open and closed) to see if such quest was already suggested before.
-->

### General
Affected tag(s) to be modified/added: [`*_rental=*`](https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Tag:amenity%3Dboat_rental)
Question asked: **What sort of boats can be rented here?**

<!-- If the answer is not obvious, please add a short explanation below each item. -->
### Checklist
Checklist for quest suggestions (see [guidelines](https://github.com/streetcomplete/StreetComplete/blob/master/QUEST_GUIDELINES.md)):
- [x] 🚧 To be added tag is established and has a useful purpose
Canoe rental is very different to motorboat rental, it is important to be able to distinguish these
- [x] 🤔 Any answer the user can give must have an equivalent tagging (Quest should not reappear to other users when solved by one)
- [x] 🐿️ Easily answerable by any pedestrian from the outside but a survey is necessary
- [x] 💤 Not an overwhelming percentage of quests have the same answer (No spam)
- [x] 🕓 Applies to a reasonable number of map data (Worth the effort)
Only just over 5000 `amenity=boat_rental` but many (>~75%?) do not have a `_rental` tag.

<!--
All of the above should be fulfilled. 
If you are not sure about how one condition applies to your suggestion or you have (very) strong reasons that a condition may not needed to be satisfied in your case, just note that down. Someone else may come up with some ideas.
-->

### Ideas for implementation

<!-- If you have any idea for how elements should be selected, add it here. -->
**Element selection:** 
`amenity = boat_rental` and `*_rental != *`

<!-- If you have any idea whether metadata per country is needed, add it here. If you know where to get this data from, mention it here too -->
**Metadata needed:** 
Don't think so. Maybe different boats in different countries?

<!-- If you have any idea on how the UI (the form appearing when the quest is asked) should look like.
You can use any way to propose a UI. Feel free to include a mockup. 
Do include all answer possibilities you consider for that quest. -->
**Proposed UI:**
Picture/icon multi-select for kayak, canoe, stand-up paddleboard, sailing boat, dinghy, motorboat and pedalboat.

Title: Add paid parking operator quest
Body:
### General
Affected tag(s) to be modified/added: [operator](https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:operator)

Question asked: **Who operates this parking lot?**

By collecting information about who parking fees are paid to, richer OSM parking experiences can be enabled, i.e. a government or company name can be matched with known entities and online payment options can more easily be presented, or research reports on private vs public parking can more easily be created. Travelers can then research parking ahead of time in a GPS app, and armchair mappers can look up operator websites for further details. (In dense urban environments it can be very important to know if a lot is related to the venue you're visiting, or if they're a brand name you trust, etc.)

By comparison, asking surveyors what type of parking a lot is and what surface it has is quite spammy: that information is almost always visible from decent satellite imagery. But only a surveyor will be able to tell if a parking lot is paid, and if so what its operator is.

### Checklist
Checklist for quest suggestions (see [guidelines](https://github.com/streetcomplete/StreetComplete/blob/master/QUEST_GUIDELINES.md)):
- [x] 🚧 To be added tag is established and has a useful purpose
- [x] 🤔 Any answer the user can give must have an equivalent tagging (Quest should not reappear to other users when solved by one)
- [x] 🐿️ Easily answerable by any pedestrian from the outside but a survey is necessary
- [x] 💤 Not an overwhelming percentage of quests have the same answer (No spam)
- [x] 🕓 Applies to a reasonable number of map data (Worth the effort)

### Ideas for implementation

By only asking for this information for parking lots we already know have a fee associated, we can limit spam.

**Element selection:** 

```
[out:json][timeout:25];
nwr["fee"]["fee"!="no"]["amenity"="parking"]["operator"!~".*"]({{bbox}});
out geom;
```

**Proposed UI:**

-----

🅿️ **Who operates this parking lot?**

`(operator name)                 `

[Can't Say...](#)

------

Title: iOS - Planning
Body: This ticket is something like the master ticket to coordinate development on an iOS port of StreetComplete. It replaces #1892 which also included a lot of discussion and research / observation work. ### TLDR: Have a look at the [Project Board](https://github.com/orgs/streetcomplete/projects/1) for a list of tasks. Contributions are welcome! # Approach The code base of this app is written in 100% Kotlin, a modern programming language that is quite similar to Swift. Kotlin code can be transpiled to JavaScript and also to machine code, just like Swift. Furthermore, many dependencies of this app by now are pure Kotlin libraries. An iOS version of this app hence can be done with [Kotlin Multiplatform](https://www.jetbrains.com/kotlin-multiplatform/). The UI code can be shared using [Compose Multiplatform](https://www.jetbrains.com/lp/compose-multiplatform/), which is currently in alpha for iOS (= usable, but API may change). It is a fork of [Jetpack Compose](https://developer.android.com/jetpack/compose) with largely the same API. Jetpack Compose is a reactive UI framework in which developers define the UI completely in code (similar idea as SwiftUI). We'll have to re-create the entire UI (incrementally) in Compose, though. This will keep the amount of code that needs to be platform dependent (Android / iOS) to a minimum. This approach has the big advantage over others in that further maintenance of this project will not increase significantly, as there will continue to be just one code base. # Steps in a nutshell - Separate all platform specific code from application logic. Replace Android/Java dependencies for application logic with Kotlin multiplatform dependencies - Incrementally migrate the UI code to Compose Multiplatform: 1. Separate data access and UI state code from pure UI code (use view models: #5070 ) 2. Migrate the UI code that currently uses Android XML layouts to Android [Jetpack Compose](https://developer.android.com/jetpack/compose). This can be done incrementally when done bottom-up (see [migration strategy guide](https://developer.android.com/jetpack/compose/migrate/strategy)) 3. Then, migrate from Jetpack Compose to Compose Multiplatform. This is a small step compared with the first one. With a little luck, Compose Multiplatform is out of alpha by then and thus the API more or less stable while the ecosystem around it is more mature. # Current State _This section will be updated continuously._ The steps necessary are described in detail in a Kanban-style [Project Board](https://github.com/orgs/streetcomplete/projects/1). Unless stated otherwise, they are explicitly up for the taking by people who read this and (I hope 😊) want to contribute to the development towards an iOS port. If you would like to implement one of the tasks described, comment in that ticket. The project board currently only contains tickets for stuff involving application logic, i.e. the point 1 from the "Steps in a nutshell" description from above. The other big, iterative, task that has no (individual) ticket yet is the UI part. This is because the migration to Jetpack Compose is a somewhat iterative undertaking for which I think it is better to have just one master ticket - this one (and then, many PRs). See the next comment for a more precise description. We very roughly estimated some time ago that developing a full port to iOS would take one man-year of work. So, the scope of this remains very large. We will only realistically achieve it together with lots of contributions from the community. Luckily, this is exactly what has been happening in the last years. So, let's shift gears! ## How you can help - You could take over a task from the project board! Or, you could familiarize yourself with the mentioned technologies / frameworks to make meaningful contributions later. In particular: Jetpack Compose / [Compose Multiplatform](https://www.jetbrains.com/lp/compose-multiplatform/). This is very new to me too, so I have to find my feet here, too - You can sponsor development! See the title "Sponsor this project" in the sidebar on the main page. More money allows me to devote more time to development on this app in general. If you know any eligible sources for larger scale funding, talk to me! - You can help with maintenance and issue triage in general. Time saved on that is time I can put into furthering development on this!

Title: Use multiplatform mocking library for tests
Body:
For mocking unit tests, we are currently using the Java library `org.mockito:mockito`.

In order that the tests for platform-independent application logic can be executed on any supported platform, mockito needs to be replaced as a test dependency.

### Mocking library requirements

- multiplatform support for at least Java/Android and iOS

- stable, well-tested and ideally (semi-)official / well-maintained

- support all features of Mockito we are using now

It is possible that when migrating, a bit more effort is necessary because as far as I know, Mockito uses Java reflection to create the mocks, which is a Java specific language feature. In other words, it may not be possible to mock non-interfaces.

Three Kotlin Multiplatform libraries for mocking are known to me, with a few comments from a short look at them, ordered by popularity on github:

#### [Mockative](https://github.com/mockative/mockative)

- seems well maintained
- good documentation
- has a guide how to migrate from Mockito to Mockative and apparently also a similar API to Mockito

#### [MocKMP](https://github.com/kosi-libs/MocKMP)

- seems well maintained
- good documentation
- backed by a company (Deezer)

#### [MockingBird](https://github.com/careem/mockingbird)

- seems well maintained
- from a quick glance, API seems to be a bit inconvenient
- oldest library, i.e. started in 2021, the others in 2022

Title: Implement `Preferences` interface on iOS
Body:
Implement the [`Preferences.kt`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Astreetcomplete%2FStreetComplete+Preferences.kt&type=code) interface for iOS with [`NSUserDefaults`](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/foundation/nsuserdefaults).

Alternatively, use the Kotlin multiplatform library [`com.russhwolf:multiplatform-settings`](https://github.com/russhwolf/multiplatform-settings) to implement the interface for all platforms or even replace the setup with this library.

See also https://github.com/streetcomplete/StreetComplete/pull/5357#issuecomment-1834648873 : in my opinion, it is best to use that library instead of a home-rolled implementation. The library is also featured in the [Compose Multiplatform Wizard](https://terrakok.github.io/Compose-Multiplatform-Wizard/). In case the mentioned multiplatform library is not used, this ticket is blocked by #5412.

Title: Implement `Logger` interface on iOS
Body:
Requires #5412.

Implement the [`Logger.kt`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Astreetcomplete%2FStreetComplete+Logger.kt&type=code) interface for iOS, most probably using the [`Logger`](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/os/logger) structure.

See also  https://developer.apple.com/documentation/os/logging/generating_log_messages_from_your_code

Alternatively, the most popular Kotlin multiplatform mobile logging libraries are [Napier](https://github.com/AAkira/Napier) and [Kermit](https://github.com/touchlab/Kermit), but I find it not worth it, given it is such a small interface to implement.

Title: Implement `Database` interface on iOS
Body:
Requires #5412.

Implement the [`Database.kt`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Astreetcomplete%2FStreetComplete+Database.kt&type=code) interface for iOS using `libsqlite3`, analogous to android-specific implementation in [`AndroidDatabase`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Astreetcomplete%2FStreetComplete+AndroidDatabase.kt&type=code)

There is an additional caveat (that can be done in a separate PR, before or after):

The code and db version upgrade logic in [`StreetCompleteSQLiteOpenHelper`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Astreetcomplete%2FStreetComplete+StreetCompleteSQLiteOpenHelper.kt&type=code) should actually be shared code too, i.e. use the `Database` interface, too.

Title: Multiplatform Locale
Body:
Requires #5412.

Java `java.util.Locale` is currently used in various places, most of them in the UI. Features used are (non-exhaustive list):

- access language code, country code and script code of user's locale

- access the display names of languages, countries and scripts in the user's locale 

- to correctly upper-, lower- and titlecase words

- to correctly format decimals (e.g. in German, decimals are separated by a comma from the number, not a point)

- to set the app's locale and the map's locale to whatever has been set by the language selector

And also:

- get the localized names of months and weekdays (using `java.text.DateFormatSymbols`) (for monthday and weekday picker)

- format time correctly (using `java.text.DateFormat` / `java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter`) (for wherever a time is displayed)

This functionality must be replaced with something that works on multiplatform. 

### Migration

In some places, `Locale` is just used as a key in a map or something. These could be just replaced by a string, i.e. the [IETF BCP 47 language tag](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IETF_language_tag) (e.g. "de-DE") to remove any dependency to any sort of specific Locale implementation.

#### NSLocale

The iOS equivalent of `java.util.Locale` is [`NSLocale`](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/foundation/nslocale). On first glance, it has all the features that the Java version has. So, a simple `actual` / `expect` implementation may be possible. 

#### multiplatform-locale

There is one multiplatform library that already does the wrapping: https://github.com/vanniktech/multiplatform-locale

However, at the time of writing, it is very fresh, so there are no guarantees about stability, continuous maintenance of the library etc. Additionally, it may have many features that we don't need while lacking others that we may need.

#### Date and time format

We are using [`kotlinx-datetime`](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime) for anything date/time-related (as a replacement for Java 8 Date/Time classes). However, this library is missing any (locale-aware) formatting of these. Though it is planned to add support for formatting dates and times in the next major release 0.6.0 (see https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime/pull/251), this is still not the Java locale-aware date/time formatting.

The iOS equivalent of date formatting is [`NSDateFormatter`](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/foundation/nsdateformatter), so at the time of writing, a simple `actual` / `expect` implementation may be possible.

Title: Multiplatform config file parsing
Body:
In various places, the app loads and parses some simple YAML files for configuration:

- [ ] a dictionary of street name abbreviations with their expansions (`abbreviations.yml`)
- [x] a dictionary of version number to html fragments that are the changelog (`changelog.yml`)
      → #5439
- [x] a list of main contributors (`credits_main.yml`)
      → #5444
- [x] a list of side project contributors (`credits_projects.yml`)
      → #5444
- [x] a list of all code contributors on github (`credits_contributors.yml`)
      → #5444
- [x] a list of all art contributors (`credits_art.yml`)
      → #5444
- [x] a dictionary of languages -> translator name -> number of translated words (`credits_translators.yml`)
      → #5444
- [ ] a dictionary of flag alignments by country code (`flag_alignments.yml`)
- [ ] a dictionary of various country properties (`<country code>.yml`)

#### keep YAML

Currently, [`com.charleskorn.kaml`](https://github.com/charleskorn/kaml) is used which is a plugin for `kotlinx-serialization`, however, no native targets are supported currently and noone is working on it at the time of writing (see https://github.com/charleskorn/kaml/issues/232). 

Another kotlin multiplatform yaml plugin exists, [`net.mamoe.yamlkt`](https://github.com/Him188/yamlkt), but according to the readme, it is (still) in alpha state. Multiline strings, which are currently used in `changelog.yml`, are "partially supported" since v0.13.0, not sure if this means whether the way multiline strings are used in this project is supported. (`changelog.yml` could be changed to be a pure HTML file, though, or one file for every version.)

If none of these libraries have proper multiplatform native support at the time this ticket is tackled, the task is to convert the listed files to another config file format like JSON or TOML and parse them as these files instead.

Some of these files are generated automatically, such as `credits_contributors.yml`, so some build scripts need to be adapted, too. Below some notes regarding each:

#### convert to TOML

- \+ well-human-readable format, easy to edit

- \- kotlinx-serialization plugin [`com.akuleshov7-ktoml-core`](https://github.com/akuleshov7/ktoml) is multiplatform but implementation is incomplete. In particular, "Array of Tables" feature is missing as the time of writing, which is necessary for `credits_translators.yml`

- \+ kotlinx-serialization plugin [`net.peanuuutz.tomlkt`](https://github.com/Peanuuutz/tomlkt) is multiplatform and is actually complete, looks well tested and has a useful interface. It's rather fresh at the time of writing, though (meaning, we don't know how well tested it is in production and how committed the author is to maintain it).

- \- the above is only marginally smaller (250KB) as `net.mamoe.yamlkt` (261KB) even though one would think that TOML is a much simpler markup language as YAML - maybe that isn't true

#### convert to JSON

- \+ less dependencies: `kotlinx-serialization-json` already is a dependency

- \- no comments possible. So, explanatory comments / docs cannot be included in these files

- still well enough human-readable, given that these files are not that often changed (`changelog.yml` could be changed to be a html file)

---

In general, at the time of writing, I think the best way forward is to simply exchange `kaml` with `yamlkt` and if this does not work, don't bother with TOML because implementation is equally "alpha" than the YAML plugins but use JSON for everything.

Title: Multiplatform `osmfeatures` library
Body:
StreetComplete uses the [iD presets](https://github.com/openstreetmap/id-tagging-schema) in various places in the app. For example, the name of a selected feature is always displayed in the subtitle of every quest form. It is also central in the Check Existence quest, Shops overlay and Street Furniture overlay.

Currently, the Java library [de.westnordost:osmfeatures](https://github.com/westnordost/osmfeatures) is used to load, parse and access the iD presets (and the [name suggestion index](https://github.com/osmlab/name-suggestion-index) that follows the same schema).

This library should (simply) be ported to Kotlin multiplatform. For more information, see https://github.com/westnordost/osmfeatures/issues/22

An alternative would be to create a Rust version of this library, and then follow the same steps as for #5413 but this will result in more effort and more maintenance effort in the long run.

Title: Use `country-boundaries-rust` on iOS
Body:
Requires #5412 to be done first.

StreetComplete needs a reverse country coder, i.e. find the country / state in which a geo position is located.

On Android/JVM, the [`de.westnordost.countryboundaries`](https://github.com/westnordost/countryboundaries) library is used for this.
For iOS/Native, the Rust library [`country-boundaries`](https://github.com/westnordost/country-boundaries-rust) can be used.

### Implementation

1. Current usages of the CountryBoundaries must be wrapped in an interface (or `expect` class but I think I slightly prefer the former)
2. For Android, use the Java library
3. For iOS, find out how to native dependencies written in to an iOS project and use that

Title: Set up KMM Project Structure
Body:
Currently, the project setup is that of a native Android app. The task is to

1. change the project structure to that of a [Kotlin Multiplatform Mobile](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/multiplatform-discover-project.html) project
2. move all files that are already pure kotlin at the time this task is done (no Java or Android dependencies) into the common module, including its tests. (The implementation for iOS is obviously empty for now)
4. test build on Android

After this, work can start on a skeleton implementation of the iOS app.

This should ideally be done when there are as few PRs open as possible, as all PRs will result to have conflicts in every single file.

This should also ideally be done in concert with @Helium314 (the maintainer of the SCEE fork/addon), i.e. the fewer unmerged branches there are, the less work it is to rebase it on top / merge it with all the moved files.

Title: Multiplatform opening hours parser library
Body:
For the opening hours quest and the postbox collection times quest, the app needs to parse the OSM opening hours syntax. 

Currently, the app uses the Java library [`ch.poole.OpeningHoursParser`](https://github.com/simonpoole/OpeningHoursParser). Internally, this parser is not generated by ANTLR but JavaCC because the OSM opening hours schema is too complex to be projected by an EBNF notation according to the aforementioned library's author.

### Implementation

So, a replacement library in pure kotlin can evidently not be generated by e.g. [antlr-kotlin](https://github.com/Strumenta/antlr-kotlin) but would need to be just as custom of a parser as the Java library. 

It should be developed in a separate repository as a separate multiplatform library. It can be pure Kotlin with no platform-dependent implementations.

#### Notes / Hints

- `ch.poole.OpeningHoursParser` is quite well-tested and MIT-licensed. Could use the tests from that library to confirm that the Kotlin reimplementation behaves similar (i.e. test driven development)

- For the `ElementFiltersParser` that parses expressions like `nodes, ways with (shop = supermarket or amenity = cafe) and !opening_hours`, I use the class [`StringWithCursor`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Astreetcomplete%2FStreetComplete+StringWithCursor.kt&type=code), which might be a very handy base to write a custom parser

Title: Multiplatform OSM API client
Body:
Requires #5408 to be done first.

The [OSM API](https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/API_v0.6) is a HTTP REST API that communicates chiefly in XML. This must be ported to Kotlin multiplatform. 

Currently, the Java library [`de.westnordost.osmapi`](https://github.com/westnordost/osmapi) is used for any communication with the OSM API 0.6, i.e. uploading map data, notes and gpx tracks, downloading map data, notes, user data, etc.

The dependency to `osmapi` must be replaced with an implementation based on

- ideally the http client from #5408 and

- a multiplatform XML parser / writer, which is stable, well-tested and ideally (semi-)official / well-maintained and *ideally* but not necessarily compatible with `kotlinx-serialization`. This is also important for other XML parsing in the app, i.e. #5369

- using `kotlinx-datetime` to parse timestamps (already a dependency)

See interfaces [`TracksApi`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Astreetcomplete%2FStreetComplete+TracksApi.kt&type=code), [`MapDataApi`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Astreetcomplete%2FStreetComplete+MapDataApi.kt&type=code) and [`NotesApi`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Astreetcomplete%2FStreetComplete+MapDataApi.kt&type=code)

### XML Parser / Writer requirements

- multiplatform support for at least Java/Android and iOS

- stable, well-tested and ideally (semi-)official  / well-maintained

#### xmlutil

There is currently one xml serializer compatible with kotlinx-serialization: [`io.github.pdvrieze.xmlutil`](https://github.com/pdvrieze/xmlutil). It is also mentioned in the Ktor docs, i.e. is the XML serializer/deserializer used in Ktor.

I didn't find any other multiplatform XML parsers/writers.

Title: Multiplatform HTTP Client (use Ktor Client)
Body:
Use a multiplatform HTTP client. Currently, we are using `java.net.URL` for these.

### HTTP connections in the app are used for

- any communication with OSM servers, i.e. uploading map data, notes and gpx tracks, downloading map data, notes, user data, ...

- querying a [simple text file](https://westnordost.de/streetcomplete/banned_versions.txt) ([`VersionIsBannedChecker`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Astreetcomplete%2FStreetComplete+VersionIsBannedChecker.kt&type=code)) 

- communicating with [an API to upload and activate photos](https://github.com/exploide/sc-photo-service) ([`StreetCompleteImageUploader`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Astreetcomplete%2FStreetComplete+StreetCompleteImageUploader.kt&type=code)) 

- querying user statistics from [a simple API](https://github.com/streetcomplete/sc-statistics-service) ([`StatisticsDownloader`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Astreetcomplete%2FStreetComplete+StatisticsDownloader.kt&type=code)) 

- downloading OSM user avatars ([`AvatarsDownloader`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Astreetcomplete%2FStreetComplete+AvatarsDownloader.kt&type=code)) 

- querying oneway traffic data from [another simple API](https://github.com/streetcomplete/oneway-data-api) ([`TrafficFlowSegmentsApi`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Astreetcomplete%2FStreetComplete+TrafficFlowSegmentsApi.kt&type=code)) 

- during OAuth access token negociation ([`OAuthAuthorization`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Astreetcomplete%2FStreetComplete+OAuthAuthorization.kt&type=code))

### HTTP Client Requirements

- multiplatform support for at least Java/Android and iOS

- stable, well-tested and ideally (semi-)official / well-maintained

- should be injectable, i.e. the http client can be passed as a (constructor) parameter to the function/class that uses it. Important in order to easily test the aforementioned classes

Below some comments on libraries I've looked at.

Note that of the candidates mentioned below, some are asynchronous clients, i.e. use coroutines, which is fine, but may necessitate more changes in the app architecture than simply replacing the java `URL` stuff: The upload and download process that uses the above-mentioned HTTP APIs currently expect these things to be synchronous and manages concurrency itself.

#### OkHttp

OkHttp is said to have basic [multiplatform support starting with 5.0](https://github.com/square/okhttp/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md), but v5.0 has been in alpha since 2021-01 and since one year, no new alpha of v5.0 has been released.

So, while OkHttp is a very very popular library, it is unknown when this ever will be stable. On the other hand, [according to the current main developer](https://github.com/square/okhttp/issues/7883#issuecomment-1837604213), only the APIs are unstable [, not the implementation].

#### Ktor Client

A candidate is [Ktor Client](https://ktor.io/docs/getting-started-ktor-client.html). 

This seems to be a bit of a rabbit hole, because it seems to be designed to be (used as) part of a larger framework, in particular in a project where the server and client(s) basically live in the same repository and share a good portion of the code, which is not the case for our app.
On the other hand, it seems [to plug-in well with kotlinx-serialization](https://ktor.io/docs/serialization.html) (for e.g. JSON and XML), so it seems to be the more integrated solution. 

The library is also featured in the [Compose Multiplatform Wizard](https://terrakok.github.io/Compose-Multiplatform-Wizard/)

#### Fuel

Another candidate is [Fuel](https://github.com/kittinunf/fuel). However, it does not look to be eligible because:

- it is only multiplatform starting with version 3, which is still alpha 
- little activity in repo, neither in response to a security vulnerability nor on v3 development. This does not inspire confidence that it is well-maintained
- all fields of the the HTTP response class are platform dependent (in the current alpha) - so, only multiplatform on paper.

#### Other

- khttp is dead. No commit since 2020. Also, not multiplatform anyway.

- kohttp is dead. No update since 2020. Also, not multiplatform anyway.

---

In general, both Ktor and OkHttp 5 seem okay, though I lean towards Ktor because OkHttp 5 is not stable yet and Ktor seems to be the solution that is more geared towards a Kotlin (MP) ecoystem.

Title: Add GPX import functionality
Body:
To map along a predefined route, set your cache big enough and export a GPX track from some other routing app by sharing it with StreetComplete.

StreetComplete imports the track to show it on the map, and - if you accept the number of downloads and area - schedules tile downloading along the track for offline usage,

Fixes #473, fixes #1757.

Body:

**Why**: In order to make a tiny step towards #1892

**Testing performed**
- [x] just a `./gradlew test`

I suggest a careful review as a bug in tests might cause us miss some issues in the future.

Title: Way with complicated surface tag shows as without surface tag
Body:
This way https://www.openstreetmap.org/way/787847733 and the oneway ways the the northeast have a rather complicated surface that is different for different lanes. However, on SC its surface is quested and in the surface overlay it shows red, i.e. as if its surface has not been tagged yet.
![Screenshot (23 okt](https://github.com/streetcomplete/StreetComplete/assets/69100427/0453c5a7-2ee3-4462-b684-4503bffd57d4)

This creates the risk that an unsuspecting user will decide that it's "mostly asphalt" or "mostly sett" and will answer the quest like this, which would be wrong.
Probably a very rare occasion so not very urgent... 

**Expected Behavior**
Ways that have a more complicated surface tag (for instance `surface:lanes:*=*|*`; there may be others) should not be considered as being without surface tag. No surface quest should appear, and they should not appear as red in the surface overlay.

**Versions affected**
54.1

Title: Translations update from Hosted Weblate
Body:
Translations update from [Hosted Weblate](https://hosted.weblate.org) for [Trail Sense/Trail Sense Android](https://hosted.weblate.org/projects/trail-sense/trail-sense-android/).


It also includes following components:

* [Trail Sense/Trail Sense User Guide](https://hosted.weblate.org/projects/trail-sense/trail-sense-user-guide/)

* [Trail Sense/Trail Sense Store Metadata](https://hosted.weblate.org/projects/trail-sense/trail-sense-store-metadata/)



Current translation status:

![Weblate translation status](https://hosted.weblate.org/widget/trail-sense/trail-sense-android/horizontal-auto.svg)

Title: Request: Update app icon
Body:
Update the app icon to be more modern (suggestion was to make it brighter and more attractive).

Not sure if I'm going to do this, since I like the simplicity of the icon - but I want to consider some alternatives before just closing this.

Title: Only load beacons within a region
Body: For nearby and maps, only load beacons which are within the visible region. Construct SQL statements based on the lat/lng bounds.

Title: Split settings by tool
Body: Some settings (ex. navigation) have multiple tools under the same settings section. These should be split so it is easier to find settings for the tool they belong to.

Title: Tool menu - settings
Body: When you long press a tool, if there's an associated settings page add a menu item for that.

Title: Improve map cropping corner drag
Body: In other apps which allow perspective cropping, when you lift your finger the point does not move. A similar mechanism should be added to the Photo Maps tool as it is easy to get the point perfectly lined up and then knock it out of alignment by lifting your finger.

Title: Tool registry
Body:
A way to add new tools without all the boilerplate.

- [ ] Register tool (shows up in tools list)
- [ ] Register quick action (available everywhere)
- [ ] Register guide
- [ ] Register quick setting tile
- [ ] Register settings
- [ ] Register volume shortcut (can be used to prevent volume actions while on the tool)
- [ ] Register triggers/actions
- [ ] Register background service (for the battery tool)
- [ ] Register notification channels
- [ ] Register diagnostics

This should also more easily allow me to move tools to a separate module or load a plugin in the future.

The initial implementation of this can just be a configuration object, but in the future it can be decoupled.

Title: Improve settings code
Body:
I don't like defining settings screens in XML and that each needs to be its own fragment. 

Create a mechanism for defining settings in kotlin and populate the same fragment/list view.

Title: Group beacons when zoomed out on map
Body:
This will both reduce clutter and improve performance. 

Group nearby beacons into a single "beacon" with a count of how many beacons are in that area (center of group).

Title: Paths have a point out of place when zooming a map
Body:
Sometimes while zooming the map, a path point will jump out of place causing a line to be drawn to it for a couple frames. It goes away pretty quick and I can't seem to get it to appear while not zooming.

I think this is because the pixels are mapping differently while zooming long paths, causing one to be out of place - but I'm not completely sure.

This may have happened after I fixed the zoom issue, but it is hard to tell since the zoom issue was already making paths jump around. Could also be some of the path clipping improvements (ruled out: RDP filter, intersection clipping).

Title: Use drawLines on charts instead of drawPath
Body:
Add an option to use drawLines instead of drawPath to improve the efficiency of rendering.

See if a setting is needed for this (depends on if there are transparent chart lines)

Title: Automations (Triggers, Actions)
Body:
While writing up #2157 I thought it may be useful to let the user trigger behaviors for other things such as time of day, location (geofence), distance travelled, etc. This could also be the foundation of a generic alert mechanism (#1915, #719, #647).

This could be a tasker/IFTTT like feature where the user specifies the trigger and a list of actions that should occur. 

This ticket will be focused on converting existing features to automations and not be user facing. After this is implemented, a new ticket can be created for allowing a user facing tool for automations.

Title: Backtrack time limit
Body:
Allow the user to set how long to run backtrack for - this is useful if they are hiking all day and forget to turn it off at night.

No limit by default, configurable in settings (maybe make it more visible on the Paths tool?).

Title: Power saving mode actions
Body:
Allow turning off or reducing the frequency (user specified) of the following services:
- [ ] Backtrack
- [ ] Weather Monitor
- [ ] Pedometer

These settings should be in the power settings, and a link should exist from within each of the tools settings to power settings ("Power saving mode behavior" or similar).

Both the disabling and frequency reduction should be undone when power saving mode is disabled.

Title: Add accessibility option to increase size of my location marker
Body:
The chevron is quite small and hard to see, epically in bad lighting conditions. If this could be larger (and maybe allow color change as well) this would be helpful.

Increasing the point size on paths might be a nice feature as well, but not as critical.

Title: Make compass smoothing a percentage
Body: Just like the barometer smoothing setting - it should range from 0 to 100 and display as a percentage in the summary.

Title: Add compass visual to compass settings
Body: It is easier to adjust the compass smoothing when there's a visual of the compass.

Title: Add app license to About section
AR astronomy time of day
Body:
- [ ] Add a date / time picker
- [ ] Add slider for the time of day
- [ ] Adjust the paths for the date
- [ ] Add a "live" toggle that resorts back to current time
- [ ] Determine how to draw paths - should it render the full day's paths except when live is enabled (which renders above the horizon?), or should it always be centered?

Title: Option to switch to gyro only after calibrating AR with the sun/moon
Body:
After calibrating the AR mode using the sun/moon (#2025) the device should switch to gyro only (maybe for a certain duration because of drift?)

This should be a setting - experiment with the default

Title: Improve AR positioning using a VPS
Body:
Maybe something simpler like optical flow can be used to determine how the device is moving. This can be fused with the GPS position / speed to better determine where the device is and avoid locations jumping around.

It's possible this can also be used to construct a depth map, but that's future research.

Title: Research 3D rendering
Body:
This might come in handy at some point, particularly with the AR features and the solar panel aligner.

I think my current AR framework would allow for this, but I want to experiment with drawing something in 3D.

Title: Trail marker detection
Body:
Sometimes when hiking it can be difficult to see the next trail marker. It may be possible to use the camera to assist with the detection.

Experiment with the following:
- A simple color filter
- Color + shape matching

Once a marker is detected, it should be possible to estimate the distance to the marker as well given the sizes are pretty standard. That also means the distance and direction can be estimated, meaning if the current location is known, the location of the marker(s) can be estimated.

Title: Estimate distance using camera
Body:
Track both the point of interest and device rotation to estimate distance using parallax. More research needed.

This might be able to estimate object size as well if distance can be estimated automatically (clinometer)

Potentially leverage stereo cameras if the device reports distance between cameras

Title: Settings user guides
Body:
- [ ] General Settings (Units, Theme, Power, Privacy, Errors, Backup / Restore)
- [ ] Sensors > Compass
- [ ] Sensors > GPS
- [ ] Sensors > Altimeter
- [ ] Sensors > Barometer (rewrite)
- [ ] Sensors > Thermometer (rewrite)

Basically the same as the tool guides in https://github.com/kylecorry31/Trail-Sense/issues/2074

Title: Augmented reality zoom bar not working
Body:
The zoom bar isn't working when an AR view is active over the camera view. This is because of the touch event consumption.

Either figure out how to pass through the touch event for the zoom bar, or make the AR view have its own zoom bar (leaning toward the later, so it can be used without the camera active).

Title: Improve fused altimeter continuous calibration
Body:
I'm not super happy with the continuous calibration - it does a good job of not letting the barometer drift, but I feel it still has the downside of the GPS elevation not being too accurate when you keep it open.

My thought is to have a GPS weight allowance that builds up over time, and each reading subtracts from it. That means the first couple readings after a while will pull the elevation in the right direction, but keeping it open will not cause it to fluctuate with the GPS noise.

Title: Tap clinometer camera to lock
Body:
If you tap the viewfinder, it does not lock the angle but it should.

It should support both the tap and tap and hold.

Title: Improve tool / guide search with keywords
Body: Add a list of keywords to each tool to improve the search results. The keywords should be alternate names, features, actions, and related activities for the tool.

Title: Add search to user guide
Body: Add a search bar to the user guide that filters the guides based on title and description.

Title: Traccar Client for AAOS (Volvo)
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
It is not related to a problem. It would be a new feature.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Port the Android app to Android Automotive OS (AAOS) (Volvo Cars). To get an accurate tracking of the car's location, routes, etc...


**Describe alternatives you've considered**
The only alternatives I found so far are somewhat expensive GPS tracking solutions, closed source, not always transparent on what happens with the data, and not able to integrate or connect with other services

**Additional context**
I don't have additional context for now.

Title: Color resources for drawable icons
Body:
Icons refactoring (b80fde0b6dc0e69c46071ce9e38edda00d7f723c) made most (all?) of drawable icons "non-themable" - they are drawn with hard-coded color values instead of colors from `\app\src\main\res\values\colors.xml`.
An example from `\app\src\main\res\drawable\ic_launcher_foreground.xml` (`path` is omitted for brevity):
```xml
<vector xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    android:width="108dp"
    android:height="108dp"
    android:viewportWidth="64"
    android:viewportHeight="64">
  <group android:scaleX="0.71"
      android:scaleY="0.71"
      android:translateX="9.28"
      android:translateY="9.28">
    <path
        android:pathData="..."
        android:strokeWidth=".84379"
        android:fillColor="#2e7d32"/>
  </group>
</vector>
```
The color should be specified as `android:fillColor="@color/primary"`.
I'm not sure whether this issue hits end-users (it is worth checking whether the app adapts properly to UI theme tuning) - I've discovered it when customizing the look for a debug build with status widget (#429).

The list of icons to fix:
* `\app\src\main\res\drawable\ic_launcher_foreground.xml`
* `\app\src\main\res\drawable\ic_sos_foreground.xml`
* `\app\src\main\res\drawable\ic_start_foreground.xml`
* `\app\src\main\res\drawable\ic_stop_foreground.xml`

Title: Profile support
Body:
I use the client for different purposes, like riding my bike, snowboarding, driving or tracking a flight. All purposes require different settings when to store a position (duration/distance) to create an accurate recording without wasting database space.

It would be great if the client would support a set of 5-10 predefined and custom nameable profiles which can selected just before starting recording. The name of the profile should be visible also as a reminder which parameter set is active.

No alternatives, the current setup leads sometimes to wrong parameters because they are only shown when selecting them for a change and thus a too detailed or too less detailed recording.

Title: Network available and location available report
Body:
Hi.
Again, i am very pleased using this app, it's great. I have been working getting the values traccar client sends, it's almost ideal to me.

I wonder if you could, please, in future versions of traccar client, add a coup of fields in the array of data it sends, i mean having this:

```Array
(
    [id] => XXxxxXXXxXX
    [timestamp] => 1686429423
    [lat] => -38.9613683
    [lon] => -68.0680459
    [speed] => 6.556485525446892
    [bearing] => 244.0159454345703
    [altitude] => 285.1000061035156
    [accuracy] => 96.0
    [batt] => 4.0
    [charge] => true
)
```

just add two like:

```
[network]  => true
[location] => false
```

 am asking since the app already reports when network is available or not in the status, it can be sent in the array, for example.

Hope you consider it, thanks.

Title: Add disable certificate validation option
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Traccar server is behind reverse proxy with self-signed certificate. (not using a domain and connect directly with ip)
Traccar android client can not connect to server and there is no option to disable certificate validation and ignore the warnings related to.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
It would be great to add an option in android client to disable certificate validation and ignore related errors.

Title: mutual tls client certificates
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Traccar server is behind reverse proxy with mutual tls
Traccar android client can not connect to server unless it provides a client certificate

**Describe the solution you'd like**
A settings menu with a file picker to manually add the p12 client cert and the cert password

**Describe alternatives you've considered**


**Additional context**
Owntracks have this implemented
https://owntracks.org/booklet/features/tlscert/

It would be great to benefit from the extra security layer mtls can provide
No traccar server work is mantatory as server side a webserver can stand in front traccar providing mtls and reverse proxy

Title: Error creating a shortcut for start or stop service
Body:
**Describe the bug**
I was using MacroDroid to start and stop the service under certain conditions. Everything worked well on my S9+ running Android 9.
Even under Android 12 everything works well (The dev of MacroDroid checked that).
But under Android 13 I get an error. 

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior: See screenshot in [macrodroidforum: <"Launch Shortcut" not working>](https://www.macrodroidforum.com/index.php?threads/launch-shortcut-not-working.3736/)

that the macro can start and stop the service

**Additional context**

Title: Possibility of sending to 2 different ips (Redundancy)
Body: It would be important to be able to configure 2 different ips for sending positions. Basically the same as fixed vehicle modules have(Redundancy). If the app is not succesful in sending information to the first IP, it will try the second IP.

Title: Bluetooth beacon / Apple "Find My" support
Body:
It would be nice if Traccar Client could broadcast "Find My"-compatible BLE beacons when it can't reach the server, so devices without 24/7 connectivity could at least get occasional updates.

https://github.com/seemoo-lab/openhaystack/ has details on how it can work.

How can I change Default server URL, I have changed the address located at app-src-main-res-valuse-values.xml, but it did not effect when I test the code,

Title: Status Widget
Body:
It would be handy to have a widget which could show the application status - whether its service running or not.
When the widget is clicked, the main window may be opened.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
On some smartphones with too restrictive built-in task managers (Oukitel Y4800 with Android 9 is a typical example), the application can be closed (killed) by the system despite all optimization settings (ignoring battery optimization, white-listing, etc.).

**Describe the solution you'd like**
A possible solution (a kind of workaround) to keep the app alive is to add an updatable widget (to the home screen), which will let system know that the app shouldn't be killed (for power optimization reasons).

**Additional context**
Refer to the [forum thread](https://www.traccar.org/forums/topic/how-to-keep-app-running-on-android-9/) for more details.

Title: Android client doesn't work on phone without SIM
Body:
Traccar Client for Android doesn't send any update on phone without SIM card

I have installed Traccar Client from PlayStore on smartphone LG-K40 and configured its ID on demo.traccar.org
Double checked that internet connection through WiFi is working , give permission to access localization services
set update frequency to 30 and start service
On Status page of client I can see 'Service started' and 'Network online' messages but no no messages are sent to demo.traccar.org  ( verified by tcpdump on gateway )

If I put SIM card into the phone, everything works fine.

Title: Reporting doesn't work on background.
Body:
**Describe the bug**
I've installed _traccar_ on my device of _Xiaomi Redmi Note 9 Pro_. Yesterday, it reported well. However, this afternoon, I wanted to see where my device is and saw no _Online_ status as the app went to background and while on the background, as far as I know, nothing works on that model. This makes tracking pointless. If I forget my smartphone somewhere, I can't track it as the app doesn't work on the background. And once in the foreground, there's no need for tracking. Would it make a use of network nodes then it could be used on laptops but they don't have GPS-modules usually installed.

**To Reproduce**
Just install the app on _Xiaomi Redmi Note 9 Pro_-device, configure it with the correct server, wait overnight and without waking up the smartphone, check the report and see that the position is unknown!

**Expected behavior**
I expect the app to report regardless on the status or model of the smartphone.


**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
 - Device: Xiaomi Redmi Note 9 Pro
 - OS: MIUI Global 12.0.1

Title: Getting java.io.FileNotFoundException: http://demo.traccar.org:5055
Body:
I simply download the code "traccar-client-android " and run it in my android studio 3.5.3 , always getting   **java.io.FileNotFoundException: http://demo.traccar.org:5055**  Exception while requesting to the server for connection . 

I already changed with the different networks and tested in various devices but getting same error : " FileNotFoundException"

My Request : 
```
http://demo.traccar.org:5055id=300108&timestamp=1582523670&lat=30.7093901&lon=76.6952626&speed=0.0&bearing=0.0&altitude=278.0&accuracy=20.802000045776367&batt=81.0
```

Title: "High" accuracy drains battery and never reports, "medium" accuracy too inaccurate
Body:
For users of the Android client who are using it to track people with phones, obviously those phones spend a lot of time indoors. When indoors on high accuracy, Traccar's behavior seems to be to _only_ use GPS. It will keep GPS on seemingly the entire time it is indoors, never getting a fix. This will drain the battery very quickly as well as not provide useful location data. Medium accuracy is only really useful in populated areas where there is sufficient wifi coverage to give semi-accurate location. And even in heavily populated areas it is sometimes unreliable.

I see two possible fixes for this:
A. Have a timeout for a GPS fix. If it doesn't work, fall back to a "medium" fix until the next location update interval.
B: Rather than high/medium/low implying a type of fix... provide a desired accuracy. If I can get a 100 meter or better fix with wifi, no need to try with GPS. Wifi fixes consume less battery and it's probably worth trying them in most cases, falling back to GPS if you end up with a "1.4 km" radius.

I think it is undesired behavior to have to choose high (which will drain batteries indoors very quickly, won't provide any location info indoors) and medium (which will work pretty well indoors, but often knowingly provide 1 mile or more of inaccuracy).

I understand Traccar is leveraging another library for location data, but that that library is poorly maintained.

Title: Some users do not discover how to add a stop
Body:
Adding stops in Organic Maps is super easy, and requires less clicks than in some other apps:
1. Build a route using "Route To" or "Route From" + "Route To" buttons after selecting anything on the map with a single or a long tap, or after selecting a search result/a bookmark.
2. Select any place and press "Add Stop" at the bottom.

It is not obvious for some users that to add a stop they should select this stop first on the map.

A simple toast hint/suggestion can be helpful here for anyone who built the route first time:
"Select any place to add an intermediate stop".

Any other ideas are welcome.

Title: [ios] Improve TTS voice selection
Body: Currently, it's only possible to select a subset of the available voices on iOS. For example, if a user selects English as TTS language, an Australian voice is selected because en_AU comes before en_US in the voice list of iOS. In addition, the selection logic in the small language list is broken and doesn't confirm to the Apple styleguide. This commit allows a user to select more voices (e.g. English (United States), English (India)) and refactors the selection logic. Technical details: - add `tts_languages.py` to `generate_localizations.sh` to update the list of supported TTS languages each time the locales are generated - use property observers to update TTS language (makes implementation easier) Fixes #6840 Fixes #3222 Fixes #2178 **Before** **After**

Body:
**Describe the issue**
When navigating (currently only tested navigating by car), if a POI (e.g. a way) is selected and the Add Place to OpenStreetMap button selected then cancelled, the four dialog buttons when not navigating appear and are interactable


**Steps to reproduce**
1. Begin navigating somewhere
2. While navigating, select a POI (e.g. a way) and select Add Place to OpenStreetMap
3. Press Cancel in the top left of the screen

 - iPhone 8


**Additional context**
This video was gathered when I was not in control of the vehicle and at no point was I in danger recording the footage. The video was recorded in December 2023, but testing indicates the problem has persisted since the latest update in January

Title: Reinstate updates of OrgranicMaps on F-droid app store
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

Organic Maps updates on [F-droid](https://f-droid.org)  app store have been suspended (on OrganicMaps [request](https://github.com/organicmaps/organicmaps/issues/6967#issuecomment-1890939017)), seemingly due to [disagreements](https://github.com/organicmaps/organicmaps/issues/7157) about what constitutes an [advertisement](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/advertisement). 

I'd like again to be able to get newest version of OrganicMaps from f-droid  (as that is the only app store I consider safe and pro-user oriented).

**Describe the ideal solution**
Organic Maps should be privacy friendly and without advertisements as its description claims, _at least **by default**_. 

While ideally [contentious Kayak-promoting advertisements code](https://github.com/organicmaps/organicmaps/pull/6523) would be completely removed, making them **opt-in** (as [suggested in this PR](https://github.com/organicmaps/organicmaps/pull/6720#pullrequestreview-1799832861)) would also be acceptable. 

On 14 Jan 2024. there were also talks about [some other solution being worked on](https://github.com/organicmaps/organicmaps/issues/6967#issuecomment-1890939017), but no details have been shared since then.


**Describe alternatives you have considered**
- Informing F-droid users of OrganicMaps with Ads anti-feature stating that _"Hotel features have paid referral links to Kayak not contained in map data."_ (this was [opposed by OM devs as unfair](https://github.com/organicmaps/organicmaps/issues/6967#issuecomment-1890782874))



**Additional context**
Is there perhaps already a place where one can follow a progress of that new solution? Is any help needed? Any estimates when one might expect it?

Title: Update first map download message
Body:
**Problem Statement**
For the average person in the United States and Europe, where cell phone signals are really good and online maps are the primary navigation apps, offline maps are not well known and to many people are a new concept.  When opening the app for the first time, they are greeted with a message "Country name, City name, xxx.x MB" and a "Download Map" button, without any context for why something needs to be downloaded and only for a certain city.

**Criteria**
If no maps have been downloaded yet, in place of the current message, display the more detailed message below.  After at least one map is downloaded, display the standard message:
In addition to the information currently displayed for map downloads, at the top of the message add the following text: "Organic Maps functions using offline maps which are downloaded to your phone, enabling navigation and fast search without a cell signal.  Download the map to get started."

Title: Highlight that drive through is available for a POI
Body:
> In the search results and on the place page, "Drive Through Available"
or simply "Drive Through" may be displayed. In a place page, there can
be a car icon with the text "Drive Through Available"/"Drive Through".
The tags for this feature include "drive_through=yes/no", as well as a
tag for drive through opening hours "opening_hours:drive-through".

A car icon can likely be used.

Title: Support area:highway and highway=* area=yes
Body: The combination of tag "area:highway" and tags "highway" along with "area=yes" is not recognized by Organic Maps. Although "area:highway" is used for mapping non-routable road borders, the combination of "highway=" and "area=yes" creates a routable feature. When a road is mapped as an area with these tags, Organic Maps displays it as a way instead of an area, while not showing any "area:highway" roads. This is similar to issue #6391. Additional information are available here: https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:area:highway

Title: Icons: missing files, inconsistent sizes, design issues
Body:
## Context As a follow-up to PRs #7118 and #7056 as well as issue #4017 I've examined current state of icon sizes and designs using a simple R script. I also hacked a wiki page to compare `clear` and `night` styles. https://github.com/organicmaps/organicmaps/wiki/Icons:-clear-vs.-night,-sizes These are the issues I found. ## Design choices As far as I can tell from visual comparison, the following `night` approaches were used: - POI icons (round and square ones) - shape color and outline color changed from white to black, but outline keeps "60%" opacity and fill not changed - other icons vary significantly - outline color changed `#ffffff` to `#111111`, outline opacity changed `80%` to `60%` ( _e.g. `motorcycle-parking-m`_) - color changed `#717065` to `#777777` (_e.g. `mountain-pass-m`_) - opacity changed `100%` to `60%` (_e.g. `firehydrant-m`_) - color changed `#7f5933` to `#717065`, opacity changed `100%` to `60%` (_e.g. `aircraft-m`_) - subway icons - can't find any system, maybe tweaked by hand Could someone who worked on night icons provide any info on how these techniques were developed? Is there some approach described anywhere? I'm worried that some of the resulting icons may look not so good next to each other. Maybe documenting a clear-to-night conversion flow would help to harmonize current designs and provide some clues to future attempts at this. ## Missing files ``` Total of 658 unique SVG file names File missing from style-night name width height xmin ymin xsize ysize bank-l.svg 21 21 0 0 28 28 File missing from style-night name width height xmin ymin xsize ysize banknote-l.svg 21 21 0 0 28 28 File missing from style-clear name width height xmin ymin xsize ysize media_shop-m.svg 18 18 0 0 24 24 File missing from style-clear name width height xmin ymin xsize ysize motel-m.svg 18 18 0 0 24 24 ``` ## Weird size or viewBox ``` Non-integer width or height name width height xmin ymin xsize ysize barrier-entrance-s.svg 5.25 9 0 0 7 12 Non-integer width or height name width height xmin ymin xsize ysize bench.svg 11.25 9 0 0 15 12 Non-integer width or height name width height xmin ymin xsize ysize elevator.svg 11.25 11.25 0 0 15 15 Non-integer width or height name width height xmin ymin xsize ysize entrance-s.svg 5.25 9 0 0 7 12 Non-integer width or height name width height xmin ymin xsize ysize exit-s.svg 8.0625 9 0 0 10.75 12 Viewbox start is not (0,0) name width height xmin ymin xsize ysize ford-m.svg 11 5 -0.5 8.5 15 6 Non-integer width or height name width height xmin ymin xsize ysize lightrail-berlin-s.svg 11.25 11.25 0 0 15 15 Non-integer width or height name width height xmin ymin xsize ysize motorcycle-parking-m.svg 21.75 21.75 0 0 26 26 Non-integer width or height name width height xmin ymin xsize ysize mountain-pass-s.svg 5.25 3 0 0 7 4 Non-integer width or height name width height xmin ymin xsize ysize parking-disabled-m.svg 11.25 11.25 0 0 15 15 Non-integer width or height name width height xmin ymin xsize ysize star-m.svg 8.25 8.25 0 0 11 11 Non-integer width or height name width height xmin ymin xsize ysize transit_monorail-m.svg 9 11.25 0 0 12 15 Non-integer width or height name width height xmin ymin xsize ysize zero-icon.svg 5.25 5.25 0 0 7 7 ``` ## What next I guess the following steps could be something like that: - [ ] Decide the fate of files missing from either `clear` or `night` style. Are they even used, if no one noticed their loss? - [ ] Collaborate to categorize icons by their semantics to further improve design inconsistencies. Another great wiki page https://github.com/organicmaps/organicmaps/wiki/Icons already has 427 icons of total 658. - [ ] Write or find some documentation for night style icon design. - [ ] Manually fix not only sizes, but also stroke widths for affected icons (`entrance`, `exit`, `ford` and others) - [ ] Establish an icon design guideline document to specify desired stroke widths, corner radius for shapes, fill preference, pixel grid howtos and a color palette. Then eventually fix all the icons to look better. Help needed for the first three tasks, and I hope to tackle the last two tasks by myself in the near future. Comments and discussion is as always appreciated @organicmaps/design ❤️
Here is the R script in case someone wants to check my findings. ```r library(xml2) get_svg_attrs <- function(path) { files <- list.files(path, full.names = T) files <- sort(files) tempdf <- data.frame() for (i in seq_along(files)) { this <- files[i] svg_in <- read_xml( x = this, encoding = "UTF-8", options = c("PEDANTIC", "NOBLANKS", "NSCLEAN") ) svg_in <- xml_ns_strip(svg_in) svg_in <- xml_find_first(svg_in, "/svg") viewBox <- strsplit(xml_attr(svg_in, "viewBox"), " ")[[1]] tempdf <- rbind( tempdf, data.frame( name = basename(this), width = as.numeric(xml_attr(svg_in, "width")), height = as.numeric(xml_attr(svg_in, "height")), xmin = as.numeric(viewBox[1]), ymin = as.numeric(viewBox[2]), xsize = as.numeric(viewBox[3]), ysize = as.numeric(viewBox[4]) ) ) } return(tempdf) } df_clear <- get_svg_attrs("~/organicmaps/data/styles/clear/style-clear/symbols") df_night <- get_svg_attrs("~/organicmaps/data/styles/clear/style-night/symbols") svgnames <- unique(c(df_clear$name, df_night$name)) cat("Total of", length(svgnames), "unique SVG file names\n\n") for (i in svgnames) { x0 <- df_clear[df_clear$name == i, ] x1 <- df_night[df_night$name == i, ] row.names(x0) <- NULL row.names(x1) <- NULL if (!isTRUE(all.equal(x0, x1))) { if (nrow(x0) == 0) { cat("File missing from style-clear \n") } else if (nrow(x1) == 0) { cat("File missing from style-night \n") } else { cat("Different attributes for clear and night \n") } print(rbind(x0, x1), row.names = F) cat("\n\n") } else { if (x0$width %% 1 != 0 || x0$height %% 1 != 0) { if(!grepl("subway-", i)) { # Skip subway icons for now cat("Non-integer width or height \n") print(x0, row.names = F) cat("\n\n") } } if (x0$xmin != 0 || x0$ymin != 0) { cat("Viewbox start is not (0,0) \n") print(x0, row.names = F) cat("\n\n") } } } ## Wiki page ---- df_all <- merge(df_clear, df_night, by = c( "name","width","height","xmin","ymin","xsize","ysize"), all = TRUE) x <- " |name|style-clear|style-night|width|height| |-|-|-|-|-| " for(i in seq_along(df_all$name)) { name <- df_all$name[i] url0 <- paste0( '' ) url1 <- paste0( '' ) x <- paste0(x, "|", name, "|", url0, "|", url1, "|", df_all[df_all$name==name,]$width, "|", df_all[df_all$name==name,]$height, "|", "\n") } clipr::write_clip(x) ```

Title: [ios] fix 'Location Services are disabled' unnecessary dialog
Body: Closes https://github.com/organicmaps/organicmaps/issues/794 ### Description: To correctly work with `CLLocationManager` and receive notifications from it, you need to follow several conditions: 1. Use a delegate to receive status updates instead of directly accessing the property (because it is deprecated) https://developer.apple.com/documentation/corelocation/cllocationmanager/1423523-authorizationstatus 2. During the manager's initialization and delegate assignment (if the application is launched for the first time), it immediately falls into the `kCLAuthorizationStatusNotDetermined` state, where you should request permission to use it. An alert requesting location usage permission will then appear on the screen. Other states should be handled here too. 3. If you request permission outside the delegate in a separate method, you will immediately receive a denied error in the delegate method  `locationManager didFailWithError` (thus following point 2). 4. Avoid using the `locationServicesEnabled` method because it may cause an error, as indicated by the compiler. If location is unavailable, the delegate will inform us about it. ___ In the old implementation, the following occurs on the first launch: 1. The didChangeAuthorizationStatus method is immediately triggered with notDetermined. 1. Then didFailWithError is triggered with userDenied(1) (and cause one alert behind second) 3. Next, we choose the permission type, and didChangeAuthorizationStatus is called again 4. Finally, a failure occurs, but this time with 0 (if allowed) This pull request fixes this situation, and errors are no longer triggered during startup. ![Simulator Screen Recording - iPhone 15 Pro - 2024-01-17 at 20 55 38](https://github.com/organicmaps/organicmaps/assets/79797627/045cae30-8bb2-4777-a9ca-6184ec736605) On this gif `Dont allow` was pressed, not `Allow once`! ![Simulator Screen Recording - iPhone 15 Pro - 2024-01-17 at 20 56 19](https://github.com/organicmaps/organicmaps/assets/79797627/f99211be-6348-4608-94a8-d61497473ecc) ![Simulator Screen Recording - iPhone 15 Pro - 2024-01-17 at 20 56 49](https://github.com/organicmaps/organicmaps/assets/79797627/c54c42e6-e9d6-4c63-9191-555f2ae9fd62)

Title: Having access to national limits informations inside a country
Body:
When crossing a border, there are generally some rules about roads. Speed limit depending, on in city, outside city, national roads, motorways, highways, and it's highway toll payement system. There are often, but not always, some big panels with national routes about speed.

On crowded routes however, it's not always possible to look at border signs, especially, as example, in Schengen space, where there is no more border stop post. This information are useful to compute defaults speed on most roads, and can then be accessed by user to remember local speed limits.

Concerning national speed limits, there is a page on the wiki of OSM, that could be used to fill the database: https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Default_speed_limits

Commons.wikimedia.org also have some panels similar to those displayed on some countries, when crossing the border, with open licenses, including several in SVG format, they could maybe be integrated in the window too?

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Category:Diagrams_of_National_Speed_Limits_road_signs

Some other information like how work toll on highspeed could be not known, for a foreigner coming in a country, that's the case for example in Czech republic, where car number must be registered on governement site. This kind of details could be of interest for driver entering in a foreign country.

Title: [ios] Fix bug with the "Continue detecting your current location" alert
Body:
Closes https://github.com/organicmaps/organicmaps/issues/6390

### Problem description:
When the device loses GPS for some reason Core raises `pendingTimeoutCallback` which triggers the Alert.
And when it happens in the background mode (when the app is closed ios stops updating location after some time), the alert will become pending in the UI queue and will be shown _when the user opens the application again_. So it produces annoying bugs.

This PR fixes this issue by skipping the alert presenting if the app state is not active.

Because the bug is not easy to reproduce on a real device, it needs to be tested carefully.

Additional improvements:
In the alert, there are 2 options to choose: `Close` and `Continue`.
In Android `Close` stops detecting location and `Continue` do nothing.
https://github.com/organicmaps/organicmaps/blob/f7b526f9d9adf54b940abbceefabf1073500330d/android/app/src/main/java/app/organicmaps/MwmActivity.java#L1994C1-L2017C17

In iOS (for some reason) alert stops detecting location first, and then `Stop` does nothing, and `Continue` restart detection.
https://github.com/organicmaps/organicmaps/blob/394d7d00147b1a47a53dbbf62fe8b19e8a8a8ed3/iphone/Maps/Classes/MapViewController.mm#L523C1-L535C2

So I decided to sync this logic and make it the same way as in Android.

Body:

Adding a symbolic link

fixes it ... for now.

Title: The actual frequency of the setting at LO Frequency offset
Body:
The signal receiving from the insat-3dr satellite in Windows 8.1.
I use a  in C-band LNB-converter with LO=5950 MHz.
The received frequency is 4781.5 MHz, in the converter it's subtracted from the LO frequency: 5950 - 4781.5 = 1169.5 MHz.

With unadjusted tuning scale, reception is successful at the frequency 1169.5 MHz.
![20240119_insat3dr_plutoplusip_noLO](https://github.com/SatDump/SatDump/assets/154206911/2525d809-bcf9-464d-857c-6fba583b9ae6)

If I set LO Frequency offset 5950 in the LO field, then the scale shows the actual reception frequency of 4781.5 MHz.
![20240119_insat3dr_plutoplusip_LO](https://github.com/SatDump/SatDump/assets/154206911/d975866f-f6af-430b-bdb7-e2d8f3ad7944)

However, there is no reception and I find a strange value in the log:
[07:11:21 - 19/01/2024] (T) Using PlutoSDR IP Address 192.168.64.61
[07:11:21 - 19/01/2024] (D) Set PlutoSDR samplerate to 8000000
[07:11:22 - 19/01/2024] (D) Set PlutoSDR frequency to 485532704
[07:11:22 - 19/01/2024] (D) Set PlutoSDR gain to 33, mode manual

[07:11:36 - 19/01/2024] (I) Waiting for the thread...
[07:11:36 - 19/01/2024] (I) Thread stopped

Title: Update command args for live goes_hrit in official docs
Body:
Hello, I noticed that on the official documentation page for the command-line usage it has an example for goes_hrit that is missing an 'e6' after the frequency value. Currently if you just copy and paste the example you get 0 dB SNR output. With the added e6 it should read:

`satdump live goes_hrit output_test_wtf --source rtlsdr --samplerate 2.4e6 --frequency 1694.1e6 --gain 40 --http_server 0.0.0.0:8080`

The page its on: https://www.satdump.org/posts/basic-usage/#live-processing

I wasn't sure where to report this, its so minor. I just had some trouble figuring it out when I blindly copied the command. Was hoping to save anyone else from the same trouble.

Title: Himawaricast decoder - compression error message Bzip2
Elektro L4 Misaligned Images with low SNR
Body:
I'm getting about 3-4 dB SNR on Elektro-L4, and images are coming in, but getting a perfect image is quite rare. Often the images are corrupted with misaligned sections.

This is using a 65cm discovery dish with linear feed. I realize the dish size might too small for Elektro, and also the feed is linear instead of circular, hence the SNR is probably too low resulting in some errors in the decoding. From what I've seen from others more than 6 dB is probably required for perfect images.

But is there any possible enhancements to the way SatDump stitches the segments together? For example in this image almost everything is there apart from some missing data in the lower right, and this appears to have caused a full misaligned segment. If the misaligned section was at least aligned the image would be usable.

![L4_8_20240112T093000Z](https://github.com/SatDump/SatDump/assets/17711170/e7f31008-4d13-40e2-8ace-fd0a4ce198e4)
![L4_4_20240112T123000Z](https://github.com/SatDump/SatDump/assets/17711170/d896153d-5da2-44da-a0b3-31eb4a343f47)

On the color images there is also often corruption with what looks like the color components not aligning.

![L4_221_20240112T063000Z](https://github.com/SatDump/SatDump/assets/17711170/902bc503-c138-4f3a-a3b3-abfde892ea89)

Also while I'm here, has anyone else noticed that while Elektro is circularly polarized, it also has a slight linear polarization to it? Optimizing the linear feed angle results in a difference of about 2 dB.

Title: Save record only when satellite is above selected minimum elevation
Body:
In Schedule and config menu there is presently an option about satellite minimum elevation.
This selects satellite passes with at least requested minimum elevation. Good.
However saved files on disk record the whole pass from AOS to LOS, not only when elevation is above minimum.
When using omnidirectional antenna such as a patch the signal SNR is not optimum due to low gain and satellite slant range. 
In order to reduce size of recorded files without interesting signal could it be possible to only save part of the pass over selected minimal elevation ?
Here is a complete record displayed with Audacity, showing the part with stronger signal.
![Capture d’écran du 2024-01-13 11-44-45](https://github.com/SatDump/SatDump/assets/14106669/edbfce35-f1e1-48cb-87cc-94e0957b8b28)

Title: SatDump hangs whole system
Body:
**Description of the issue**
When I open satdump-ui, whole system becomes unresponsive. Sometimes the mouse stops moving and for some reason the Caps Lock key light starts flashing on and off, sometimes it doesn't hang immediately, but I can't kill satdump-ui using `pkill -9` and cannot restart the computer. 
**Hardware (SDR/PC/OS)**
Arch Linux, Hyprland (Wayland)
Laptop with AMD Ryzen 5 5500U
AMDGPU open source for graphics driver.

Built from source, commit 21a11f86e545c3b7aeca75d6519830d04745a52e

Title: Suggestion to add satellite name to recorded file name
Body:
I started to play with "Schedule and Config" option for automatic recording of different SARSAT 
satellite beacons. This is a very convenient process.

Presently the output name of saved files are of the form : 

2024-01-11_19-43-10_1024000SPS_1544500000Hz.wav

Although theses file names are already convenient to recall date, frequency and sample rate, this format lack satellite ID  that would help recovering the origin of signal recorded.

As different satellites telemetry may be captured I suggest to add satellite names in front, such as for example :  

NOAA-19-2024-01-11_19-43-10_1024000SPS_1544500000Hz.wav
or
METEOR-M2-3-2024-01-08_20-04-40_1024000SPS_1544500000Hz.wav

Consequently saved files objects would be a posteriori identified more easily.

What do you thinkabout it  ?

Title: Frequencies errors when setting manual Sample rate and LO offset
Body:
Let me explain my testing procedure.
I use an HF signal sent by a TS-790 transceiver tuned to 1296.100 MHz.
Satdump-ui is used for listening the test signal received by a RSPduo synchronized by GPS.

I selected 1296 MHz as receiving frequency and read the frequency under the mouse cursor.

Choosing any preselected samplerate gives correct absolute and relative frequencies reading.

If I enter a manual sample frequency equal or superior to 1.024 Msps absolute and relative frequencies reading are correct.

However entering manually a sample frequency under 900 Ksps gives wrong frequencies reading except for 250 Ksps.
For example choosing 480 Ksps lead to wrong frequency readings at 1296.19 MHz and 188.95 KHz. 

When introducing a LO offset things are getting bad.
First, when choosing pre selected 1.024 Msps, frequency is correctly displayed at 1.2961 GHz and offset 100 KHz is also correctly displayed when mouse cursor is over the peak on the spectrum.

However after introducing a 0.10000 LO offset the frequency is still correct but the peak offset at 1.2961 is now wrongly displayed 200 KHz, not 100 KHz.

The same for samplerates over 1024.
Strangely, with 250 Ksps samplerate entered manually the offset reading is back to a correct value of 100 KHz...

Selecting manual samplerate gives approximately the same results down to 960 Ksps frequency where offset is read 200 KHz.

These errors not only affects frequencies reading but also saved files frequencies.
This introduces unrecoverable issues when ones try to synchronize on telemetry beacon frequency.

Title: [Feature] Separate Num Avgs for FFT and Waterfall
Body:
Under Recorder > FFT, for usability sake I recommend having two different "Avg num", because the waterfall will show more signal features if the number of avgs is super low, like 1-5, but then that makes the PSD go nuts, I personally like to use 100 avgs for the FFT (at least when I'm at a high sample rate).  And by having a low default (e.g. 5) for the waterfall side, folks who are newer to RF who don't realize what they are missing out on will be taken care of =)

Title: Automatic image rotation for POES sattelite images
Body:
**What would you want to be added to satdump?**
Hello Satdump Team, I think a automatic image rotation feature for images from polar earth orbiting satellites like the NOAAs or Meteors could be a useful addition.

**Describe the feature you'd like to have**
Functionality that automatically rotates the generated images based on the direction of the current overflight of the satellite of interest.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Manually rotating all the images if necessary.

**Additional context**
I think even a very simple algorithm could do this job pretty reliably: The algorithm calculates the Latitude of the satellite of interest at its AOS and at its LOS (this can be done with the celestrak-data). If the Latitude of the satellite has decreased during the time-span between AOS and LOS, its a north-to-south overflight, no rotation needed, but if the opposite is the case, the overflight went from south to north, thus the image has to be rotated.

Thanks 😄
