Title: Option to automatically generate all possible enhancement images
Body:
Hey SatDump Team, I really like the newest additions to the NOAA APT decoder, with the decoding of the telemetry data now possible, the data is now so much richer of information, especially the temperature data, I love it!

When I use the the recorder to automatically track, record  and decode, I normally get the raw image, the separated A and B channel images and also (when the telemetry data is kinda clean of noise) the separated channels 1-5, depending on what the satellite is currently transmitting.
Now, as far as I understand it, the current nightly build now supports only the enhancements (in the viewer tab) that are possible with the data from the available channels, which is a nice step forward, so you don't have to click on the enhancement to only see that they are not compatible with the current dataset.

Now, currently, my workflow after the images are automatically processed and saved consists of going into the viewer tab, enabling the map overlay to show borders and capital cities on the map, and then generating and saving all the enhancement images possible with the available channel data.

**What would you want to be added to satdump?**
An option to enable automatic generation of all enhancements possible with the channels of the currently tracked and processed satellite would be really cool. I think that the offline processor of NOAA APT data does this already (with some enhancements, but not all?), but not the live tracking processor.
Also, maybe an option to automatically generate all these images, but with map overlay, would be really useful.

**Describe the feature you'd like to have**
An option in the settings tab to enable automatic generation of all image enhancements possible with the channels of the currently live tracked satellite and an option to enable generation of a additional version of all these images, but now with additional border and Lat/Lon grid overlay.

I think this not only is relevant to NOAA APT images, but could be an option also useful for all other satellites that produce images where image enhancements are applicable.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Doing all these steps manually.

Thanks 😀

Title: Introduce processing modulation choice (BPSK, QPSK, etc...)
Body:
Presently satdump-ui Processing menu offers a choice with a number of satellites.
If by no chance the satellite we are receiving is not in the list how could we process transmitted data ?

I am using remote satdump_sdr_server and local satdump-ui.
Setup is a simple patch 1.5 MHz antenna on which I replaced the preamplifier with a more efficient Nooelec SawBird. A second Nooelec 1.542 MHz Amplifier is attached to RTL-SRD.COM V3 bias-T on PC running satdump_sdr_server. With this setup I am receiving Galileo and GLONASS MEOSAR SARSAT transponders with LNA gain set to only 30 to get same noise level as with AGC. LEOSAR satellites (NOAA, METEOR, METOP) have very strong signals due to low orbits around 800 Km.

My ultimate goal would be to decode SARSAT (Search And Rescue SATellite) telemetry for emergency calls.
LEOSAR transponders retransmit 406 MHz devices emergency beacons. A 2400 bauds beacon is multiplexing the received beacons with other data. 
Satellite transmission centered on 1544.5 MHz is modulated linearly in phase. The Processed Data Stream (PDS) beacon is coded in Biphase NRZ-L at 2400 bauds with a modulation index of 0.39 (SARR-1) and 0.35 (SARR-2).

I would appreciate to have the possibility to select in a sub processing menu not the satellite but rather the modulation type.
This would be a first step toward decoding beacons the format of which is published and available publicly.
https://radiotelescopelavillette.wordpress.com/balises-406-mhz/

73 de Bernard, f6bvp /ai7bg

Title: Remote Gui Application Control
Body:
**What would you want to be added to satdump?**
I would like remote GUI application control to be added. 

**Describe the feature you'd like to have**
For example, when using my 3D printer I use Octoprint to control the printer from my computer. The 3D Printer is in garage, Computer is inside house. I use Raspberry Pi with [Octoprint](https://octoprint.org/) connected to printer, to allow upload, download, control, move axis, so on. It would be cool if Satdump could somehow implement similar system.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I consider using [VNC Server](https://ubuntu.com/tutorials/access-remote-desktop#1-overview) with Ubuntu on Raspberry Pi so I can have remote desktop. Then also having [folder sync to google drive](https://www.insynchq.com/i/google-drive/linux/ubuntu) using insync. But more complicated & fragmented setup for all users, also remote desktop i think uses lots of cpu of raspberry pi which will be needed for satdump processing perhaps, I don't know exactly because haven't tried yet. 

Goal would be to have automated rotator and sat file sync to cloud so can access end image from smartphone or laptop anywhere in world or share on social media platform such as twitter/instagram/etc

**Additional context**
At present, I use Satdump with my rotator and automatic tracking, i like it very much. However, If I could mount a raspberry pi to the rotator, connect sdr, remote access control GUI web interface, and captured image could be uploaded to cloud drive. This way rotator could be left alone forever. Also adding spyserver type control for global access.

Thanks for reading :)

Title: SatDump AppImage
Body:
**What would you want to be added to satdump?**
Generic AppImage binary for Linux instead of just Ubuntu/Debian package.

**Describe the feature you'd like to have**
AppImage is a well known Linux distribution format that allows easy and beginner friendly distribution of software that can work on various Linux systems. Please, consider distributing SatDump for Linux as an [AppImage](https://appimage.org).

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Possible alternative could be a [Flatpak](https://flatpak.org). Both of these formats have their upsides and downsides, but I think that AppImage is probably more suitable for this project, it is more generic and can be distributed as a direct download link just like the current *.deb package.

**Additional context**
Adding AppImage builds for SatDump should not be very hard, there already are workflows available for this that can be used directly with GitHub. See for example how another SDR-related project called Gqrx handles this:
https://github.com/gqrx-sdr/gqrx/blob/master/.github/workflows/build.yml
https://github.com/gqrx-sdr/gqrx/blob/master/appimage.sh

Just one imperfection :-(

Testing the app and looking at all documentation, I couldn't find this --- Is there a way to make the app send one last final POST before stopping logger? It would be even better if one of the params in %ALL indicated that we are stopping now (e.g. `stop=1` or `%STOP`)? This should happen regardless of any time or distance minimum limits. Title: Custom URL: How to identify a specific phone by parameters
Body:
Hi folks,
Thank you so much for providing and maintaining this repository.

I stumbled over it and could straight setup the ```Custom URL``` use case. Kudos!

I did manage to receive the HTTP request at a test server.

Here are my settings.

```
# performance
## log gps/gnss locations
Checked

## log network locations
Checked

## log passive locations
Unchecked

## logging interval
60s

## keep gps on between fixes
Unchecked

Checked

## allow auto sending
Unchecked

## discard offline locations
Checked

## Url
https://<host>/log?lat=%LAT&lon=%LON&time=%TIME&s=%SPD&id=%AID&ser=%SER

POST
```

My test server is receiving data from the phone successfully. For instance, refer to this log entry.

Title: Logging stops
Body:
Hi there,
First of all thank you for this very nice app which I've been using for many years now. I recently switched my old phone for a OnePlus 8T but since then I've experienced problems with GPSLogger stopping logging once the screen locks. Basically the same problem as https://github.com/mendhak/gpslogger/issues/1051 and https://github.com/mendhak/gpslogger/issues/916.

System
* OnePlus 8T, KB2003 with OxygenOS 13.0
* Android security update 5 March 2023
* Build number KB2003_11_F.63
* GPSLogger 126 (F-Droid)

Config
* GPSLogger is locked on the apps switcher as suggested on https://dontkillmyapp.com/oneplus?app=GPSLogger under App locking
* Android settings - Battery - More settings - App battery management - GPSLogger - Allow background activity is on
* Android settings - Battery - More settings - App battery management - GPSLogger - Allow auto launch is on
* Android settings - Battery - More settings - Optimize battery use - GPSLogger is set to "don't optimize"
* GPSLogger has the following permissions: access precise location in foreground, access approximate location in foreground, access location in background, read files from shared storage, show notifications, run at startup, have full network access, view network connections, ask to ignore battery optimizations, run foreground service
* GPSLogger settings - Log passive locations: tried both on and off, made no difference
* GPSLogger settings - Hide notification from lock screen is off

Analysis
Debug file is in the attachment.
It seems that GPSLogger is being killed by Android (line 104), but I've got no clue why.

The strange thing is that another GPS logging app, μlogger (https://github.com/bfabiszewski/ulogger-android) works fine on the very same phone. I prefer the user experience and configurability of GPSLogger, though.

Maybe the following is relevant. I noted that GPSLogger does not seem to request the "notification on lock screen" permission even though the "hide notification from lock screen" setting is off in GPSLogger. GPSLogger simply is not listed under Android settings - Notifications & Status bar - Lock screen. μlogger is listed there but strange enough doesn't show a notification on the lock screen during logging. There is an icon from μlogger on the screensaver, though.

Any ideas?
[debug.txt](https://github.com/mendhak/gpslogger/files/11242939/debug.txt)

Title: Improve offline locations logging
Body:
I was so happy when the option to not discard offline locations was added, it was the only thing that kept me using Traccar Client but I have some concerns on the way it works.

I think that checking if the network is available is not the right thing to check to add a location to the queue because it makes points disappear in many situations:

- If the server we log to is down 
- If the network is unstable so the request fails (which is often the case when going hiking or when in the subway)

I think that the only thing that should indicate if a point can be "forgotten" is if the request is successful (or event better, if we can validate that it has returned a HTTP status code we choose in the app)

Right now the functionality is here but I can't use it in my daily life, I'm losing too much data

Title: Better Default Data Source
Body:
I don't know if this is possible, but I think it would be useful for new app users and new EV owners if EVMap picked a better default data source.

Some people are in a hurry, some just click "next" and "accept" on every button they see, some find the change to an electric car to be overwhelming and data sources are yet another thing they have to learn... and while none of this is the app's fault, better defaults always help.

### Background:

Recently a family member had to go on a trip cross country and back on an electric car. He's neither the most tech savvy person or experienced with electric cars (although he gets the basics). He installed some random app from Google Play which showed him some chargers... but apparently their data wasn't very complete and he was using slower chargers and getting stressed about getting to the next charger.

Someone suggested asking me as I "know about these things". I use and contribute to OpenChargeMap, so I knew data was good. I suggested EVMap as it's clean and fast, but I forgot to tell him to use OCM as data source... a few minutes later he tells me that there's even less chargers showing. I reinstall EVMap, realise the problem, tell him how to change from GoingElectric.de to OCM, and problem fixed. He starts seeing fast chargers, the trip goes much smoothly, all goes well.

We both failed here. I didn't tell him to select OCM because I had forgotten EVMap's first steps (I set up it once and that was it). In his hurry and stress, he clearly didn't read the description of each data source map and was just trying to get to the map.

### Ideas:

I'm completely out of my depth here, but maybe it can be helpful: 

- I was going to suggest using Open Charge Map as the default option as that seems to be the best option for most of the world, but in places like Germany/central Europe GoingElectric.de might be a better choice? Doing this by itself might not be ideal for every user.

- Can the default source change depending on the device's language? Also not perfect, but assuming most people use their devices in their own language, could OCM be selected by default and GoingElectric selected if the language is German, Dutch, etc?

- Using the user's location probably isn't the best idea because the app doesn't ask for it right away, it would require more steps before using the app, and the user might not want to give permission. Even if they do, location services don't always work.

- Forcing the user to manually pick a source before showing the map could work. Highlight the options if the user tries to "continue" without selecting it. This adds some friction to the setup process though.

- IP based? Would require an internet connection, maybe a server, privacy policies, fairly accurate IP databases... this adds cost and maybe too complicated?

Is there a better way to do this? Probably. I'm out of ideas though.

### Final words:

Just to be clear, this it not a problem with EVMap (it works well as it is) or even GoingElectric (their data seems to be very good in central Europe), but as they say, "defaults matter" and OCM seems to be the best option of the two for a lot of the world. Or at least it seems that way to me.

Maybe it's something that could be changed/improved.

Keep up the good work, Johan!

Title: Implement OpenStreetMap data source
Body:
as discussed in #97, this adds support for OpenStreetMap as a data source for charging stations.

TODO:
- [x] implement filters
    - [x] basic filters: free parking/charging, open 24/7, min power, connectors, number of connectors
    - [x] filter by operator or network (requires a `ReferenceData` implementation which saves the list of possible choices)
- [x] make sure that we don't consume too much RAM when importing the data (currently, they are first fully loaded into memory and only then stored in the database - instead we could do this in chunks)
- [x] Add better progress indicator while initially importing the data?
    - [x] phone UI
    - [x] Android Auto / AAOS
- [ ] Implement a mechanism to manually or automatically (e.g. once per week) update the data
- [x] Try to make real-time data work (should work for chargers where OSM has data about connectors and powers)
- [ ] Ask Chargeprice whether they can add support for OSM (currently [their API](https://github.com/chargeprice/chargeprice-api-docs/blob/master/api/v1/charge_prices/index.md) only supports OCM and GE - adding OSM support would probably be quite a bit of work for them, especially as OSM data may include different spellings of the same charger network/operator)
- [x] improve representation of missing values in the UI (e.g. currently when the charging power is missing, "null" is shown - and if no details about connectors are available, the screen is pretty much empty)
    - [x] phone UI
    - [x] Android Auto / AAOS
- [x] Move to production webserver
- [ ] Update description of OSM in the data source selection screen
- [ ] Implement opening hours parsing (for this we need to replace the current EVMap OpeningHours structure with the more flexible OSM format, see https://github.com/simonpoole/OpeningHoursParser)
- [ ] Check out if we can get any more details from the OSM data, such as
  - [x] the address?
  - [x] [links to photos](https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Photo_linking) (not sure if this is being used for chargers so far?)
  - [ ] [EVSEID](https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Tag:amenity%3Dcharging_station) (may be useful for better realtime data)
  - [x] [Charger website](https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:website)

Title: Info: Renault AAOS VHAL does weird stuff.
Body:
This is strange behaviour I encountered while a tester used my app on his Megane E-Tech.

Renault seems to have a broken implementation of the Vehicle Hardware Abstraction Layer, causing it to report implausible values to apps using it. In case of EVMap this affects die displayed state of charge in the vehicle infos:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/12702685/219458532-20ceff5b-7a19-41e4-bd8b-e9fa5ad7aa6e.png)

The SoC did not change at all. The logs made by my app tester revealed the following:

```
16.02.2023 03:28:15.755: Display distance unit: 35
16.02.2023 03:28:15.755: Car name: BCB, Renault, 2021
16.02.2023 03:28:15.755: Max battery Capacity: 6553500.0 // (0xffff * 100).toFloat() ???
// ...
16.02.2023 03:28:15.915: Additional data:
    Speed in m/s:        0.0
    Power in mW:         17857.145 // 18W while charging with 11kW
    Gear selection:      4
    Connection status:   true
    Battery level in Wh: 34700.0
    Ignition state:      3
    Ambient temperature: 11.0
// ...
16.02.2023 03:30:54.113: DRIVE STATE: CHARGE -> PARKED
// ...
16.02.2023 03:30:56.469: Additional data:
    Speed in m/s:        0.0
    Power in mW:         Infinity // (confused unga bunga)
    Gear selection:      4
    Connection status:   false
    Battery level in Wh: 35000.0
    Ignition state:      3
    Ambient temperature: 11.0
```

There is nothing that can be done inside of an app to fix what ever this c*** is, except for maybe setting a fixed battery capacity value.

Since this is nothing that hurts EVMap's primary functions this isn't a big issue (while tt is for my own developement 🙃). This is just meant to be a little side note if anyone driving a Renault complains about this.

Title: AAOS: add additional vehicle data through CarPropertyManager
Body:
On Android Automotive OS, [`CarPropertyManager`](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/car/hardware/property/CarPropertyManager) gives access to more vehicle data than the [`CarInfo`](https://developer.android.com/reference/androidx/car/app/hardware/info/CarInfo) API we are currently using.
For example, [`EV_BATTERY_INSTANTANEOUS_CHARGE_RATE`](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/car/VehiclePropertyIds#EV_BATTERY_INSTANTANEOUS_CHARGE_RATE) gives the current consumption or charging rate, which we could add in the vehicle data screen.

However, this can only be implemented on AAOS and we still need to use `CarInfo` for compatibility with Android Auto.

see also: https://polestar.fans/t/car-stats-viewer-bordcomputer-in-besser/14091
Cc @Ixam97.

Title: Realtime data: Reject matching if candidate charger is closer to another known charging site
Body:
For this charging station, EVMap is currently showing realtime data that actually belongs to a different station a bit less than 150 meters away: https://www.goingelectric.de/stromtankstellen/Deutschland/Ruesselsheim-am-Main/Karolingerring-Karolingerring-51A/68190/

This is because this station is missing in the EnBW data, and the other station has the same combination of plugs and is close enough that the matching algorithm still considers it as valid.

As the other station also exists in GoingElectric, we could pass the AvailabilityDetector algorithm a list of nearby charging sites and use that list to reject candidate chargers from the realtime data source that are close enough to the site we are looking for, but actually closer to a different site.

To make sure that the list of nearby charging sites passed to the algorithm is from a large enough radius, we would need to make sure that the bounding box in which we load chargers is never smaller than the matching radius of the AvailabilityDetector.

Title: Show a list of nearby chargers with the lowest charging cost
Body:
It would be great if EVMap could show a list of nearby chargers that also shows the charging cost. That list should find nearby chargers and get the cheapest charging tariff for each charger.
For price calculation the already existing charging cost settings should be used. Tariffs should be limited to those configured as owned by the user ("My charging plans").

Possible settings for that list:
- Search radius
- Selection of a filter (same filters as on the map)
- Sort order (either by price or by distance)

I think that list should show 5-10 chargers. Or maybe also make that available as a setting.

This would require a new page, similar to the list of favorites, but with additional information fields for:
- Price
- Charging tariff
- Charging duration and/or average charging power

Optional enhancements to this feature:
- Maybe the already existing list of nearby chargers on the home screen of Android Auto/Automotive could be extended with the price information? At least with the charging price, if there is not enough space for everything. (But I think there should still be a seperate page on Android Auto/Automotive with the full list of informations, just like on the phone.)
- This could optionally be combined with #143 to show the cheapest chargers along the route or at the destination.

Title: geoid height correction is missing
Body:
As far as I can see height data is systematically wrong.
In my location I get heights which are about 40 m too high for example but as the geoid is different on each point of the earth it might be a totally different offset at your location.

This is probably due to the fact that geoid correction is missing.
See for example this thread: https://github.com/Akaflieg-Freiburg/enroute/issues/28

A simple example to do pretty good geoid correction is at [geoid.py](https://gist.github.com/pyRobShrk/98c43c25d4d3c5d105e90fa0b58cf823) which uses the [EGM96](https://earth-info.nga.mil/GandG/wgs84/gravitymod/egm96/binary/binarygeoid.html) gridded data [WW15MGH.DAC](https://earth-info.nga.mil/GandG/wgs84/gravitymod/egm96/binary/WW15MGH.DAC) and interpolates with splines. Btw. this file and method is for example also used for the geoid correction [osmand](https://github.com/osmandapp/OsmAnd).

Title: Feature Request: interval for live synchronization
Body:
Hi,

I am using ulogger with a custom URL (phonetrack). I'd appreciate functionality to have the live synchronization have a different interval than the minimum time.

Let me give an example:
- set the minimum time to 5s
- set the (not implemented) synchronization intervall to 10 minutes

The device then would only connect to the internet every 10 minutes and send 10 * 60 / 5 logging entries.

This functionality would help save battery life (because it would not send as often as the minimum time) but would allow for keeping detailed accuracy.

I hope this makes sense and I did not misunderstand how the live synchronization currently works.

Great work and thanks!

Title: Handle Instagram links with query string
Body:
Currently Instagram links in these formats are handled:
- `openstreetmap`
- `instagram/openstreetmap`
- `instagram.com/openstreetmap`
- https://www.instagram.com/openstreetmap
- https://www.instagram.com/openstreetmap/

But Instagram links can also come in these formats:
- https://www.instagram.com/openstreetmap?hl=it
- https://www.instagram.com/openstreetmap?igsh=ODA1NTc5OTg5Nw==
- https://www.instagram.com/openstreetmap/?hl=it
- https://www.instagram.com/openstreetmap/?igsh=ODA1NTc5OTg5Nw==

These should be handled as well.

I think this can be achieved by editing the regex in [InstagramProvider](https://github.com/Zverik/every_door/blob/6e55dea2c2a5c41c43082811b68ac326b822069b/lib/fields/helpers/website_fmt.dart#L178):

```diff
- regexp: RegExp(r'(?:instagram(?:\.com)?/)?([^/ ]+)/?$'),
+ regexp: RegExp(r'(?:instagram(?:\.com)?/)?([^/ ?]+)/?(\?[\w=]+)?$'),
```

I compared the regular expressions in regex101:

Before:
![image](https://github.com/Zverik/every_door/assets/8406735/c38eebc7-7404-4dca-bdba-90d543d7f5c1)

After:
![image](https://github.com/Zverik/every_door/assets/8406735/d92c09c3-3a74-404e-87c4-ade75da82269)

Title: addr:neighbourhood and addr:suburb
Body:
In addition to [addr:block](https://github.com/Zverik/every_door/issues/455#issuecomment-1763369436), it is very common for addresses in India to use components which (based on community feedback) are best tagged using `addr:neighbourhood` and `addr:suburb`.

For example -
A-86, Gali No.1, Rajeev Nagar, Bhalaswa, Delhi
addr:block=A
addr:housenumber=86
addr:street=Gali Number 1
addr:neighbourhood=Rajeev Nagar
addr:suburb=Bhalaswa
addr:city=Delhi

Using the raw tags editor for these fields requires users to know the OSM tags to use, which defeats the purpose of using EveryDoor.

Please add support for `addr:neighbourhood` and `addr:suburb`. Thank you.

Title: Smart capitalisation in 4.0 ?
Body:
I see in release notes that smart capitalisation  has been implemented in https://github.com/Zverik/every_door/issues/133, but I'm unable to discern where/how is it used.

I was guessing that if I want to add new cafe for example, and when entering it's name the text field would use ["Sentance case"](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Letter_case#Sentence_case) (i.e. only first letter of _first word_ forced uppercase when entering it in `name` text field) or _"Word case"_ (which I guess would likely make uppercase every first letter of _every word_? AKA [Start case](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Letter_case#Start_case))

- However, that does not seem to work. E.g. in area with several amenities with `name` field, I try to add new `amenity=cafe`, and typing its name in text field it is entered in **all lowercase**. Saving the cafe, it is also shown in list in all lowercase. Does that mean the smart capitalization did not work at all? Or would the case be mangled only silently (behind user back) on upload (which would be quite bad choice IMHO)? 

  Here is example video, what am I doing wrong? (There are more then 3 named cafes around)

  https://github.com/Zverik/every_door/assets/156656/fad8e25c-5c19-42d3-8100-a251e7dc011f

- if "word case" means what I've outlined above (i.e. [Start case](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Letter_case#Start_case)), is that good choice; i.e. is it actually commonly used somewhere in the world? I know USA and several other countries commonly use [Title Case](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Title_case), but that is not really making **every** word start with uppercase letter, just majority of them. (Or is it supposed to be just a simpler approximation of Title Case?) 

- trying to guess whether the country uses _Title Case_ or _Sentence case_ based on nearby names is likely problematic. See https://github.com/streetcomplete/StreetComplete/issues/4784#issuecomment-1515381979 and rest of that issue for background. In short, many countries which use _Sentence case_ will often have names of people/places in names of amenities, and personal names will also always be uppercased even in _Sentance case_ (e.g. existence of several nearby cafes named like _"Cafe Nikola Tesla"_, _"Cafe Albert Einstein"_, _"Cafe Lev Nikolayevich Tolstoy"_  does not imply that nearby _"Small bear cafe"_ cafe should be spelled "Small Bear Cafe")

- I've tried looking at `Settings` / `About Every Door 4.0` / `System log` after entering the cafe, but it is completely empty. I see some `_logger.fine(xxxxx)`  in that commit https://github.com/Zverik/every_door/commit/3d3c7139cf9106bd4bb8bd4fd1968a7f79793f0e, so I hoped I should see something in log? Should I see anything there, or is that another issue to report? Note that I have not uploaded my fake/test cafes from video if that matters.

I'm using  F-droid release of EveryDoor 4.0 on Android 10 (EMUI 12)

Title: Way to replace an address node with an amenity
Body:
In some countries addresses reference entrances. So when an entrance is for a shop or amenity, it seems natural to not create a second node, but to replace the entrance address node with the shop, adding the address to it.

Currently one can create an amenity (tagging the address) and then delete the nearby address node, but that means the location of the address would be altered. One can also edit the address node and change its type to an amenity, but this feels weird, and possibly the address might be reset.

See [this changesets and surrounding area](https://www.openstreetmap.org/changeset/141816144#map=19/52.34470/4.89104) for an example.

Related to #616: a shop may take up an entire building, and in this case tags might better go on the building contour.

Feels like it should be a checkbox somewhere — maybe between regular and extended field lists, and only for amenities.

Title: All amenities in this building mode

Title: Increase size of the POI map
Body:
I know this has been mentioned before (#170), but I want to bring it up to discuss my thoughts on the matter and get some feedback from other users.

According to the FAQ, the map is tiny because it's not the point of the app. However, I have some thoughts about that.

- I have a small phone, and the tiny map is probably extra-tiny for me (see screenshot below). I only have access to the Filter and Locate buttons. When I click Locate, sometimes the map zooms out pretty far (I assume to include the closest points? but it's not clear). Even zoomed out, I usually cannot see most of the points that are in the list. So I either have to zoom in for better context, or out to see the other points.
- I cannot easily zoom in, especially one-handed. There is not enough screen space to double-tap-hold-drag to zoom reliably, so I usually move the map. The zoom buttons don't show up because there isn't enough space, and per the first point, I often have to zoom in or out.
- While the point of the app is to map what you see, the POI list includes things close enough to see (across a parking lot, for instance), but doesn't always show them on the map.
- I often walk past a POI, or it's on the other side of a street, and then move the map to add it. With such small screen space, it's hard to zoom/pan/move at all.
- The other mapping modes all have bigger maps, and those work great.

I would love it if the map stayed the same size regardless of distance panned from myself. I think the ~50% height for the map and for the list is completely functional, and I have no downsides to it. Never have I thought that I needed to see more items in the list, and it's scrollable anyway (although maybe some visual indicator to show this might be useful for new users).

Could this maybe be a setting to allow users to choose?

Please chime in with thoughts. I don't know if I'm alone in this simply because my phone is smaller.
![image](https://github.com/Zverik/every_door/assets/7786285/88e6b0c4-4290-4fa6-9942-9309f3b80840)

Title: Duplicate selection list entries for waypoints with non-unique names
Body:
XCSoar versions having and not having the problem
-------------------------------------------------
7.40, but I have noticed it on earlier versions. I don't know what version it started on.


System information
------------------
Both Android & Windows
![Screenshot 2023-12-07 122035](https://github.com/XCSoar/XCSoar/assets/97711900/5d48f147-6091-402a-979d-71d0a80b49f1)

Steps to reproduce the behavior
-------------------------------
Nav
WaypointList
In the Name field type KAI and click OK


Expected behavior
-----------------
Each waypoint will appear once in the list.


Actual behavior
---------------
Some waypoints appear twice, as per the screenshot attached. There are 2 VRPs named "Kaituna Br", and each appears twice.


Do you have any idea what may have caused this?
-----------------------------------------------
I suspect that it's related to having two waypoints with the same name. Unfortunately, the NZ official list of VRPs at https://www.aip.net.nz/assets/AIP/Air-Navigation-Register/1-Permanent-Airspace/1_06_NZANR_Part_71_Visual_Reporting_Points_VRP.pdf contains a duplicate "Kaituna Bridge". In addition there is a VRP named "Feilding" and an airfield named "Feilding". 


Do you have an idea how to solve the issue?
-------------------------------------------
I guess that somehow duplicated entries are **each** processed twice.

As a workaround, I have manually edited the waypoints.cup file so that the Kaituna Bridge and Feilding waypoint names are unique, and the problem is no longer visible.

As an experiment, I generated a waypoints.cup file with 2 of each of the "Kaituna Bridge" waypoints and loaded it into XCSoar. This resulted in 8 entries being shown for "Kaituna Bridge" in the selection list.

[waypoints.zip](https://github.com/XCSoar/XCSoar/files/13592655/waypoints.zip)

Body:
XCSoar version
--------------
XCSOAR 7.40 - git c24da2fb07

What should XCSoar do differently, what functionality should be added?
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Kobo CLARA 2E has a bluetooth chip. It could be helpfull to send audio over ble to have audio vario on an external speaker.


What are you trying to do, what is the use case for the suggestion?
-------------------------------------------------------------------
I succeed in playing a wav file audio over bluetooth with alsa using bluealsa-plugin already inlcuded in original Clara 2E rootfs.
XCSoar is using salsa lib which mention "alsa-lib plugins and configurations are completely dropped". The ble plugin is thus not usable.

As XCSoar is compiled with static, we could maybe use alsa instead of salsa. However, the plugin architecture mentions "the external plugins are implemented in a shared object". This solution is thus non trivial.

Could it be possible to have some dynamic link library of XCSoar (breaking its static behaviour)
Any other suggestion to implement it ?

Title: New Feature - Arrival Time Ring (ATR)
Body:
<!--

Welcome! - We kindly ask that you:

  1. Fill out the issue template below - not doing so needs a good reason.
  2. Use the forum if you have a question rather than a bug or feature request.

The forum is at: https://forum.xcsoar.org

Thanks for understanding, and for contributing to the project!

-->


XCSoar version
--------------

<!--
Which XCSoar version are you using?
-->

Irrespective of version, but 7.x latest

What should XCSoar do differently, what functionality should be added?
----------------------------------------------------------------------

<!--
Please describe the feature you'd like us to add/change.
-->

Add a new feature called Arrival Time Ring (ATR). 
  
  Similar to a fuel range ring found on powered aircraft.....
![image](https://github.com/XCSoar/XCSoar/assets/71841979/2bf69fcd-9742-4141-b1c6-2ad40a64b31f)

The Arrival Time Ring (ATR) would be a ring centered around your current goto waypoint, or task finish. The ATR ring would be specified for an arrival time (say 5pm). If you are inside the ring you would finish before 5pm and if you are outside of the ring you would finish after 5pm. The radius of the circle would decrease as you got closer to 5pm (in this example).

Radius of the circle (d) would be V_ete * T_remain
where V_ete is the input to the ETE info box and T_remain is the diff between now and the specified arrival time.

Radius would be a simple circle, nothing fancy. No value is gained by modulating the circle with wind or terrain. V_ete already probably accounts for wind.
Update rate can be lower as this does not need high fidelity.


The ring would have some text on its radius to inform the user what airport and what time it is indicating.
There would be a config setting to set the arrival time and also enable/disable.


What are you trying to do, what is the use case for the suggestion?
-------------------------------------------------------------------
If the forecast says the lift will die at 5pm and you want to be home by 5pm this is a graphical reminder that you need to be inside of this ring to finish or be home by 5pm. While flying an out/return task I constantly find myself calculating the return time, what time is it now, how much further I can go before I can turn back and still finish before the thermals quit. I would use this feature every flight. 
For task flying this would be a good graphical indication that you can make that last turnpoint before heading for home, or not, or how far to fly into the last AAT circle, and still make it home before thermals quit.

There would also be another ring for sunset.


<!--
Please describe briefly what you're trying to do, that would be possible if
the suggested feature was implemented.
-->

Title: Better flarm colours
Body:
This PR shows FLARM targets in different colors according to their FLARM rate. 30s climb rates are compared to the largest between 30s average or set MC. 

Red = climbing well (better than your 30s average/ MC)
Yellow = Climbing ok (worse than your 30s average/ MC but still climbing)
Blue = not climbing

light colours = >50 above
normal colours = same level
dark colours = >50m below

Potentially too confusing for most users, but also very powerful for comp pilots. I have been flying with it for a few comps, the main advantages are: 

1) Bubble awareness -> Are gliders climbing better above or below? should you wait for that next pulse to hit?
2) spread of gliders -> who's climbing the best? are you in the best bit? who is actually climbing vs. circling? See if gaggles are centered, and guess where the core is before even getting there.
3) Next thermal -> are they out-climbing you? will you connect when you get there (are they at your level)?
4) Final glide -> Are gliders ahead pulling up in lift?

Title: Driver for LX Navigation Eos57
Body:
<!--

Thank you for your interest in contributing to XCSoar! Please read the
following information to make it easier for us to review your changes.

We appreciate if you make sure to:

  - Document the changes (in the NEWS.txt file, and the manual when relevant)
  - Enable maintainer edits[1] (in case we need to help with the PR)
  - Check your commits and their messages (so they're all tidy and coherent)

[1] https://help.github.com/en/github/collaborating-with-issues-and-pull-requests/allowing-changes-to-a-pull-request-branch-created-from-a-fork

-->


Brief summary of the changes
----------------------------
Driver for LX Navigation Eos57 variometer.

Features
----------------------------
- Receiving NMEA data, correctly interpreting altitude
- Task declaration, including observation zones shape
- Flight log download
- MacCready and bugs and ballast synchronization

Compatibility
----------------------------
- Tested on Eos57
- Might work well with Era and Eos80 too. That is not tested, and it is possible that some features will not work properly. Anyway, it will most likely work better than the LXNAV driver. 

Known issues
----------------------------
- Declaration does not set the Landing entry in the task settings. This behavior is the same as with the LXNAV driver and is probably caused by a bug in Eos's firmware. It does not pose any problem for usage, as the Landing point doesn't have any practical usage.
- Ballast synchronization may not be precise. The value provided by vario is relative to polar reference mass. The reference mass of vario's polar (can be different from XCSoar's polar) cannot be directly obtained and is estimated from polar coefficients. 

<!--
Please note that the commit messages is where detailed descriptions of the
changes should be made - this section is just for a brief summary/overview.
-->


Related issues and discussions
------------------------------
New generation LX Navigation Device #389
Wrong altitude from Lx Eos variometer #969
ERA80-Xcsoar #415

Title: OGN Live Tracking
Body:
<!--

Welcome! - We kindly ask that you:

  1. Fill out the issue template below - not doing so needs a good reason.
  2. Use the forum if you have a question rather than a bug or feature request.

The forum is at: https://forum.xcsoar.org

Thanks for understanding, and for contributing to the project!

-->


XCSoar version
--------------
7.39

<!--
-->


What should XCSoar do differently, what functionality should be added?
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Add an OGN live tracking option in addition to Skylines and LiveTrack24 

<!--
-->


What are you trying to do, what is the use case for the suggestion?
-------------------------------------------------------------------
Continuously display OGN live tracking using store and forward communications with the OGN server via the mobile phone cellular network to achieve a complete flight trace. To achieve a single OGN trace when using your flarm, in addition to XCSoar OGN live tracking, XCSoar would need to use the same ICAO address as the flarm. There are significantly more mobile phone towers than OGN receivers which will ensure, in most cases, a continual live OGN flight trace. This would also improve the safety aspect of an out landing assuming you were at least in range of a mobile phone tower.

<!--
-->

What should XCSoar do differently, what functionality should be added?
----------------------------------------------------------------------

Adding the wind/Gust reading to the map derived from publicly available weather stations in the ground. 
XcTrack has made this feature possible with  simple work around as described and visually presented here:

https://www.pdcs.ch/aktuell/2020/07/24/xctrack-windstationen-anzeigen/

It would be extremely helpful if XcSoar would provide a similar feature. 
 

<!--
Please describe the feature you'd like us to add/change.
-->

To use the already available Weather report feature that is under the Info>Weather menu and allow it to be visible on the map similar to waypoints except with current wind/gust data visible next to them. 


What are you trying to do, what is the use case for the suggestion?
-------------------------------------------------------------------

To make landing safer and to avoid better prior planning for landing by knowing the wind conditions on the ground 


<!--
Please describe briefly what you're trying to do, that would be possible if
the suggested feature was implemented.
-->

To include free and near realtime realtime local weather stations basic data such as Wind and gust information we would be able to make better landing approaches or perhaps to seek alternative landing locations than originally planned for safety purposes. 

This feature is already available in XCtrack as the workaround has described by a Swiss pilot in the following link:

https://www.pdcs.ch/aktuell/2020/07/24/xctrack-windstationen-anzeigen/

Title: Instant wind visualization/calculation
Body:
XCSoar version 7.36
--------------

I'd like to have an instant wind estimate, respectively an appropriate visualisation in case it is present. Instant wind I define here in contrast to a synoptic wind, that does not change much during a xc flight. An instant wind sensor senses the wind change in a below one minute time frame. The exact latency of an instant wind sensor is related to the signal to noise ratio of available pressure, GNSS and IMU inputs. Know examples of such sensors are hawk, anemoi and Larus. The latter certainly with the most expensive gnss and imu hardware and thus with the smallest latency around 5sec ([stefly](https://www.stefly.aero/product/larus_glider_sensor/) u. [Larus](https://github.com/larus-breeze) ).

What are you trying to do, what is the use case for the suggestion?
-------------------------------------------------------------------
Visualize the instant wind estimate on the current XCS display for various tactical flight decisions.

Suggestion would be:
1. Same arrow as the current on circing around the glider symbol, but with a different color.
2. Arrow with a different radial position around the glider symbol.
3. Different graphical item/arrow

Steps envisioned to get there:
a) XCS should be able to read NMEA sentences that deliver instant wind and handle the info. I am currently enabling [XCV ](https://xcvario.de/)to convert the anemoi protocol to a standard NMEA 0182 sentences.
This first step will enable the more sophisticated second step.

b) Provide a software solution for the instant wind indication when an GNSS, IAS and AHRS source is connected to XCS. The calculation as said will depend on the quality of those sources and a first implementation will need some configuration parameter to handle inputs properly. The instant wind quality is expected to be inferior compared to a boxed solution with high quality seonsors, but still possible. The sources of the Larus Breeze project are available for integration as stated from the author and are GPL3.

Hope the merged feature request is now a bit more elaborate.

Title: NMEA line endings
Body:
<!--

Welcome! - We kindly ask that you:

  1. Fill out the issue template below - not doing so needs a good reason.
  2. Use the forum if you have a question rather than a bug or feature request.

The forum is at: https://forum.xcsoar.org

Thanks for understanding, and for contributing to the project!

-->


XCSoar version
--------------

7.34-Android


What should XCSoar do differently, what functionality should be added?
----------------------------------------------------------------------

More tolerant to line endings of NMEA

and/or

Visualize the line endngs in the monitor mode


What are you trying to do, what is the use case for the suggestion?
-------------------------------------------------------------------

NMEA has a specified line ending CR+LF, however, due to various auto-LF or auto-CR or other weird combinations of things those line ending can get mixed up, changed, corrupted, etc. - would it be worth to make the code more tolerant ?

As well, as those line ending are criticial to the operation, as an NMEA without proper CR+LF is dropped, it would be important to visualze it in the monitor mode, as a naive user can't figure out what's going on when he sees for example the PGRMZ on the monitor log but the XCsoar can't recognize this device as a Baro... and only a deep bnary dump helps here and so it would be so much easier if the monitor would simply print the line ending to make sure the user is aware of the possible issue.

Ideally, in the monitor mode, the NMEA with a wrong check and/or wrong line ending should be flags with a different color or something to attract attention and this is actually an issue critical to the operation. Many problems can be spotted far quicker this way, I believe.

Title: Impossible to download newest flight from LXNAV S8/S80/S10/S100 when more than 128 flights in logger
Body:
XCSoar versions having and not having the problem
-------------------------------------------------

All versions, probably

Expected behavior
-----------------

When trying to download flight from the logger,  the list should show all flights in the logger, or at least the most recent ones

Actual behavior
---------------

The list shows the 128 *oldest* flights, newer flights can not be downloaded.


Do you have any idea what may have caused this?
-----------------------------------------------

XCSoar puts an arbitrary limit for the download list at 128 entries

Do you have an idea how to solve the issue?
-------------------------------------------

Easiest: We just increase the number of entries to a value that is higher than the max number of stored flights (which is unfortunately unknown). I tested 512, and so far it works, but in a few years we might reach 512 and it might fail again. I did not find a way to artificially generate flights to test the limit without doing actual flights.

(See pull request for this quick solution)

Otherwise: Use a list without fixed length.

Better solution: Only download the *newest* N flights rather than the oldest. Not sure if the protocol allows that easily, I was not able to understand it in reasonably short time by reading the quite complicated download code.

Title: Terrain Display/generation (not Map raw Data) - big river Elbe missing
Body:
XCSoar versions having and not having the problem
-------------------------------------------------
For years, XCSoar had the Problem of not displaying an important visual navigation aid correctly. The river Elbe around Hamburg, Germany. I opened an issue back then and it was put in the "Map/Terrain" corner and got one answer - We've got bad/old terrain Data. The river is 50-100m wide and this cannot be put on bad raw Data. I lived with it because in the old maps, the part of the river west of Hamburg was missing - and I was flying in the east. Now, when I generate new terrain files, they switched. The part east of Hamburg is missing. And the westerly part of the river is there.

Expected behavior
-----------------
Showing the river as it should be an like it is in OSM or similar open sources.

Actual behavior
---------------
The River (blue) abbruptly stops at a point and only displays green terrain eastwards of that point.

Steps to reproduce the behavior
-------------------------------
I can load old terrain files to have the bug on the other side of Hamburg.

Do you have any idea what may have caused this?
-----------------------------------------------
It must be somewhere in the MAP/Terrain generation. Or in a reduction algorithm. But a 100m wide river cannot just stop at one point. The other side of the river is there. and much smaller.

Do you have an idea how to solve the issue?
-------------------------------------------
Whoever coded the map generation should have an idea where this could happen. I can supply screenshots and areas to reproduce the effect.

Title: Quality Of Life Improvements
Body:
This app is fantastic! It is by far the best FOSS map solution for android out there... However, it could use a few touch ups:

- A UI overhaul
  ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/88250914/165986462-a0453766-985b-4ea9-bf01-0839856fa2b1.png)
  Something like this is what I had in mind. The next street name should be far more visible than the current one. And the color contrast needs to be increased for readability. The font size should be increased too... In addition to total trip time, time until next turn should be displayed, though this isn't overly crucial.
- Make the TTS audible when playing music. I'm not sure how this' done exactly, but in other map apps, it dampens the audio of other apps when it gives instructions.
- Make some instructions more clear:
  - "Stay right" when merging onto highways should be renamed to "Merge to the rightmost lane" and "Turn right onto `street/highway name`".
  - When continuing straight onto another street, it prevents one's ability to look for the next turn clearly. Optionally, combine "continue straight" instructions and simply display the next "stay/turn left/right" instruction. This is highly useful on the USA's `freeways`/high-speed highways.
  - Roundabouts are a bit confusing, the turns here should be displayed as "At the roundabout, take the nth turn onto `street name`".
  - Specific turn lanes should be specified, e.g. "Using the 2nd from the left lane, turn left". This should be audibly relayed, but displayed with a different icon in the map-view navigation instead of text.
- Stop rotating the map based on the compass! Stationary GPS doesn't work reliably enough for this purpose and causes confusion/inconvenience. Perhaps it could be disabled unless you're biking/walking. This should at least be a configuration option.
- Stoplights/signs should be displayed on the map, this' a feature in Google Maps, and it's quite nice.
- Time based delay calculation... AKA take rush-hour(s) into account.
- POI searching is nonexistant... Perhaps integration with Acastus-Photon would work here?
- Alternate routes should be displayed and added as optional, toll roads should be ignored by default, but mid-navigation switching should remain an option.
- The open-in feature doesn't work in this app. It doesn't open search when an address is provided, and should. It often contains `%20`s which means the strings aren't being parsed/sanitized correctly.
- LOD should be customizable, as of right now, the map shows too many details for my liking, this should be customizable.
- Night mode! My eyes!!
- Feet/Meters should be simplified to Miles/Kilometers when it's over 0.5 (~2700/500) of either of these units.

Most of these features are present in other map-apps, namely Google's and Apple's. If this app gained these features, there'd never be any reason to use the proprietary ones! :D

Title: Deleting one track deletes another due to identical timestamp IDs
Body:
Track objects use their [recordingStart.time](https://github.com/y20k/trackbook/blob/04f82f42bf921d59a8e9b06badc93d6d9391ebe6/app/src/main/java/org/y20k/trackbook/core/Track.kt#L74) as their ID. However, it seems that even though this value is given in milliseconds, it only has a resolution of 1 minute. I am finding that ALL of my tracks in Trackbook have a recordingStart time that is exactly aligned to 1 minute. This is also evident in the way they are exported to GPX files.

![2021-10-18_01-45-53](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7299570/137700349-67b86a19-2ede-4a98-85cc-5f88ee5fed78.png)


I believe this is causing a problem when deleting tracks. If you have two tracks that were created in the same minute, deleting one of them will also delete the other.

Here is a recording:

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7299570/137700239-1aa000cc-93e4-4b27-963e-b7b8856e6cbe.mp4

I know this situation might sound silly, but I think it's possible that a user might accidentally create a small track, then start a new track to record their walk / hike. But if they delete the accidental track, it will also delete their hike track.

To reproduce:

1. Create multiple small tracks very quickly
2. Delete one of them
3. The others will also be deleted

Possible solutions:

1. Figure out why recordingStart is aligned to the minute, and make sure it aligns to the millisecond or second instead.
2. Assign a random ID to every track for internal use.

So the problem is in [TracklistAdapter.kt](https://github.com/y20k/trackbook/blob/master/app/src/main/java/org/y20k/trackbook/tracklist/TracklistAdapter.kt#L155) but I am not sure if the crash comes from removeTrackAtPosition or removeTrackById.

I found this [stackoverflow thread](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/33789345/whats-better-notifydatasetchanged-or-notifyitemchanged-in-loop), and I tried adding notifyDataSetChanged to the functions, like this:

```Kotlin
    /* Removes track and track files for given position - used by TracklistFragment */
    fun removeTrackAtPosition(context: Context, position: Int) {
        CoroutineScope(IO).launch {
            val positionInTracklist = position - 1
            val deferred: Deferred<Tracklist> = async { FileHelper.deleteTrackSuspended(context, positionInTracklist, tracklist) }
            // wait for result and store in tracklist
            withContext(Main) {
                tracklist = deferred.await()
                notifyItemRemoved(position)
                notifyItemChanged(0)
                notifyDataSetChanged()
            }
        }
    }


    /* Removes track and track files for given track id - used by TracklistFragment */
    fun removeTrackById(context: Context, trackId: Long) {
        CoroutineScope(IO).launch {
            // reload tracklist //todo check if necessary
            val positionInTracklist: Int = findPosition(trackId)
            val deferred: Deferred<Tracklist> = async { FileHelper.deleteTrackSuspended(context, positionInTracklist, tracklist) }
            // wait for result and store in tracklist
            withContext(Main) {
                tracklist = deferred.await()
                val positionInRecyclerView: Int = positionInTracklist + 1 // position 0 is the statistics element
                notifyItemRemoved(positionInRecyclerView)
                notifyItemChanged(0)
                notifyDataSetChanged()
            }
        }
    }
```

After adding this line, I have not been able to recreate the crash :+1:. However, I am writing this issue instead of a pull request because I don't know all the implications of notifyDataSetChanged or whether it's actually the best solution.

To reproduce:

1. Record multiple tracks.
2. Delete the one that appears at the top of the list. Deleting by swiping and by clicking the trash can button both exhibit the crash.

Possible solutions:
1. notifyDataSetChanged?

I hope this information is helpful. Thanks for all your work!

Title: Feature request: configurable update frequency, not fixed to 15s
Body:
Hi y20k. Thank you for this app. I have read through the other issues and your FAQ and I understand you want to keep configuration to a minimum. However I think an update frequency setting would be quite reasonable alongside the accuracy threshold setting.

For example, when I am walking and improving sidewalks / crossings on OSM I would like updates more frequently than 15s, preferably down to 1-2s, but when I am hiking I'd be satisfied with slower updates, like 60s if it helps preserve battery.

If you are open to the idea I can try to write a PR for it. I would just put another slider like the accuracy threshold setting. Thanks for your time.

Edit: It seems that getting fine-grained GPX tracks would also require changing / configuring `DEFAULT_THRESHOLD_DISTANCE`, which you may not wish to do. I didn't want to add two new options of course.

Title: Color last samples differently to improve visbility when overlapping with previous path
Body:
Hi!

I find Trackbook really nice, because it is so clean, polished and minimal.

I have a single really annoying inconvenience: when I'm passing over an already visited section on the trip I cannot see the current location, as all samples have the same color. (eg. returning from a peak on a trip) If the last sample could be distinguished by different color, or different sized marker (maybe both) that would improve greatly upon this. If the last few samples could also be distinguished (maybe a gradient from the "old" sample color to the "fresh" sample color over the last 3-5 samples) that would also improve the sense of direction.

Greetings!

Title: Replace GPX Save Feature With Share Sheet
Body:
The feature that lets you save a recording as GPX should be implemented as an Andoid share sheet. The share sheet should offer to: 

- save recording as GPX to storage
- share the recording as GPX with other apps that can handle `application/gpx+xml`

Title: POLL: Should Trackbook leave Google Play?
Body:
A couple of month ago I installed [LineageOS for microG](https://lineage.microg.org/) on my phone.  LineageOS for microG is an Android distribution that comes with the [F-Droid](https://www.f-droid.org) store preinstalled. I must say, since I was using mainly Open Source apps before, I really did not miss the Google Play Store much. That made me think: Should I pull Trackbook from Google Play. I personally like [F-Droid](https://www.f-droid.org) a 1000 times better than Google Play. F-Droid for me is by far the better home for my app.

I am interested in your thoughts ...

Title: Erractic location
Body:
Beta apk from #2 ,

Recorded a bunch of tracks today. Noticed on one of my runs, the distance was way off. It said about 5KM, though I  just walked 2. I noticed that some of the points were completely erratic. One was on the other end of the road, which might explain the distance. 
I suspect this is due to the location from network rather than GPS, since I was inside a store when I noticed this. Is it possible to have an option where we can select only GPS instead of GPS+Network?

Title: Bump com.google.android.material:material from 1.10.0 to 1.11.0
Body:
Bumps [com.google.android.material:material](https://github.com/material-components/material-components-android) from 1.10.0 to 1.11.0.
Release notes

Sourced from com.google.android.material:material's releases.

1.11.0

Important

Elevation Overlays within default component styles have been replaced by the new Tonal Surface Color system. Instead of blending the Primary color with the Surface color based on an elevation value, components now use a specific Surface color role that can be more easily understood and customized.

Documentation for the affected components and which Surface color roles they use has been updated in https://github.com/material-components/material-components-android/commit/2114a11378fd801b32cd7431bb8a2c94de4f462c.

What's new since 1.10.0

  • New Tonal Surface Color system! Check out the design guidance and the commit with documentation updates for more details.
  • New Carousel variants! 1.11.0 introduces three new Carousel variants: center-aligned hero, uncontained, and fullscreen. Check out the Carousel documentation for more details.

Dependency Updates

None.

Note: Earlier versions of 1.11.0 used other versions of the androidx.activity 1.8.0 library dependency, but there is no change in the dependency from 1.10.0, the last stable MDC library version.

Library Updates

  • Badging
    • Adjusted badge vertical offset for certain components to fit design requirements (78cc54b8c39da437732ff3d7c96de8f345e09348)
    • Added note in attachBadgeDrawable method docs about menu item views being re-used when there is a structural change in the menu. Since badges are attached to the views, this may change the menu item that the badge is intended for (5e6ea582801ebfe2af8457261581a2ad43c8051a)
  • BottomNavigationView
    • Set badges to be null instead of removing them from the sparse array so that removing badges before restoring badge states will not override the current state (9f2e6864d2170d633709e7aa66b9e8e178352ef0)
  • BottomSheet
    • Fix sheet corners animation (de271320e4ba046c73e35e18f687ee4637f3a6ac)
  • Carousel
    • Ensure that extra small size is not larger than the large size (17baf71972016cf4608d9c83b902bb2debee585b)
    • Fixed crash when recyclerview has a size of zero. (26c31292012868d7e9889f6ecee7559c61caa5b2)
    • When navigating with keyboard, scroll focused item to nearest focal keyline, not the first focal keyline (fb9c1c6edfbdbe4791043a128c03a7fad5fd99f0)
    • Force hero strategy to be start-aligned if there are not enough items to make it center-aligned (9a2347bda5312dd6dc3ab7818a6f71297dcf018b)

Title: Mark "minute jumps" in the name
Body:
Depending on the listing and its formula it may be reasonable, that a value 29.000 + 1.234 becomes 30.234 or may be obvious for a human reader, that jumping to the next minute it not intended ("in German: Koordinatensprung")
Someone therefore proposed that such points could get a mark in their name such that one can decide whether to consider that point or not (in order to avoid a checkbox for it because most people probably will not read or understand what the checkbox is good for).

Title: Unexpected calculation results
Body:
See this test as an example:
assertEquals(10.0, Calculator.evaluate("abs(y(x+1))", x:-12, y:1));

The formula evaluates to abs(1(-12+1)) = abs(1-11) = 10

I would rather consider this an error case because -11 has an unary minus which must not be interpreted as a two input minus operator.

The desired behavior would be that such cases result in an exception - maybe later on in ExpressionBuilder's evaluation, e.g. by keeping it abs(1(-11)). That way such points would be sorted out while correct ones will still be calculated.

Probably geocachers won't check beforehand for single digit terms like (x-3) that the input range for x should not start at 0, so this may become relevant in practice.

Title: Extend multi-variable possibilities
Body:
Just a first vague idea for better usability now that we have two variables...
Not really sure yet if it really simplifies usability:

IS: Given listing formula like (A+B) * B * B, with B being known (e.g. 7), A unknown, you need to pre-solve the formula for given B or insert B three times

GOAL: What about just taking the formula as it is and selecting letter A range 0-9, letter B range 7 (one single value range), then pressing show button. Letters could be offered in dropdown depending on what is contained in the formula, to keep it faster than entering three times B manually.

I should take care not to destroy functions like exp, abs etc. if letters can be chosen freely instead of just x, y

Title: [FEATURE] Add Option to Lock North Orientation on Map
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
The map screen is very sensitive to losing its north up orientation when zooming.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Please add an option to lock the map orientation to where north is up. Or, alternatively, tweak the map settings to make changing the orientation require a far more deliberate action.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
The current workaround is to manually click the compass button to restore north up orientation after each and every zoom action.

Title: [FEATURE] Add Option to Display Imperial Units
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When viewing location detail cards (the ones that display altitude, speed, etc.) and more generally whenever the app displays your current distance from another location, there is no readily apparent way to change from metric to imperial units.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Please add a feature to display units in imperial. This could be done via an app-wide toggle in settings, or possibly set by default based on the metadata from the device Locus is installed on.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
The only alternative currently is to convert units outside the app.

Title: [FEATURE] Release on the Play Store
Body: Releasing the app on the Play Store would offer a convenient and secure method for users to download and keep it updated.

Title: [FEATURE] Update frequency
Body:

Title: [BUG] Locations weren't updating anymore
Body:
## OS
<!-- Tick on which OS this issue happens -->
* [x] Android
* [ ] iOS

## Android specific
If you ticked `Android`, please specify on which Android flavor this issue happens

* [x] Samsung Android (Samsung Phones)
* [ ] MIUI (Xiaomi Phones)
* [ ] Stock ROM (Google Phones)
* [ ] Huawei Android (Huawei Phones)
* [ ] GrapheneOS
  * [ ] I do have Google Play Services installed
* [x] LineageOS

## Describe the bug
This weekend I discovered that my locations on all 3 phone's did not update anymore.
I did not get any updates only the error that there was no data and that is was possible that the tasks were not running anymore.

So I did some research and discoverd that the relayservers I selected in the app were not working anymore. I unselected the relausevers and selected new ones. The ones that are on top of the list. I then generatied a new link and added it to the other phones and visa versa. That made it working againg.  

This made me think about how the app is working. Is looks like the app generates the link with the selected relayservers included. Because the locationsharing dit not work by only changing the relayservers in the app and the share. I had to generate a new link to make the share work again. Is this how it works? And what if my wife is sharing her location and it stops sharing because the relayservers selected on her app aren't working anymore? She had to do several things to make it work again. That's not handy but maybe the only way? Or is this a bug? Or is this how it works but can we figure out another way to fix the sharing when a relayserver stops working. Ofcourse it was strange that al 5 relayservers did not work anymore. But it made me wonder what was happening.

## To Reproduce
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Just wait thill the relaysevers are not working anymore.

## Expected behavior
I hope that is it possible to select new relayservers in the app and the share to make it work again without generating a new link, or maybe that the link is automatically changing for the phones who are follwing the task. 
## Screenshots
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

## Additional context
Add any other context about the problem here.

## Priority
Please set a realistic priority on how important this issue is to you. We will fix all bugs, but urgent bugs (such as the app not starting at all for example) are more important than fixing some styles for example.

* [ ] Low
* [x] Medium
* [ ] High
* [ ] Urgent
 
## App Version
0.15.2 both versions

Title: [FEATURE] Map caching settings
Body: I suggest adding a map cache limit and preferably a cache location (internal memory or memory card if available) to the settings. Accordingly, in this case, it should be possible to clear the cache without clearing other user data of the application, and use the cached data when re-accessing the same terrain at the same scale. This will speed up the display of the map, reduce mobile traffic, which is critical in areas with low Internet speed or its high price.

Title: [FEATURE] Share location only if I'm in the specified area
Body:
I suggest, in addition to the schedule of automatic switching on and off according to the schedule, in the settings of each "share" optionally add the ability to specify a circle (center and radius), when the data of this "share" is located in which the data of this "share" will be transmitted, but not outside it. Use Case: It will often be useful for my colleagues to see where I am at the moment. But only if I'm in a city or nearby suburbs. If I went somewhere far away, I have no reason to share with them a detailed track of my movements. However, so that they do not think that I (or they) have communication problems, a "out of tracking zone" / "somewhere far away" signal should be sent, and this should be my status displayed on the screens of those who track my location.

An alternative is to manually turn off and on the appropriate "share" when leaving the area and when returning to it.

Title: [FEATURE] Customizable notification when approaching a monitored device
Body:
I suggest adding to the properties of each shares the distance to me at which (or less) the Locus will notify me with the selected sound signal / TTS-voice.

This would be useful for the opportunity to meet up with a busy buddy who finally ended up in the same neighborhood. Or, when searching in the natural environment, to prevent excessive convergence of neighboring search groups.

Most likely, it will be necessary to add notification frequency settings if the user has not marked the notification as viewed. And, most likely, preventing repeated notifications if, for some time, another device left the notification zone, and then crossed its borders again.

Title: [FEATURE] Replace the huge panel "... share active" with a counter indicator
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
In the current version of the application, a large panel is displayed at the bottom (by the way, the fact that this is a sliding panel is completely non-obvious). It is needed only to notify the user about the number of current recipients of the coordinates of this device.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
The same functionality would be clearer, for example, in the form of a round indicator-button in the lower left corner of the screen with the number of recipients of the current device's coordinates.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
In general, remove this indicator. In Google Maps, a similar function works flawlessly without displaying anything extra on the main screen, and even on the system notification bar. And it's quite convenient. Don't display all possible variables just because you can.

Title: [FEATURE] Allow users to specify custom track line thickness and transparency
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
At present, the track line is extremely thick, overlapping toponyms and cartographic elements, and disproportionate to them. At the same time, when there are several lines, or the movements of tracked trackers occur in a limited area, the information content of the map with tracks is greatly reduced.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
In the application settings, the user can change the preset value of the track thickness (24 px) to something else, as well as specify the transparency from the default value (0%) to, for example, 90% in 10% increments.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
At the moment, I don't see an alternative solution to hide an opaque line of enormous thickness with the available means.

**Additional context**
![Screenshot_2023_0831_154711_app myzel394 locus](https://github.com/Myzel394/locus/assets/3509797/33a0920c-40f2-420b-87d8-f7b8ade2a79d)

Title: [BUG] Clarifications in the Download section of the README.md file
Body:
Currently, the instructions contain the following text:

> Locus comes in two variants for Android:
> 1. GMS: This variant uses Google Play Services to get your location. If you have Google Play Services installed, you need to use this variant.
> 2. FLOSS: This variant is completely libre and does not use any proprietary software. If you have NO Google Play Services installed, you need to use this variant.
> https://apt.izzysoft.de/fdroid/index/apk/app.myzel394.locus / https://github.com/Myzel394/locus/releases

For example, I discovered this application for the first time, and I choose which version I should install. Since I have Google services on my smartphone, I have to select the "GMS" option. This letter combination does not occur anywhere else in the text, and I can assume that the first link at the end of the section corresponds to the first item of the list (GMS), and the second to the second item (FLOSS).

You should explicitly state that the "GMS" version is only available on Github (files named locus-arm64-gms.apk, locus-armeabi-gms.apk, locus-universal-gms.apk and locus-x86-gms.apk in depending on the device), and it can only be updated through a notification in the app itself.

Also, it makes sense to point out that only the FLOSS version is available on [F-Droid](https://f-droid.org/en/packages/app.myzel394.locus/), and there are no plans to place a GMS version on F-Droid.

And finally, the fact that the F-Droid version does not work with smartphones that have Google services installed should be explicitly stated in the description of the application on F-Droid.

Title: [FEATURE] Manual location refresh?
Body:
Hello,

I've noticed that while locus is in background (swiped away from recent apps) it only updates once an hour which may be too long for many to wait for a location update (I thought it was supposed to be 15 minutes, if im not mistaken?)

Can you implement an option, that the user who creates the task must allow called "Manual location refresh" or something like it, where those who are viewing location can update their location, as that person may not be in same location anymore depending on when it was last updated.

Thats how Google maps works with location sharing feature. When user clicks to see friends location, google pings their device to get its location and sends it back. I believe its done this way to avoid further battery drain by constantly sending the location when its not needed.

Title: 🎷 [Important] How should we handle analytics?
Body:
I'd like to add analytics to Locus. But no worries, I don't want to add Google Analytics or any other privacy infringing analytics - I want to add privacy-friendly analytics that **require users to opt-in**.
Having at least some analytics would help me massively finding out what's most important to you users and I can see where the app works fine and where not.

Based on that I'd like to add two criteria for any analytics:
* Both the analytics frontend as well as the backend must be open source
* The backend must be self-hostable (I will probably host the backend myself)

Unfortunately, there doesn't seem to be many free privacy-friendly analytics tools available for Flutter. So far, I have only found:

* [Plausible Analytics](https://plausible.io/)

If you know any other analytics tool or have anything else to say to this discussion, please comment here.

I'll leave this issue open for some time to give you guys some time to comment and discuss.
